volume.h1:
SOUTH INDIAN INSCRIPTIONS
VOLUME XXVI
INSCRIPTIONS COLLECTED DURING THE YEAR 1908-09.

inscription.head:
No. 1.
(A.R. No. 1 of 1909.)
KŌḌIKKARAI, TIRUTTURAIPPUNDI TALUK, TANJAVUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the Siddharmaḍam on the sea-shore.
It is in Nāgarī characters and Marāṭhī language.

It refers to Bābā-Gōsāvi, son of Sētu[bā]vā-svāmi, described as advē-
(vait)-āgrēsara, and mentions rājādhirāja mahārājarāje Pratāpasiha, (and his
son), mahārājādhirājīvarāje Tulajā-mahārāja, svāmibhakti-dhuraṁdhara-rājē
Aṁṇājirāva and raiśa bhāgavata Dāvagōṭhagē, son of a certain sēṭha, probably
named Gē[ga]. It ends with a benediction.

l.detr: || Śrīmat advēt = āgrēsara rājē śrī-Sētu-[bā]vā-svāṁi || chiraṃ ||
|| Sē[ṭha] Gē[ga] chiraṁjīva raiśa bhāgavata Dāvagōṭhagē sāṁtē• śva ||
|| dattara āravidiṁ salāni matama • va niraṁtara phalā asē ||

inscription.head:
No. 2.
(A.R. No. 2 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

It records the royal gift of all lands in Piḷḷai vēḻārkuḍikkāḍu in Pūṅguṉṟa-
nāḍu, specifying that three parts of it were to be utilised for food offerings during
Sundarapāṇḍiyan-sandhi to the goddess Tirukkāmakōṭṭa-nāchchiyār, consort
of the god Rājēndrachōḷīch-churamuḍaiya-nāyanār in Poṉṉamarāpati, situated
in Puṟamalai-nāḍu and one part as maḍappuṟam for the Sirivāraṇapiriyaṉ
Śrī-maṭha. The latter of the gifts was entrusted to Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟa-
muḍaiyāṉ Suṇḍararpperumāḷ.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 312.

inscription.head:
No. 3.
(A.R. No. 3 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription records the gift of lands in some villages and the antarāya
and other levies from them by [Rājēndra]śōḻaṉ Kēraḷaṉ alias Nishadarājaṉ
for the daily expenses in the temple of the same god as occurring in No. 2 above
of Poṉṉamarāpati, stated here as situated in Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rāja-
rājappāṇḍi-nāḍu.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 147.

inscription.head:
No. 4.
(A.R. No. 4 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This is built in at the end and damaged. It seems to record that Rājēndra-
śōḻaṉ Kēraḷaṉ alias Nishadharājaṉ, probably identical with the person occurring
in No. 3 above, made some provision for the installation of Śivaliṅga and for
the consecration of the vimāna (main shrine) probably at [Poṉṉamarāpati]
which is here stated to have been situated in Puṟamalai-nāḍu, a subdivision of
Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarājap-pāṇḍināḍu.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 132.

inscription.head:
No. 5.
(A.R. No. 5 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription records a gift of land as maḍappuṟam to a maṭha called
Nilamai-Aḻagiyāṉ tirumaḍam (see No. 13 below) in the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam
(sacred precincts) of the temple of Rājēndraśōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-nāyanār by
Tirukkoḍuṅguṉṟamuḍaiyāṉ Aḻagiyadēvaṉ alias Nishadharājaṉ of Poṉṉamarā-
pati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 150.

edition.l: varkku yāṇṭu 18- vatu puṟamalaināṭṭu poṉṉamarāpati tirukk[o]-

inscription.head:
No. 6.
(A.R. No. 6 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

p.b: Sundarapāṇḍyadēva: (Māṟº Sundº Pāṇḍya I) Year 11+1 = 1227-28 A.D.

inscription.p: The king is described here by the title Śōṇāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyaruḷiya.

It records that the group of people called the Maṟamāṇikkar of Poṉṉam-
arāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu and other districts conferred the title of Maṟach-
chakravarti-piḷḷai on Tiruvaraṉkuḷa-muḍaiyāṉ as a reward for his reciting
the Pēr-vañji, and also granted some tax-free lands for reciting verses in praise
of Perumāḷ of Poṉṉamarāpati.

Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 278 where a fuller
text with another part is given. According to this publication the inscription
is found in the Vishṇu temple.

l.note:
A fuller text is given in Pudu. Ins. No. 278. A fresh copy of the inscription has
to be obtained.

inscription.head:
No. 7.
(A.R. No. 7 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

p.b:
Sundarapāṇḍya: Śaka. 1293, Virōdhi (for Virōdhikṛit), Tai 3 = 1311 A.D.,
December 29 (Wednesday). The Śaka year was probably a mistake for 1233.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of a bowl, bell, incense-burner and lamp
to god Rājēndraśōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyanāyanār at Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-
nāḍu by Vīranārāyaṇa ••• ••• alias Iṉbavaṉapperumāḷ for the merit of the king.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 778 (for 773 ?).

inscription.head:
No. 8.
(A.R. No. 8 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription records the grant, as dēvaḍimaikāṇi, of six mā of land and
six house-sites to a dancing girl named Māṇi and her relatives who were
dedicated to the service of god Rājēndraśōḷīśvaram-uḍaiya-nayiṉār at
Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu for the merit of Varāhakaṇḍiyadēvaṉ, a
Kaikkōḷa of the Aḻagiya-śokkaṉār temple at Madurai.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 817.

inscription.head:
No. 9.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM, TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription records the construction, at Poṉṉamarāpati
in Puṟamalai-nāḍu, of the central shrine (Śrī-vimāna) and the installation in
it of god Rājēndraśōḷīśvaramuḍaiya-mahādēva by Vīmaṉ (Bhīmaṉ) Rājēndra-
śōḻaṉ alias Nishadharājaṉ as well as the distribution of the days of worship
amongst the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 137.

inscription.head:
No. 10.
(A.R. No. 10 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This record registers a gift, by Tirukkoḍuṅkuṉṟamuḍaiyāṉ Kēraḷaṉ
alias Nishadharājaṉ, probably identical with the donor of No. 5 above, of
the village Veḻkūr alias Malayadhvaja-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam in Puṟamalai-
nāḍu, as tirunāmattukkāṇi and also of the taxes such as kaḍamai, antarāyam,
veṭṭimuṭṭam, ecchōṟu, kūṟṟariśi elavai, araiśāvu, śilvari, peruvari and others
from the lands in the village to the Tirukkāmakōṭṭa-nāchchiyār alias
Āluḍaiya-nāchchiyār of the Rājēndraśōḷīśvara-muḍaiya-nayanār temple at
Poṉṉamarāvati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu for various offerings including tiruppaḍi-
māṟṟu to the goddess.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 245.

inscription.head:
No. 11.
(A.R. No. 11 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

p.b:
Kulaśēkharadēva: Year [.] 3, Aṟpaśi 1 = (Māṟavarman Kulaśēkhara
I; c. 1268-1312 A.D.)

inscription.p: The king is given the title Emmaṇḍalamum-koṇḍaruḷiya here.

This inscription is built in at the left end and the date portion cannot, there-
fore, be made out. It is also incomplete.

It records a gift of tolls on horse, aviḻākkaṭṭu, handloom clothes, etc.,
to the Śiva temple of the place to meet the expenses connected with the cele-
bration of the annual festival of the deity, by the people of the Patineṇ-vishayas
(eighteen divisions) and the nagarattār.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 586.

inscription.head:
No. 12.
(A.R. No. 12 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription, damaged in the last line, records the tax-free gift of the
village Virāttalai in the Poṉṉamarāpati-ppaṟṟu together with a number of
levies specified here for the maintenance of a taṇṇirpandal (water-shed) at
Poṉṉamarāpati for the use of the people of the locality, established by Āṟkāḍu-
kiḻār Tiruveṇkāḍuḍaiyār alias Vāṇādarāyar. A long list of taxes imposed
at that time is also given.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 656.

••••• vaitteṉ ••• ••••• kākka

inscription.head:
No. 13.
(A.R. No. 13 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in the maṇḍapa in the south prākāra of the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription in Tamil characters of about the 12th century records that
the flight of steps (sōpāna) and the cloister round the shrine (tirunaḍai-māḷigai)
were built by Nilaimaiyaḻagiyāṉ. Below the last line of the inscription is a
sketch of a kumbha flanked by lamp-stand ?
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 1009.

inscription.head:
No. 14.
(A.R. No. 14 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the gōpura of the Chōḷēśvara temple, right of entrance.

This record registers a royal order addressed to the authorities of several
temples in Poṉṉamarāpatippaṟṟu restraining them from using the water in
Iḍaṅgamikāman, unlike before.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 513.

edition.l: ṭu 8- vatu aṟpa-

inscription.head:
No. 15.
(A.R. No. 15 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Inside the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

It records a gift of paving stones (pākkal) for two sections in the mahā-
maṇḍapa in the temple of Perumāḷ Aḻagapperumāḷ-viṇṇagara-emberumāḷ of
Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu by Parambai-ūruḍaiyāṉ [Vaḍi] viraḻagiyāṉ
Tirumēṉiyār of Dēśiuagndapaṭṭaṇa, a part of Sundaraśōḻapuram in Ollaiyūr-
kūṟṟam in the subdivision of Teṉ-Kōnāḍu of Kōnāḍu alias Kaḍalaḍaiyādilaṁ-
gaikoṇḍaśōḻa-valanāḍu.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 788.

edition.l: [śrī]cakāttam 1367- ṉ mel cellā-

inscription.head:
No. 16.
(A.R. No. 16 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription in Tamil characters of about the 14th century, records
an order of Nishadarāyaṉ addressed to the authorities of the same temple of
Tirumēṟkōyil-Aḻagapperumāḷ-viṇṇagara-emberumāṉ of Poṉṉamarāpati in
Puṟamalai-nāḍu, granting all the taxes due from some communities of Śēvūr
alias Aḻagapperumāḷ-nallūr, a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam village of the god, for burning
two perpetual lamps in the temple from the month of Kārttigai in the 2nd year
of Nishadarāyaṉ.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 986.

l.ta:
niṣatarāyaṉ Olai puṟamalaināṭṭup poṉṉamarāpatit tirumeṟ-
koyil aḻakapperumāḷ viṇṇakara emperumāṉ koyiṟtiruvaṭippiṭik-

inscription.head:
No. 17.
(A.R. No. 17 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
Śaka 1368, Mēḍa (Mēsha) Muṟṟiḍi (Pūrva-paksha), vaḻuvilēḻu-pakkam
(saptamī) Aṟka-vāram (Sunday), Puṇarda-nāḷ (Punarvasu) = 1446 A.D., April 3.

inscription.p:
It begins with a Tamil verse mentioning the dedication of herself to the
temple, here called Aḻagar of Teṉ-Poṉṉai-nagar and other maiden performance
(araṅgēṟṟam) of Śīraṅganāyakī, a dancing girl, and records the gift to her of
one mā of tax-free land in Puduvūr, a tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam according to the
custom of Śēnāpati-tirukkai-vaḻakku.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 781.

l.ta:
eḻilpuṉaiyuñ cakāttamāyirattu muṉṉūṟṟaṟupatteṭṭataṉ meṟcelu

veṉṟic ciraṅkanāyaki tāṉ teṉ-

tirukkai vaḻakkamāka tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam putuvūr vayalil [i]ṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭa
rūpāy orumā innilam

kaṭapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku koyil kaṇakku tappilāveḷāṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 18.
(A.R. No. 18 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.
The king is described here as Śōṇāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyaruḷiya.

This inscription registers a royal order to the servants of the same temple
of Aḻagapperumāḷ-viṇṇagar-emberumāṉ-āḻvār, granting the two villages,
Śēvūr and Muñjaikkuḍi, which formed the category of Vallakūṟṟuva-koḍu-
vāṭkāṇi, as tax-free dēvadāna for the daily offerings in the temple. Guru-
kularāyaṉ, Avaṉinārāyaṇa Viḻupparaiyaṉ and Vāṉavaṉ Viḻupparaiyaṉ
figure as the signatories.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 289.

inscription.head:
No. 19.
(A.R. No. 19 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
Śaka 1375, Mithuna śu. 11, Monday, Anurādha = 1453 A.D., June 18
The
ended on the previous day at 24.

inscription.p:
It registers a gift of money by the Śrībhaṇḍārttār of Aḻagapperumāḷ-viṇṇavar-
emberumāṉ temple at Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu to two women-
Mallāyi and her daughter Ulaguḍaiyanāchchi and others who were strangers
to the place but who had desired to settle down there by accepting service in
the temple.

Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 793 where the text
of the entire record is given.

pūṟuvapa(k)kṣattu tuvā-

yil

l.note: See the rest of the lines in Pudukkottai Vol. Page 541 G. No. 793.

inscription.head:
No. 20.
(A.R. No. 20 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

p.b: Śaka 1371, Śukla, Kuṁba I = 1450 A.D., January 26, (Monday)

inscription.p:
It is in the nature of an order by the deity Sundararāja, registering the
gift of the village Maṉāttār alias Saṅkāyipuram, bifurcated from Mūlaṅguḍi
in Poṉṉamarāpati-nāḍu, here called as the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam of the deity, as
sarvamānya to Tiruvanandāḻvār Pāligaivaruḷidāsa of the Ātrēya-gōtra, des-
cribed as a kōvaṇavaṉ, who sang the hymns of Śaḍagōpaṉ (i.e., Nammāḻvār)
at the request of his Śēnāpati.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 7.
3. Read piṟamotūta

niṉṟa oṉṟumukkālum sarvamānnyam

inscription.head:
No. 21.
(A.R. No. 21 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Sundararāja-perumāḷ temple.

p.b:
Māṟavarmaṉ Kulaśekaradēva: Year 18, Siṁha-śu. 5, Monday, Uttirāḍam =
1285 A.D., August 6. The nakshatra was Chittirā.

inscription.p: This inscription is incomplete.

The king is described here as Emmaṇḍalamum koṇḍaruḷiya. It seems to
record a sale of land (sēnāpati-peruvilai) by the residents of Pūlāṅkuṟichchi of
Puṟamalai-nāḍu to the servants of the temple of Aḻagapperumāḷ-viṇṇagara-
emperumāṉ of Poṉṉamarāpati, in the same nāḍu.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 398.

edition.l:
ratevaṟku yāṇṭu 18- vatu siṁhanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu pañcamiyum tiṅ-
ka[ka]ḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa ut[ti*]rāṭattu nāḷ

peruvilaiyāka viṟṟukkuṭuttapaṭi innāṭṭup
pūlāṅkuṟicciyūrāyicainta ūrom yiṟṟaināḷāl vippatukkuyicaintu viṟṟukkuṭutta

inscription.head:
No. 22.
(A.R. No. 22 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in a field in the same village.

This, much-damaged and defaced record, is identical in contents with
No. 12 above.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 657.

inscription.head:
No. 23.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1909.)
POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in another field in the same village.

p.b: Circa 14th century; Chitrabānu Chittirai 8 = 1342 A.D., April 2.

inscription.p:
Only the praśasti, commencing with Mūvarāyakaṇḍan kaṇḍanāḍu-
koṇḍu, etc., of one Pratāpa Māvali Vāṇādarāyaṉ Maharāśa Sundarattōḷu-
ḍaiyāṉ, is preserved, the rest of the text having been damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 24.
(A.R. No. 24 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar of a rock-cut cave in the hill at the place.

This unfinished record mentions the temple of Tirumalai uḍaiya-mahādēva-
at Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, one of the villages irrigated by (the river) Tēnāṟu in
Rājēndraśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Rājarāja-pāṇḍināḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 25.
(A.R. No. 25 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the second pillar in the same cave.

This inscription records the gift of lands for food offerings to god Tirumalai-
uḍaiya Tēnāṟṟu-Nāyakar at Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, one of the villages irrigated
by (the river) Tēnāṟu, in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu, by Vira-
śēkharan Āḻuḍaiyān alias Adaiḷaiyūr-nāḍāḻvān. The gift lands, stated to be
situated at Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi comprised half the share of the donor the other
half of it also being donated to the same deity in the place as per the record
No. 26 below, by another donor who may have been closely related to the donor
of the present record, perhaps a younger brother.

l.ta: kā[ñ]ci[ra]n tuṭavai rūpāy mukkāl m ārā
m alli vayakkal rūpāy ç varai
yakkal rūpāy mukkāṇi kū ḵm nāvaṟ-
ri cey rūpāy (1/4)m tiru[vārū*]r vayakkal rūpāy

inscription.head:
No. 26.
(A.R. No. 26 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar in the same place.

This inscription records the gift of lands for food offerings to god Tirumalai-
uḍaiya-Tēnāṟṟu-Nāyakar at Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, one of the villages irrigated by
(the river) Tēnāṟu, in Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍu in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu by Uyyavandāṉ
Gaṇḍan alias Gāṅgēyan alias Tiyāgamīgāman. The lands, stated to be situated
at Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, comprised the other half of the share belonging to the
donor. The first half of the share formed the gift to the same deity in No. 25
above, by another donor who was probably the elder brother of the donor of
the present record, as can be guessed by the title Adaiḷaiyūr-nāḍāḻvār borne
by him, a title which is not borne by the donor of the present record.

l.ta: nam kaliyāṉantuṭavai rūpāy kū m kāñ-
ṟai teṟku rūpāy m māṟi nāṭṭān tuṭavai
ç varai kū ḵ nāṭṭān vayakkal rūpāy

inscription.head:
No. 27.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the third pillar in the same place.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 204.

inscription.head:
No. 28.
(A.R. No. 28 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the fourth pillar in the same place.
This record commences with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga of the king.

It registers the remission of tax by the person mentioned below in favour
of the deity described in No. 24 above, of the dēvadāna lands situated in all the
villages including Seṅgaṇi, Palavānkuḍi, Puravuvari-nallūr, Śiru-Veṭṭivayal,
Maṇiyāttiṟal and Pālaivāyil belonging to Aiññūṟṟuvaṉ Uyyavandān alias
Adaḷaiyūr-nāḍāḻvān Kāṅgāyaṉ.

l.note: Left unfinished; only a few letters are wanting.

inscription.head:
No. 29.
(A.R. No. 29 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same pillar in the same place.

This inscription, in late characters, is dated Plavaṅga, Tai 5, and records
the sale of a maṟukāl (sluice) to Nayiṉār Tiruppālaivāśal Mudaliyār by the
residents of Śāḻi alias Tiruchchamaṇamaḻagiya-nallūr, one of the villages
irrigated (by the river) Tēṉāṟu.

l.ta: m paṇṇi kuṭuttapaṭi iṟṟaināḷivaṟku viṟkiṉṟa maṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 30.
(A.R. No. 30 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the rock, left of entrance into the same cave.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 208.

inscription.head:
No. 31.
(A.R. No. 31 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the verandah of the north wall of the same cave.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 219.

inscription.head:
No. 32.
(A.R. No. 32 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

This inscription records the gift of tax and all other levies such as antarāya
from the village Kīḻkkaṇṇamaṅgalam for food offerings to god Tirumalaiuḍaiya-
nāyanār of Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, at the time of the procession of the deity, by
Vīraśēkharan alias Adalaiyūr-nāḍāḻvān, who is described as Nāṭṭāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 33.
(A.R. No. 33 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same verandah, south side.

This incomplete inscription commences with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu
viḷaṅga, etc., of the king.

It refers to the residents of Iḍaikkuḍi alias Iravikulamāṇikka-nallūr
Seṅgaṇi and other villages.

edition.l: l ūromum tāyankuṭi ūromum atti Incomplete.-

inscription.head:
No. 34.
(A.R. No. 33-A of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

This inscription in modern characters, engraved in continuation of No. 33
above is dated Āṅgīrasa, Āṉi 9. It seems to record the authorisation, by the
trustees, etc., of the temple of Subrahmaṇya at Kuṇṇakkuḍi, for the payment
of one paṇam by each family residing in the village Naḍuvakkōṭṭai belonging
to the deity probably for the repairs etc., of the temple, during famine times.

edition.l: § āṅkira[ca*]varuṣam āṉimātam 9 kuṇṇakkuṭik Engraved in continuation of No. 33 above.-
ṭi 1- kku oru paṇam §

inscription.head:
No. 35.
(A.R. No. 34 of 1904.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar of another rock-cut cave in the same hill.

This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc. of
the king.

It records the gift of 10 mā of land on the southern outskirts of Kūnkuḷam
as tiruviḻāppuṟam (for festival purposes) to god Tirumalaiuḍaiya-nāyanār
of Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi by the villagers of Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi alias Tenpugalūr.

inscription.head:
No. 36.
(A.R. No. 35 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the second pillar in the same cave.
This inscription commences with the praśasti, Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc.

It records that Vīraśēkharan alias Virudarājabhayaṁkara Adalaiyūr-
nāḍāḻvān granted a remission of taxes on all the dēvadāna lands situated in a
number of his villages irrigated by the river Tēnāṟu, including Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi,
Vēlavūr and Śāḻi.

inscription.head:
No. 37.
(A.R. No. 36 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the second and third pillars in the same place.

The record commences with Pūmaruviya tirumaḍandaiyum, the
praśasti of the king who is described as Śōṉāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyarḷuliya.

It records the gift of paddy perhaps purchased out of the money got
from the produce of lands in Niyamam, one of the villages irrigated by (the
river) Tēnāṟu in Kēraḻasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu to a deity (name lost) probably by
Gaṇḍaṉ Āḻuḍaiyāṉ alias Kalvāyil-nāḍāḻvāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 38.
(A.R. No. 37 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the third pillar in the same place.

This two-line inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters of the 7th and 8th
centuries, describes the rock-cut temple as Maśilīchchuram (Māśilīśvaram).

inscription.head:
No. 39.
(A.R. No. 38 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the fourth pillar in the same place.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 217.

inscription.head:
No. 40.
(A.R. No. 39 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the verandah of the same cave, below two images.

p.b: Sundara-pāṇḍyadēva (Māṟavarman Sundara pāṇḍya: I Year 8: 1223-24 A.D.

inscription.p: This, much-damaged record, describes the king as Śōṇāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyaruḷiya.

It seems to record the gift of lands and levies in a place (name lost) to god,
Āḷuḍaiyapiḻḻaiyār in the temple of [Tē]nāṟṟu-Nāyakar in Tiru[kkuṉṟakkuḍi]
one of the villages irrigated by (the river) Tēnāṟu, in Kēraḻasiṅgavaḻanāḍu,
probably by Gaṇḍaṉ Amudagaṇḍaṉ of Niyamam.

inscription.head:
No. 41.
(A.R. No. 40 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a wall in front of the same cave.

This record is much damaged. It describes the king as Śōṇāḍu-koṇḍu
Muḍikoṇḍaśōḻapurattu vīrābhishēkamum paṇṇiyaruḷiya.

It seems to record a gift of land in Niyamam for food offerings to god
Tēnāṟṟu-Nāyakar by Gaṇḍan Udaiyañcheydāṉ alias Gāṅgēyaṉ of Niyamam,
one of the villages irrigated by (the river) Tēnāṟu in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 42.
(A.R. No. 41 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Agastyēśvara shrine at the foot of the hill.

p.b: Māṟavarman Kulaśēkharadēva I: Year 22, Āṉi = 1289-90 A.D.

inscription.p: This incomplete record describes the king as Emmaṇḍalamum-koṇḍaruḷiya.

It refers to the dēvakanmi, etc., of the temple of Uḍaiyār Tēnāṟṟu-Nāyakar
of Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, one of the villages, irrigated by (the river) Tēnāṟu in
Adalaiyūṟ-nāḍu in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu.

vatu āṉi mātam kera[ḷa]ciṅkavaḷanāṭṭu atalai-

[śrī]kāryañceyvārkaḷ śrīmāhē

inscription.head:
No. 43.
(A.R. No. 42 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Agastyēśvara shrine at the foot of the hill.
This inscription is built in at the end and damaged.

It gives details of date such as trayōdaśi, Monday and Puṉarpūśa, the
other details being in the built-in part.

It seems to register the sale of the village Muḷḷikkuṇḍu, a dēvadāṉa of
god Tēṉāṟṟu-Nāyakar, as kuḍinīṅgā-kāṇi to a person (name lost), by the
trustees of the temple of the god mentioned above.

inscription.head:
No. 44.
(A.R. No. 43 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the east wall of the Madhunadīśvara shrine at the foot of the hill.

This damaged record describes the king as Śōṇāḍu-koṇḍu Muḍikoṇḍa-
śoḷapurattu vīrābhishēkam paṇṇiyaruḷiya.

It seems to record a gift of lands in Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, made tax-free, for
food offerings to god Uḍaiyār Tēnāṟṟu-Nāyakar of Tirukkuṉṟakkuḍi, by Gaṇḍaṉ
Udaiyañcheydāṉ alias (name lost), probably Gāṅgēyaṉ, the same as the one
figuring in No. 41 above, of Niyamam, one of the villages irrigated by (the
river) Tēnāṟu in Kēraḷasiṅga-vaḷanāḍu. The lands are stated to have been
previously sold to a person named Tiruttaḷikōdai alias Vikkiramapāṇḍya-
paṉmaṉ of Niyamam, by Gaṇḍaṉ Udayañcheydāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 45.
(A.R. No. 44 of 1909.)
KUṈṈAKKUḌI, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the roof of a natural cavern called Jñāniyārmaḍam on the hill.

This record in Brāhmī characters of about the 2nd century B.C. is written
curiously in the manner as if the letters are mirror images. So we have to read
the inscription with the help of a mirror.

inscription.head:
No. 46.
(A.R. No. 45 of 1909.)
TRIPURĀNTAKAM, MARKAPUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a slab in the courtyard of the Tripurāmbā temple in the bed of the tank
at the place.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 480.

inscription.head:
No. 47.
(A.R. No. 46 of 1909.)
UṆḌAVALLI, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the verandah of the kitchen in the rock-cut Anantaśayana temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol., X, No. 560.

inscription.head:
No. 48.
(A.R. No. 47 of 1909.)
UṆḌAVALLI, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar set up in front of the Bhāskarēśvara temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 737.

inscription.head:
No. 49.
(A.R. No. 47-A of 1909.)
UṆḌAVALLI, GUNTUR TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XVI, No. 88.

inscription.head:
No. 50.
(A.R. No. 48 of 1909.)
ŚIRIPURAM, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the Rāmaliṅgēśvara temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 179.

inscription.head:
No. 51.
(A.R. No. 49 of 1909.)
ŚIRIPURAM, SATTENAPALLE TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the second pillar in the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 177.

inscription.head:
No. 52.
(A.R. No. 50 of 1909.)
VĒMŪRU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a pillar in front of the Rāmaliṅgēśvarasvāmin temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 86.

inscription.head:
No. 53.
(A.R. No. 51 of 1909.)
VĒMŪRU, TENALI TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 87.

inscription.head:
No. 54-56.
(A.R. Nos. 52 to 54 of 1909.)
ŚIDDĀPURA, MOLAKALMURU TALUK, CHITALDROOG DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the Brahmagiri, Yanumula-Timmayya and Jaṭiṅga-Rāmēśvara rocks
at Śiddāpura.
Published in C.I.I., Vol. I, p. xxvii.

inscription.head:
No. 57.
(A.R. No. 55 of 1909.)
PALLĀVARAM, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near a well in the village.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 780.

inscription.head:
No. 58.
(A.R. No. 56 of 1909.)
PALLĀVARAM, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near a well.
This late record contains details of date, viz., Pramāthin, Āvaṇi 15.

It registers that the villagers of Agaram Pallāpuram alias Avubaḷamma-
samudram sold a piece of land for a well to Śēku Muśāmiyar, son of Śēkuā
Alāvattiṉ.

l.note:
Written in continuation of the third part of the Telugu inscription, published as
ṇo. 780 of S.I.I., Vol. X.

inscription.head:
No. 59.
(A.R. No. 57 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, left
of entrance.

The details of date given, viz. Ś. 1328, Viyaya, Tulā śu. 15, Wednesday,
Aśvati correspond to 1406 A.D. October 27. The nakshatra ended on the
previous day at ·40.

It records the royal gift of the village Jananāthanallūr alias Śirudaiyūr
situated in Āyiravēlippaṟṟu in Āmūr-kōṭṭam as kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāṉa and as
tax-free sarvamānya, far Bokkarāyaṉ-śandhi (after the king), in and for
the repairs to the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-nāyinār of Tirukkaḻu-
kkuṉṟam a taṉiyūr in Kaḷattūr-kkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
koṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūr koṭṭattu taṉiyūr tirukkaḻukkuṉṟattu
uṭaiyā[r*] tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuṭaiyaṉāyinārkku pokkarāyaṉ cantikku irāyar
aruḷi ce[y*]tapaṭikku kuṭiṉiṅkā tevatāṉamāka āmur koṭṭattu āyiravevi-
paṟṟil cenanātanallūrāṉa ciṟutaiyūrkku ellaiyāvatu

kiḻpārkkellai tāḻampaḷḷattukku meṟku tenpārkellai taḻuvacūḻainta-
nallūrkku vaṭakkum melpārkellai maṭayattūr accalampākkattukku kiḻakkum
vaṭapārkkellai koṇṭampākkattukku teṟkum iṉṉāyinār tirunāmattukkāṇi
toṇṭa[r*]kaḷnāyakaviḷākam uḷpaṭa

niṅkalāka ivūr • ta

kālum maramum kiṇaṟum maṉṟum kaṉṟumepāḻum uḷpaṭa

paṇṇi

maravaṭai ērimiṉ vilai tiruvācalil piṟakkum paḻavari putuvari

inscription.head:
No. 60.
(A.R. No. 58 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, left
of entrance.

This inscription is written below No. 59 above. The details of date given
here are the same as found in No. 59 above and their equivalent is, therefore,
1406 A.D., October 27. The nakshatra ended on the previous day at ·40.

It seems to be in the nature of confirmation of the grant of the village
Jananāthapuram alias Śirudaiyūr in Āyiravēlippaṟṟu to the temple of Tiru-
malaiyāḷuḍaiya nāyinār by the king as recorded in No. 59 above by the people
(nāṭṭār) of Āyiravēlippaṟṟu in Āmūṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa śōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
cātanam paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi eṅkaḷ paṟṟu ceṉa-
[nāta]nallūrāna ciṟutai ūr [nāṟ]pāṟkellaiyum tāna

pūcaikkum tiruppaṇikkum nāṭṭu iṟaiyiliyāka kuṭiniṅkāt tevatānamāka iṉta
ūril irācakāttukku irukkum poṉ aṟupatumviṉiva-

kumpaṭi cammatittu cilārekaip paṇṇikkuṭuttom uṭaiyār tirumalai
yāḷuṭaiya nāyaṉārku āyiravelipaṟṟu nāṭṭavar[o*]m ippaṭikku ivai vicamur
kiḻavar eḻuttu ivai [ciṟu*]tai ūr kiḻavar eḻuttu ivai āmurnāṭṭu veḷār
eḻuttu

ivai māppūti malaiyāḻvāṉ eḻuttu ivai poṉṉampalakūttaṉ e[ḻu*]ttu
ivai •••• ṟṟūrruṭaiyān e[ḻu*]ttu ivai vayyakaporaimalaikiniyaniṉṟāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 61.
(A.R. No. 59 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kicthen in the Bhaktavatsalēsvara temple, left
of entrance.

This record, in characters of about the 13th century A.D., gives details
of date, viz., Tulā, śu. 6, Friday, Mūlam which may correspond to 1265 A.D.,
October 16, f.d.t. ·20; the nakshatra ended on the previous day at. 67.

It records the gift of the village Karumārampākkamēṟi, made tax-free
by the people (nāṭṭār) of Kaḷattūr-paṟṟu to the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟa-
muḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa
maṇḍalam for offerings during the Kāliṅgarāyaṉ-śandhi established by
Kappalūruḍaiyāṉ Karumāṇikka •••• alias Kāliṅgarāyar of Kappalūr alias
Ulagaḷandaśōḻanallūr in Muttūṟṟu-kkūṟṟam in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam and for the
festival held in Aippaśi on the day of Pūrāḍam, the natal star of Kāliṅga-
rāyar.

l.ta:
tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuṭaiya nā[ya*]ṉārkku kaḷattūrpaṟṟu nāṭṭārom pāṇṭimaṇṭalattu
muttū-

ṟṟukkūṟṟattu kappalūrāṉa ulakaḷanta coḻanallūr kappalūruṭaiyāṉ karumāṇikka
••• ṉāṉa
kāliṅkarāyar kaṭṭiṉa kāliṅkarāyaṉ cantikkum ivaruṭaiya jeṉma nakṣat

cit tirunāḷaikku veṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷukkum nāṅkaḷ mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyākak
kuṭutta karumārampākkam eṟi

ūrkerallaikkum uṭpaṭṭa naṉcey puṉcey poṉvari taṟiiṟai taṭṭārppāṭṭam
erimiṉpāṭṭam iruntu paṟimāṟuvār

cekkukkaṭamai maṟṟum epperpaṭṭa upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa mutalaṭaṅka iṟaiiliyākak
kuṭuttom caṉtarātittavaraiyāka mutala-

ṭaṅka iṟaiyiliyākak kuṭuttom kaḷattūrpaṟṟu nāṭṭārom ivai irācanārāyaṇa
tamiḻavatarayaṉ eḻuttu ivai kaṅkaikoṇ-

pallavarāyaṉ aḻakiyavarataṉ eḻuttu ivai vāḻavantāṉ

eḻuttu ivai tāḻampākkiḻāṉ eḻuttu ivai vikkiramacoḻattamiḻatarayaṉ
eḻuttu ivai valavatarayaṉ eḻuttu ivai karuṅ-

kuppai cittavaṭatevaṉ eḻuttu ivai mācilāmaṇi[yāṉaā]ḻvāṉ eḻuttu
ivai tamiḻatarayar tiruvālakoiluṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu

ivai vikkiramacoḻatta[mi]ḻavatarayaṉ vaṭukanātaṉ eḻuttu ivai[neru]mūrkiḻavaṉ
tiruvoṟṟiyūruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu ivai

nāraikiḻāṉ[e*]ḻuttu ivai karuṅkuppai eḻuttu ivai ariyalūruṭaiyāṉ
k[ā]kkunāyakaṉ eḻuttu ivai uṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu i-

vai [ku]ṉ(n)tai eḻuttu ivai aruḷāḷaperumāḷ eḻuttu ivai cempi-

ivai karikārcoḻanerumurnāṭṭu veḷā-

inscription.head:
No. 62.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, left of
entrance.

This record is much damaged. The details of date given, viz., Śaka
•••• Paridhāvi, Siṅga,-śu. 7, Viśākhā, Sunday, correspond to 1432 A.D.,
August 3. The cyclic year falls in Śaka 1354.

It seems to record a transaction (details lost) by the body called Śrī-Rudra-
Śrī-Mahēśvaras of the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tiru-
kkaḻukkuṉṟam, a taṉīyūr in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
kkiḻamaiṉāḷ ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk kaḷattūṟ koṭṭattut taṉiyūr
tirukkaḻukkuṉṟattu uṭai-
yār tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār koyil śrī ru

inscription.head:
No. 63.
(A.R. No. 61 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, right
of entrance.

The details of date, viz., Ishabha-śu. 13, Sunday, Aśvati correspond to
1348 A.D., April 27, but the paksha was bahuḷa.

This inscription records that some persons (names given) belonging to
Puduppaṭṭiṉam alias Sōḻamāttāṇḍanallūr Uṟaiyūraṉi in Moṉdūr-nāḍu, a sub-
division of Āmūr-kōṭṭam, a district of Jayaṅgōṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, sold their
village Maṅgalam for 350 poṉ to the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiyanāyaṉār
at Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

edition.l:
maṅkalam svastiśrī [||*] tiruvāykeḻvimuṉṉāka caka[la*]lokaccakravatti śrīirāca-
ṉārāyaṇaṉ campuvarāyarkku yāṇṭu 10- vatu iṣapanāyaṟṟu pūrvapakṣattu

nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭa-
lattu āmurkkoṭṭattu moṉtūrnāṭṭu putuppaṭṭiṉam āṉa

coḻamāttāṇṭanallūr uṟaiyūraṇiaḻakiyaṉāyaṉ tirukkaḻukkuṉṟa uṭaiyāṉum
tirumaṇamāyal uṭaiyāṉum kuṉṟamaṟiṉtāṉum irā[ca*]rā[ca*]tevaṉ

kuṉṟa uṭaiyāṉum ciraṅkanātaṉum tiruvaraṅka uṭaiyāṉ attiyūr āḻvā(ṉ)-
ṉum ivvaṉaiva-

kuṉṟattu uṭaiyār tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam uṭaiya ṉāyaṉārkku tirunāmattukāṇi
āka viṟṟukkuṭutta

kollai nilamum nattamum maramum kiṇaṟum

tirunāmattukkāṇi āka viṟṟu-

paṭāta nalppaṇam

nāṉkel-

laikku uḷppaṭṭa nañcai nilamum puñcai nilamum maṉṟum kaṉṟumeypāḻum kāṭu
koṭumeṭu ēriyum ēri nirkoppum maramum kiṇaṟum uḷppaṭa vilai āvatākavum

itukku ituve cācanamākavum itukku itu allatu veṟu poruḷ māvaṟuti
poruḷ celaolaikāṭṭu eṉṉappeṟātākavum ivvūrkku epperppaṭṭa kallum
illai

ṟakkai[k*]koṇṭu irukālāvatum mukkālāvatum uṭaiyār tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam

inscription.head:
No. 64.
(A.R. No. 62 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, right
of entrance.

This inscription records the setting up of the shrine of Shaṇmukha-Piḷḷaiyār
in the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār by Vāṉavaṉ-Mahādēvi Uḍai-
yāṉ Śivadāsar Ishabhavāhanadēvaṉ Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyān, the madhyastha
of Vāṉavaṉmādēvichchaturvēdimaṅgalam in Āmūr-kkōṭṭam and the gift of
four cows for a sandhi lamp to that shrine by the madhyastha mentioned above
and his younger brother Āḷavandāṉ.

l.ta:
uṭaiyār tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuṭaiya nāyaṉār [koyil] ṣaṇmukappiḷḷaiyārai eḻun-
taruḷuvitta cikaraṇattāṉ āmurkkoṭṭattu vāṉavaṉmātevi-

āḷavantāṉum ippiḷ[ḷai]yārkku vaitta cantiviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyil tiru-
nuntāviḷakkukkum maṉṟāṭikaḷil mātaṉāṉa tirukkaḻukkuṉṟak-
āka uru nālum kaikkoṇṭu muḻaikkoṉ makaṉ kānampi
vacam iv uru nālum kāṭṭikkuṭuttu iccantiviḷakku oṉṟukkum
ippaṭi kaikkoṇṭamaikku ivai muḻaikkoṉ makaṉ kānampi eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 65.
(A.R. No. 63 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, right
of entrance.

The record gives the details of date viz., Vijaya, Aippaśi 26, which correspond
to 1406 A.D., October 25 (Monday).

It records the royal gift of taxes like paṭṭainūlāyam, śekku-āyam,
eruttuchchammādam, āṭṭaichchammādam, poychchammādam, etc., due to
the Cheṭṭis, etc., of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟam and Dēvamaṇḍalam for the festival of
procession held in the month of Tai in the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-
nāyanār. The executor of the gift was Nāgīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ Villavarāyaṉ,
the agent of the king.

l.ta:
pūpatirāyar aruḷic ceyalpaṭi[kku]
yaraicar kāriyattukku • kaṭava [śrī] n[āki]

tiyati uṭaiyār
tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuṭaiya ṉāyanā[r*] taittirunāḷ tiruveḻic-

u naṭakkumpaṭi tirukkaḻukkuṉṟattilum irukkum ceṭṭiyaḷ kaikoḷar
kavaṟaiyaḷ taccavaṭi

vāṇiyar cekkuvāṇiyar ce[ṉai]kkaṭaiyar maṟṟum palakācāyavaṟkattukkum koḷḷum
paṭṭaṭai nūl āyam cekku āyam erut-
tuccammātam āṭṭaiccammātam poyccammātam maṟṟum āya[va*]kai upātikaḷum uṭpaṭa

caṟuvamāṉyam ākiviṭṭa aḷavukku inta upaiyattukku ivvakaiyil āyattil
ākiṟa mutal koyil paṇṭāratti-
ṇinavar keṅkaikkaraiyil kārampacuvai koṉṟa pāvattai peṟuvāṉ §

inscription.head:
No. 66.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, right of
entrance.

This inscription records the gift of 32 cows and a bull for a perpetual lamp
to the temple of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-nayiṉār by Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍai-
yān of Neṟkuḷam.

l.gr: svasti [||*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍalīśvaran harirāya vibhāṭan bhāṣai

edition.l:
r vaitta tirinuntāviḷakku 1- ku pacu 32m iṣapam 1- m tiṇṭikkoṉmakaṉ
vaiyiravaṉ cāvāmuvā naṟpacuvāka

kaikoṇṭu nāḷ 1- ku uḻakku ney irācakecariyāl candrātittavaraiyum cellumpaṭi
cilārekai paṇṇi-

inscription.head:
No. 67.
(A.R. No. 64A of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the north wall of the kitchen in the Bhaktavatsalēśvara temple, right
of entrance.
This inscription is engraved in continuation of No. 66 above.

It records the gift of 32 cows and a bull for a perpetual lamp to the temple
of Tirukkaḻukkuṉṟamuḍaiya-nayiṉār by Aḻagiya-Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyār
of Neṟkuḷam.

l.gr: svasti [||*] śrīman mahāmaṇḍalīśvaran harirāya vibhāṭan pā-

ṣaikku tappuma Read va rāyar kaṇṭaṉ vijaiyapūpatirāyarku cellāniṉṟa śagābdam
1331- ṉ mel uṭaiyār tirukkaḻukuṉṟamuṭai-

kkoṉmakaṉ nayiṉāṉ cāvāmuvā naṟpacuvāka kaikoṇṭu nāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 68.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
At the top of the second pillar in the upper verandah of the Orukal-
maṇḍapa on the hill.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 16.

inscription.head:
No. 69.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the first pilaster in the upper verandah of the Orukalmaṇḍapa on the
hill.

These record the signatures of the Dutch officers who visited the Maṇḍapa
in the 17th century.

inscription.head:
No. 70.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the first pillar in the upper verandah of the Orukalmaṇḍapa on the hill.

These record signatures of the Dutch officers who visited this maṇḍapa
in the 17th century.

No. 71.
(A.R. No. 68 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the second pillar in the upper verandah of the Orukal-maṇḍapa, on the
hill.

These are records of the signatures of the Dutch officers who visited this
maṇḍapa in the 17th century.

These record the signatures of the Dutch officers who visited this maṇḍapa

(A.R. No. 73 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the fourth pillar in the lower verandah of the Orukal-maṇḍapa, on the
hill.

These are records of the signatures of the Dutch officers who visited this
maṇḍapa in the 17th and 18th centuries.

inscription.head:
No. 77.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the walls of the lower verandah of the Orukal-maṇḍapa, on the hill.

These miscellaneous writings also record the signatures of the Dutch
officers who visited this maṇḍapa in the 17th and 18th centuries.

inscription.head:
No. 78.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1909.)
TIRUKKAḺUKKUṈṞAM, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: At the entrance into one of the temple kitchens on the hill.

Of these miscellaneous writings, some record the signatures of the Dutch
officers who visited this place in the 17th and 18th centuries and some, in Tamil
and Telugu, record the donations of South Indians.

inscription.head:
No. 79.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1909.)
TIRUPPŌRŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On two pillars in the maṇḍapa in front of the Dēvayānaiyammaṉ shrine in the
Kandaśvāmi temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 27.

inscription.head:
No. 80.
(A.R. No. 77 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīśvara
temple.

This damaged record commences with Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara, the praśasti of the
king.

It registers a gift of cows and sheep for lamps to the temple of Mahādēva
at Tiruvāṉmiyūr in Kōṭṭūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam.

l.ta: muṭiyu muṉnavar pakkaṟ ṟeṉnavar vaitta cuntaramuṭiyu mi

inscription.head:
No. 81.
(A.R. No. 78 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the west wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīśvara
temple.

This damaged and incomplete record contains part of the praśasti of the
king which begins with Tiṅgaḷērtaru etc., and seems to record a gift of land
in Tiruvāṉmiyūr, after purchasing it from the residents of Tiruvāṉmiyūr in
Kōṭṭūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, for offerings,
evidently to the god of the place under orders of the king's officer in
charge of the kōṭṭam, named Peru Arasūr Uḍaiyān Sīruḍaikkaḻal of Ārkkāṭṭu-
kūṟṟam in Pāṇḍikulāśani-vaḷanāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 82.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīśvara
temple.

This incomplete inscription commences with Tirumaṉṉni vaḷara etc., the
praśasti of the king and mentions Kōṭṭūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅ
goṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and refers to māḍāpatya of the temple.

l.note: The letter ṭa is written below the letter tu •

inscription.head:
No. 83.
(A.R. No. 80 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīś-
vara temple.

This damaged inscription commences with Iraṭṭapāḍi ēḻarai ilakkamum,
etc. and mentions the Śivabrāhmaṇa Gautamaṉ of dēvadāna Tiruvāṉmiyūr
in Kōṭṭūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 84.
(A.R. No. 81 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīś-
vara temple.

This inscription commences with Tirumaṉṉi vaḷara, etc., the praśasti-
of the king and records the gift of 5 kāśu for the offering of garland during the
pūjā at noon daily of god Mahādēva of Tiruvāṉmiyūr by Kīḻmayānamaṅgala
kiḻāṉ ••• ••• •••• yaḍakki Gaṅgādharaṉ of Pūśipākkam alias Śrī-Parān-
takanallūr in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭu-nāḍu in Uṟṟukkāṭṭu-kkōṭṭam. The recipients of
the gift money were the members of the Tiruvuṇṇāḻigai-mūlasthāna-sabhā
of the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruvāṉmiyūr in Kōṭṭūr-nāḍu in Puliyūr-
kkōṭṭam.

l.ta: ñ cuḷḷicūḻ matiṭ koḷḷippākkaiyu naṇṇaṟ karumuraṉ ma-
ṇṇak kaṭakkamum porutiṭart tiḻattaracartamuṭiyum āṅkavar tevi-
yaroṅkeḻil muṭiyu muṉṉavar pakkal teṉṉavar vaitta cuntara-
keraḷar muṟaimayiṟ cūṭiya kulataṉamākiya palar pukaḻ muṭiyuñ ceṅka-
mpotu cārttiyaruḷuvataṟku vaitta kācu aiñcu ikkācu aiñcum i-
paṟittaṭṭuvārkku i[k*]koyilil paṇṭāranāḻiyāl nicata[m*] nāḻi nellāka

inscription.head:
No. 85.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the east wall of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīś-
vara temple.

This fragmentary inscription contains a portion of the praśasti of the king
and records a gift of money and paddy for offerings, evidently to god Mahā-
dēva of the place, and the undertaking given by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the
temple. Details not clear.

l.note: Beginnings of the lines are lost.
Continuation of the lines is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 86.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1909.)
TIRUVĀṈMIYŪR, ŚAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On the base of the Tripurasundarī-ammaṉ shrine in the Vēdapurīśvara
temple.
It records a gift of money and paddy for offerings to god Mahādēva.

inscription.head:
No. 87-99.
(A.R. Nos. 84 to 96 of 1909.)
JAUGAḌA, BERHAMPUR TALUK, GANJAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock in the hill.
Published in C.I.I., Vol. I, pages XV and XVI.

inscription.head:
No. 100.
(A.R. No. 97 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab fixed into the wall of the entrance to the Jagga-Rao Observatory.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 211.

inscription.head:
No. 101.
(A.R. No. 98 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a stone lying near Paravastu Raṅgāchārya's house in the same village.

This inscription records the gift of a maṇḍapa called Śanivāra-maṇḍapa
to the temple of Karumāṇi[k*]ka-Āḻvār at [Viśā]khapaṭṭiṉam alias Kulōt-
tuṅgaśōḻa-paṭṭaṇam by a merchant who was a native of Pandalāyini-kkollam.

l.note: Each line of writing of this record is underlined.
Cf. Kalaimagaḷ, Virōdhi, MAśI, p. 183. 1 = 229 A.D. ñanuary, February.

inscription.head:
No. 102.
(A.R. No. 99 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On another stone in the same place.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 651.

inscription.head:
No. 103.
(A.R. No. 100 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the back of the second stone lying near Paravastu Raṅgācharya's house.

The details of date, viz., Śaka 1102, Puraṭṭādi, daśamī, Monday, Pūśam,
correspond to 1180 A.D., September 15, f.d.t., ·40; f.d.n., ·20. The paksha
was bahuḷa. The cyclic year was Sarvari.

This damaged inscription seems to be a Tamil version of the Telugu record
published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 651 and refers to the gift of Aññuṟṟuvar
••• ••• •••• at Viśākhapaṭṭaṇa to a native of Malai-maṇḍala, by a merchant
of (Mātōṭṭam) alias Irāmakulavallipaṭṭaṇam and by “the Twelve Persons”
of Śrīmat-Viśākhapaṭṭaṇam alias Kolōttuṅgaśōḻapaṭṭaṇam.

edition.l: śāsaṉattiṉpaṭi[ kku viśākapa] ṭṭaṇattu

inscription.head:
No. 104.
(A.R. No. 101 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a tomb-stone on the Darga-hill in the village.

Published in Epigraphia Indo-Moslemica,
Vol. [?] No. [?]

inscription.head:
No. 105-107.
(A.R. Nos. 102 to 104 of 1909.)
VIŚĀKHAPAṬNAM, VISAKHAPATNAM TALUK, VISAKHAPATNAM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On two other tomb-stones in the same place.

Published in Epigraphia Indo-Moslemica.
Vol. [?] pp. [?]

inscription.head:
No. 108.
(A.R. No. 105 of 1909.)
KUPPALNATTAM, TIRUMANGALAM TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock near the Jaina images on the hill.

This damaged inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu characters of c. 10th century
records that five images were caused to be made by different persons like a lady
disciple and others (whose names are not clear) in the name of certain persons
(name lost).

inscription.head:
No. 109.
(A.R. No. 106 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Kāśi-Viśvānātha temple.

This inscription, of which the beginning of all the lines are lost, records
a gift of one achchu of money by Śōḻan Ilaṅkēśvaradēvaṉ to the temple of
Tiruvālandurai-uḍaiyār at Tiruvālandurai in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, for the decoration
(tiru-mēlpūchchu) of the idol.

l.ta:
vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ iraṇ[ṭu] koṅkumoṉṟāka āṇṭaruḷiṉa śrīvira-
rāce

••• ikkoyilil pūcikku[m*] nampiyār talaiyāka kaḻunir pokaṭṭuvāṉ kaṭai
yāka tevaraṭiyār uḷpaṭa āṇaimāṟum[ā]-

inscription.head:
No. 110.
(A.R. No. 107 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription records a sale of land by Amaṭṭaṉ-Kāri Ilaṅkaimāṉ,
a maṉṟāḍi of Iraṭṭaiyaṉpāḍi in Vaikāvi-nāḍu to Tambirān-tōḻaṉ an ascetic of
the Tirunīṟiṭṭān-tirumaḍam, situated in the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam of the temple
of Āḷuḍaiyār Muttiṟattīśiramūḍaiyār at Koḻumam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] virarāceṉtira tevaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāniṉṟa tirunalli
y[ā*]ṇṭu 15 vatiṉ etiṟ 4- vatu vaiykāvin[āṭṭu] iraṭṭaiyaṉpāṭiyil maṉ-
ṟāṭikaḷi-

l amaṭṭaṉ kāriyāṉa ilaṅkaimāṉeṉ karaivaḻināṭṭuk koḻumattu āḷuṭaiyār
muttiṟatticiramuṭaiyār tirumaṭaiviḷākattu tiruniṟiṭṭān tirumaṭatti-

ṟ kumpiṭṭirukkun tavaciyāṟ tampirāntoḻa[ṟ]ku nāṉ viṟṟukkuṭutta nilamāvatu
iṭṭikaittuṟai manṉaṟaiyil nāṅkaḷ ūriṉpakkal vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya puṟamacakkaṭa

nilaṅ kāṇiyaraikkāṇi muntirikaikku nāṉkellaiyāvatu kuḷattuvāykkālukku
meṟkum uḷḷur emperumāṉukku nāṉ tirunaṉtavaṉamākaviṭṭa kuṟaikku vaṭak-

kum peruvāykkālukku kiḻakkum kuḷattuvāykkālukkum peruvāykkālukkum maṭap
puṟamākak koṇṭa kol[lai*]kkut teṟkum iṉṉāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa ni-

cata emmilicainta vilaipporuḷ ciriyakki paḻañcalākai accu eṭṭum paḻaṅkācu
oṉṟukkum ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 111.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription in characters of about the 12th century, seems
to have recorded a grant of gold and lands to god Āḷuḍaiyār ••••• at Koḻumam
in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu by the Sabhā of a chaturvēdimaṅgalam (name lost), and
mentions Adhirādarāja-vāykkāl.

l.ta:
koṇṭatu ya ••• cempon irupattiru kaḻañcukkup pon kaḻañcukku ••
•••••
ṅkalamumāka nel muppatteḻāvatukku miṟaitaṅka kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu eṅ ••• •••

•• yākavana • nelluṅ kaḷattu vayalukkumāka [ti]ra[m]nāṟkalamāka vanta
nellumiṉ nā Fragment.-

inscription.head:
No. 112.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.
This fragmentary inscription mentions Koḻumam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 113.
(A.R. No. 110 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription of which the date portion is lost, records the
gift of 15 kaḻañju of gold by a person (name lost) for a perpetual lamp evidently
for a temple (name lost) and the undertaking given by three Śivabrāhmaṇas
named Chāmuṇḍan Chandraśēkhara of Kāśyapa-gōtra, Dakshiṇāmūrtti-
bhaṭṭaṉ and a third whose name is lost, who received the gold, for burning
the lamp.

edition.l:
tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukku vaittapon patinaiṅkaḻañcuk kuṭivaḻi 2 •••
•••••

l.ta: ḷakkuc celuttuvārāka ippaṭi cammatittu ippon koṇṭom civa

inscription.head:
No. 114.
(A.R. No. 111 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription of which the date portion is lost mentions
the maṉṟāḍi Amaṭṭaṉ-Kāri Ilaṅkai ••• •••, evidently the same as the one who
figures in No. 110 above.

inscription.head:
No. 115.
(A.R. No. 111-A of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary record mentions the maṭha Tirunīṟ[iṭṭāṉ] situated in
••• ••• •••• ram-uḍaiyār tirumaḍai-viḷāga, evidently the same as that
which is mentioned in No. 100 above.

inscription.head:
No. 116.
(A.R. No. 112 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.
This fragmentary record mentions Eṟipaḍainallūr in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 117.
(A.R. No. 113 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription of which the date portion is lost, mentions
Pārā-tiriyaṉ Aḻaga[ṉ] of Puḷḷamaṅgalam, Tambirāṉ-tōḻaṉ, an ascetic, and
a Brahmadē[śa] in Vaikāvi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 118.
(A.R. No. 114 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription dated in the 82nd day of the 21st year of the king records
the royal order granting 16 nāḻi of rice daily for food offerings to god Aruvarai-
āttatta-Piḷḷaiyār, set up at Koḻumam by some people (name not clear).

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ koḻumattil [ tantātata] •••• ku
nam olai kuṭutta paricāvatu ivarkaḷ namakku naṉṟāka eḻuntaruḷuvitta aru-
[va]raiyāttattappiḷḷaiyāṟku a-

mutupaṭikku ivarkaḷ nammaivantu veṇṭikkoṇṭamaiyil nāḷ oṉṟukku amutu-
paṭi arici patiṉaṟu nāḻiyāka muttavāṭ kutiraic cevakattile ••••

cilaiyilum veṭṭikkoḷka yāṇṭu irupattoṉṟāvatu nāḷ eṇpattiraṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 119.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription records a grant of land in Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr to a
maṭha (name not clear) in the Adirājēndran-tirumaḍai-viḷāgam in Koḻumam
by Gaṅgan-śōḻan Aṇukkappallavaraiyan alias Aṇukkanambi of Eḻśilūr in
Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu pattāvatu karaivāḻināṭṭu
eḻicil ūra • •

kaṅkancoḻan aṇu[kkappallavarai]yanāna aṇukkanampiyena koḻumattu atirāta-

tirunā ••••• tirumaṭattukku nān viṭṭanilam kaṇṇāṭipputtūril •••

inscription.head:
No. 120.
(A.R. No. 116 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the bali-pīṭha in the Veṅkaṭēśa-perumāḷ temple.

This fragmentary inscription mentions the god Adiya-śōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār
and a certain Iyakkatimiñcheya-pallavaraiyaṉ.

edition.l: svastiśrī [||*] virarājentradēvaṟku yāṇṭu 23 •••••

inscription.head:
No. 121.
(A.R. No. 117 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place.

This fragmentary inscription, of which the date portion is lost, probably
records a grant of 12 achchu of money by a person or persons (name not preserved)
for the decoration (tiru-mēlpūchchu), evidently of a deity (name not preserved).

inscription.head:
No. 122.
(A.R. No. 118 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records the royal order addressed to Anantanārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭan
alias Tiruvālukku-aḍiyaṉ of Aḻagar-Tirumalai in Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam informing
the grant of land, for the merit of the king, to the maṭha of the servants, engaged
in burning lamps, of the temple of Aḻagar-Tirumalai by Śeña •••• nārāyaṇan,
Āḷavandi alias Brahmapallavaraiyan of Śrī-Vīranārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam,
a brahmadēya in Vīrakēraḷa-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 123.
(A.R. No. 119 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

It records the gift of land in Vīraśōḻachaturvēdimaṅgalam in Vīrakēraḷa-
vaḷanāḍu, as tiruviḷakkuppuṟam, by Kuraṅgāṭṭu Śrī-Kṛishṇan of Śrī-Kumaraṅ-
gavīma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu to the temple
of Kariyapirāṉ for burning 16 lamps in the temple.

l.ta:
maṅkalattu mu ••• ••• •••• māvarai iṉṉilam oru
māvaraiyiluḷḷa pāṭṭaṅkoṇṭu nāḷ oṉṟukkut tirumāḷikaiyil Eṟṟivarum

inscription.head:
No. 124.
(A.R. No. 120 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This incomplete and damaged inscription refers to several flower-gardens
in Kumaraṅgabhīma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, named after Adhirādhirājaṉ,
Śaḍagōpan, Dēvakī-putraṉ, etc.

inscription.head:
No. 125.
(A.R. No. 120-A of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription dated in the 151st day of the 4th year of the king
record an order of the king to the residents of Koḻumam granting of some
land to the east of the tank called Kēraḷakēsarippērēri in Koḻumam together
with the incomes from levies like iṟai, puṟavu, siddhāyam, elavai, yugavai,
etc., as amāvāsippuṟam for offerings to god of a temple (name not preserved),
during the procession on the Amāvāsyā days.

l.note: Written in continuation of No. 123 above.

inscription.head:
No. 126.
(A.R. No. 120-B of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription, of which the beginning is lost, in characters of
about the 12th century, seems to record the order of the king, granting of land
and incomes from levies as abhishēka-dakshiṇā to god Karumāṇikkāḻvār and
of paddy for offerings and for the reciters of Tiruvāymoḻi.

inscription.head:
No. 127.
(A.R. No. 121 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription records the grant of land as maḍappuṟam to
god Kariyapirāṉ of Śrī-Kumaraṅgavīma-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya
in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu by Tiruppēṟpālāśiriyaṉ Śrī-Vāsudēvaṉ Śrī-Nārasiṅga-
bhaṭṭa-sōmayāji alias Vīraśōḻa-Brahmēndraṉ who is stated to have received
the land from Perumāḷ Vīraśōḻa-dēva.

inscription.head:
No. 128.
(A.R. No. 122 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription records an order of the king addressed to the resi-
dents of Koḻumam granting some thing (details not preserved) to meet the
expenses relating to the procession, during a festival, of the diety Karumāṇik-
kapirāṉ of Kumaraṅgabhīmach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

In continuation of this record are engraved two others which also seem to
be grants of land, etc., for the procession of the same deity, by some individuals.

kaikku nāṉ

inscription.head:
No. 129.
(A.R. No. 123 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This badly damaged inscription records the gift of paddy for offerings
to the god Karimāṇikkāḻvār at Kumaraṅgabhīma-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam by
the king.

inscription.head:
No. 130.
(A.R. No. 124 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This very much damaged inscription records an order of the king relating
to the grant of land and certain levies probably for raising and tending a flower-
garden. The other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 131.
(A.R. No. 124-A of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḻ temple.

This badly damaged inscription describes the king (name lost) as Para-
kēsarivarman Tribhu ••• ••• and seems to record a gift for offerings. The
other details are lost.

••• ••• tākavum ••• ••• •••• ṉāṅkaḷ celuttuvatākavum ••

inscription.head:
No. 132.
(A.R. No. 124-B of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḻ temple.

This very much damaged inscription describes the king (name lost) as
Parakēsarivarmaṉ and records the royal grant of land for a flower-garden,
named after the king, for the temple of Emberumāṉ Kariyapirāṉ at [Kuma-
raṅga]bhīmach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 133.
(A.R. No. 125 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This damaged inscription records the order of the king addressed to a
temple servant (name lost) granting a piece of land, together with iṟai, puravu
siddhāyam, elavai, ugavai, etc., situated on the eastern side of the big channel
at ••• ••• śōḻaṉmādēvinallūr to the servant who performed worship in a temple
(name lost) for the merit of the king on the Ēkādaśī day in the bright half of
Mārgaḻi coinciding with the natal star of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 134.
(A.R. No. 126 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west and south bases of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

It records the order of the king addressed to the servants of the temple
of Kariyapirāṉ assigning the paddy due as svāmibhōga to the Brahmachchakra-
vartis, the priests in the (various) temples (tirumuṟṟam) in all the three
districts, viz. Vaigāvi-nāḍu, Karaivaḻi-nāḍu and Vīrakēraḻa-vaḷanāḍu and
the dakshiṇā given to the Brahmachchakravartis in the temple of Kariya-
pirāṉ on such occasions as festivals for food offerings to god Śiṅgapperumāḷ
(i.e., Narasiṁha) set up in the temple by the Brahmachchakravartis.

koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭān kayapirāṉ koyilil cikāriyañ ceyvārkaḷukku nam

inscription.head:
No. 135.
(A.R. No. 127 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west and south bases of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records the gift of land by the Brahmachchakravartti Śrī-
vallabhan Sundarabāhu-bhaṭṭa-sōmayāji of Bhāradvāja gōtra, belonging
to Pullūr, for food offerings to the god Śiṅgapperumāḷ (i.e., Narasiṁha) set up
in the temple of Kariyapirāṉ by him.

inscription.head:
No. 136.
(A.R. No. 128 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records the royal order, addressed to two individuals in-
forming them of the gift of land to Nārāṇa-dāsaṉ of Vaṉkurugūr for maintaining
a flower-garden dedicated to the god Emberumāṉ Vēdanāyaka-perumāḷ of
Tirunārāyaṇapuram for the merit of the king.

inscription.head:
No. 137.
(A.R. No. 129 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records an order of the king addressed to the Śrī-Vaishṇavar
and the temple servants of Tiruvaraṅga in Śōḻamaṇḍalam granting lands
situated in the paṟṟu belonging to the sabhā of Kōvaṉputtūr alias Vīrakēraḷa-
nallūr in Pērūr-nāḍu for food offerings daily and on special occasions to the
god Aḻagiyamaṇavāḷa-perumāḷ.

l.ta:
koṉermaikoṇṭāṉ coḻamaṇṭalattut tiruvaraṅkat tiruppati-
yil śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷukkum cikāriyañceyvārkaḷukkum nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu
aḻakiyamaṇavāḷaperumāḷukku

[patiya]mutu cirappu amutukku nāḷḷoṉṟukku nel kalamāka āṇṭoṉṟukku
munnūṟṟaṟuppatiṉ kalattuk[ku*] nām viṭṭa nilamāvatu perurnāṭṭu kovaṉputtū-
rāṉa virakeraḷanallūr

maṉṉaṟaiyil mutaṟcatirattu nālāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu nilannālumāvum
iraṇṭāñcatirattu nālāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu nilam nālumāvum iraṇṭāñcatirattu

ukavai maṟṟu[m*] eppeṟpaṭṭatuṅ koḷḷātomākavum immaṉṉaṟai orupūviḷai-
yiluḷ innilam pattu mā-

maṇṭalattu śrīvaiṣṇavarkaḷ

inscription.head:
No. 138.
(A.R. No. 130 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south base of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This incomplete inscription records an order of the king addressed to the
Nagara-dāsa of Vaṉkurugūr granting lands in Paṟutikulach-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam situated to the east of the tank at Koḻumam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu
for maintaining a flower-garden named after the king dedicated by Jananātha-
brahmādhirāyan to the god Vēdanāyaka-perumāḷ, for the merit of the king.

l.ta:
rāyan namperāl namakku naṉṟāka vetanāyakap
perumāḷukkuttā •••• cai ceyvikka tirunantavaṉattukku jīvaṉattukku

inscription.head:
No. 139.
(A.R. No. 131 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records the gift of 10 achchu for food offerings to the god
Kariyapirāṉ at Śrī Kumaraṅgabhīma chchaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya,
in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, by Dēvaṉ, Śilambaṉ alias Tribhuvaṉagaṅgadēva a feuda-
tory (Sāmanta) of the king.

l.ta: tu ceytaruḷa perumāḷ cāmantaril tevaṉ ci-
t toṭukkiṉa accup pattu accoṉṟukku uṭ[ai]-

rupputiyitu taṉmam cantirātittava[ṟ] śrīpaṇṭāra mutalile celutti

inscription.head:
No. 140.
(A.R. No. 132 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

The inscription gives the details of date viz., Khara, Āḍi 10, which may
correspond to 1411 A.D., July 8 (Monday). The Śaka year was 1333.

It records a royal dānapatra (deed) to the Śrīvaishṇavas and Poruḷāḷa-dāsa,
the Sthānikaṉ in charge of the Śrīkāryam of the temple of Emberumāṉ Kariya-
pirāṉ at Agaram Kumaraṅgabhīman alias Paridārasahōdarach-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam, communicated to them by the king's agent Tiruvāḻvān of
Bhārgava-gōtra belonging to Perumarudūr. The deed related to the grant of 20
mā of land on the outskirts of Tiruvālanduṟai and some amount of poṉ in lieu
of the irai and siddhāyam in respect of this land as tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam to the
god Kariyapirāṉ. It is stated that of this land one-third portion was to be
deducted as it was assigned to the god Kumaraṅgabhīmīśvaram-uḍaiyār at
Melaik-Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr.

inscription.head:
No. 141.
(A.R. No. 133 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the west wall of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription, which is slightly damaged, records the order of the king
addressed to the Śrīvaishṇavas and the person in charge of Śrīkārya of the
temple of Kariyapirāṉ in Kumaraṅgabhīmach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Karai-
vaḻi-nāḍu granting lands in a number of chaturvēdimaṅgalas (names specified)
together with elavai, ugavai, etc., excluding kaḍamai, as puravūrt-tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam,
evidently to the god Kariyapirāṉ. Agaram Adhirādhirādhich-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam is also mentioned.

l.ta:
maṅkalattu kariyapirān tirumuṟṟattu śrī vaiṣṇavarkaḷukkum śrīkāriyam
ceyvāṉukkum nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu ivvemperumāṉ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam

inscription.head:
No. 142.
(A.R. No. 134 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription records a gift of paddy for food offerings to the god Kari-
yapirāṉ of Kumaraṅgaviyamach-charuppēdimaṅgalam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu by
Vīrandaḻiyaṉ alias Udaiyādichchadēvaṉ who is also stated to have purchased
from Aḻagapperumān of Śōḻamādēvi of Karaivaḻi-nāḍu some land described
as Kariyapirāṉ kuḍinīṅgāt-tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam.

edition.l: ṇṭu 27- vatu virantaḻiyaṉāna utaiyā-

inscription.head:
No. 143.
(A.R. No. 135 of 1909.)
KOMARALIṄGAM, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the Karivaradarāja-perumāḷ temple.

This inscription dated in the 60th day of the 25th year of the king records
his order granting a land called Paṉṉirukulavaṉ situated in the village of
Ulaguḍaiyapirāṭṭich-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu which was
separated from Karaivaḻi-nāḍu for food offerings to the ten Āḻvārs in the
temple of the god Aḻagar (in the standing pose) at Tirumāliruñjōlai in Kīḻ-
Iraṇiyamuṭṭa-nāḍu, a sub-division of Pāṇḍi-maṇḍalam, during the
Vīrarājēndran-sandhi named after the king.

l.note: This letter mu is written below the line just underneath a •

inscription.head:
No. 144.
(A.R. No. 136 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

This inscription records the setting up of (an image of) Āḷuḍaiyār Nittaniṉrā-
ḍuvār (i.e., Naṭarāja) in place of the old one, probably damaged during the
war of the northerners, of the temple of Āḷuḍaiyār Vīrachōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār
at Koḻumum (i.e., Saṅgrāmanallūr) in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, by a certain Āḷavandāṉ
Aviṉāśi alias Kachchiyarāyaṉ. It also records his gift of lands, to provide
for offerings to the same deity, at Kaḍaṟṟūr alias Irāsarāsanallūr in Karaivaḻi-
nāḍu, purchased for certain specified amounts of money, in āvaṇach-śrī-yakki-
paḻañchalāgai coins.

vaṉac cakkaravattikaḷ śrī virarācentiratevarkkut
tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāniṉṟa tirunalliyāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 145.
(A.R. No. 137 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records that the king caused the construction of a temple
and the installation in it of (the liṅga) called Vīrachōḻa-īśvaram-uḍaiyār after
him, at Koḻumam to mark the occasion of the solar eclipse that occurred on
the day of the ruler's asterism of nativity. It further records the grant of the
(right of worship permanently) in the temple and other grants made by
the king to a certain Ejñamutti Mūlasthānamuḍaiyān alias Kaṇṇabhaṭṭaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 146.
(A.R. No. 138 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same Wall.

This records a gift, made by the king, of land and certain privileges to
the architect named Maṇiyaṉ Kayilāyam alias Vīrachōḷa-āchāriyaṉ who built
the temple of Vīrachōlīśvaram-uḍaiyār at Koḻumam. It further records
that the architect would arrange for the setting up of the images (of the deities)
in metal and stone in, and for doing repairs to, the temple as well as for pro-
viding paintings in the temple including the Tirunaḍaimāḷigai and the Tiruv-
eḍuttukkaṭṭi.

l.ta:
koṉeriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ ciṟpācāriyaṉ maṇiyaṉ kayilāyamāṉa
viracoḻa ācāriyaṉukku

nammolai kuṭutta paricāvatu koḻumattu āḷuṭaiyār viracoḻicuramuṭaiyāraiyum
eḻuntaruḷu-

inscription.head:
No. 147.
(A.R. No. 139 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged. It appears to record a gift of land in Vīranā-
rāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya, in Vaikāvi-nāḍu, to the god
Vīrachōḷa-īśvaramuḍaiyār in Koḻumam. One of the boundaries of the land is
stated to be Chēraṉai-mēṉkoṇḍa-chōḻaṉ-peruvadi (road ?).

l.ta:
yaṇa mannaṟaiyiliṟ ceraṉai meṉkoṇṭacoḻaṉ peruvatikkuk kiḻakkun tiruvaraṅka-
vāykkālukku vaṭakku mutaṟ kaṇṇāṟṟu añcāñcaturaṟattuṇṭattu meṟkaṭaiya nilame

araimāvum ivvatikku meṟku ivvāykkālukku vaṭakku iraṇṭāyiraṅ

kiḻkaṭaiya nila • •

inscription.head:
No. 148.
(A.R. No. 140 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same shrine.

This inscription is incomplete. It records some grant to the servants
of the temple of Vīrachōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār in Koḻumam, by the citizens (Naga-
rattār) of Eṟipaḍanallūr in Tuvarāpati-nāḍu of Oṟṟimmal in Uṟaiyūrk-
kūṟṟam in Irāśagaṁbhīra-vaḷanāḍu in Chōḷa-maṇḍalam.

inscription.head:
No. 149.
(A.R. No. 141 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.note: The year is given as 20 (i.e., 1202-03 A.D.) in A.R.S.I.E., for 1909-10.

inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged. It appears to record a gift of land in the
village of Vīranārāyaṇa-chaturvēdimaṅgalam in Vīrakēraḷa-vaḷanāḍu.
Mentions Vīrakēraḷa-mannaṟai and Vīrākēraḷa-vāykkāl as well as a land
belonging to a certain Vīranārāyaṇa-brahmārāyan.

inscription.head:
No. 150.
(A.R. No. 142 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

It records a gift of paddy made by a certain Śirināyakan alias Vīrachōḷa-
Nuḷamban who was a member of the temple executive committee to provide
daily one uḻakku of oil for a lamp given by the same person to the temple of
Vīrachōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār. In this connection Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr in Kaṟaivaḻi-
nāḍu is enjoined to measure out a certain quantity of paddy.

l.ta:
āvatu koyilatikārañ ceyvārkaḷiṟ
cirināyakanāna viracoḻaṉuḷampanen āḻuṭaiyār viracoḻi

ramuṭaiyārkku vaitta tiruṉuntāviḷakkonṟukku nāḷonṟukkeṇṇai uḻakkāka
nāḷ munṉūṟṟaṟupatukkeṇṇai toṇṇūṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 151.
(A.R. No. 143 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ Vīrarājēndradēva: No date: c. 1207-52 A.D.

inscription.p:
It is incomplete and damaged. It refers to the devastation caused to
Kaṟaivaḻi-nāḍu during the king's invasion and to the damages done to the
temples in a number of places (in the nāḍu). It records the gift made by the
king of the village Iraṭṭaiyaṉpāḍi in Vaikāvi-nāḍu as a dēvadāna to a number of
temples including the temple of Vīrachōḻīśvaram-uḍaiyār, etc., probably of
Koḻumam, the temple of Tirumaruduḍaiyār of Kaḍaṟṟūr, etc., It is stated that
this gift excludes the dēvadānas, tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam and maṉṟāḍu already existing
in the village and that the existing tenants would continue there and no tenant
from another village should be allowed to be a tenant here.

ētat viśvamahipālamaulibṛndrairalaṅkṛtam [||*] virarājēndradēvasya
śāsanam śāśvatam bhuvi [||*] tribhu

inscription.head:
No. 152.
(A.R. No. 144 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

It refers to the king as one who ruled the two Koṅgu countries as one. It
records a gift of land in Paḻani in Vaikāvi-nāḍu to provide for food offerings to
the god Vīrachōḷa-īśvaramuḍaiyār, made by Gaṇḍarādichchan-śēṭṭi alias
Adirādirājakkaṇḍiyadēvan, a sāmanta (feudatory) of the king. Among the
lands mentioned in connection with the boundaries of one of the gift lands is one
called Śivapādaśēkhara.

inscription.head:
No. 153.
(A.R. No. 145 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

p.b: Tribhº Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ (Vikramachōḷa): No date: c. 13th century.

inscription.p:
This inscription is much damaged and its lower part is not available. It
appears to record a gift made by the king of land in Kallāpuram alias Vikra[ma*]-
chōḻanallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, to a certain Kaṇḍan Kāvan alias Śivapattaṉ
(Śivabhaktan).

l.ta: ril nām ivaṉukku iṭṭa •••• onṟukku ni-

inscription.head:
No. 154.
(A.R. No. 146 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This is built in at the beginning and seems to be incomplete. It appears
to record a gift of land made by a certain Āḻvi alias Vikkiramachōḻa-bhuvana-
chchak[kiravartti] in favour of the tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple of
Vīrachōḻīśuramūḍaiyār in Koḻumam in Karavaḻi-nāḍu. Also mentions the
village of Ōḍattuṟai.

inscription.head:
No. 155.
(A.R. No. 147 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

This inscription records a gift of land made by a certain Iyāḻvallāṉ
Pāṇḍiyan alias Vikkiramachōḻa Iruṅgōḷaṉ, a mudali of the king. The lands
gifted are stated to be situated in Kīraṉūr in Poṅgalūṟkkā-nāḍu. The gift
was made to the god of the temple of Vīrachōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār in Koḻumum,
and it was for the merit of the donor and for celebrating the Kārttigai festival,
for food offerings to the god and for the taking out in procession of the deity.

l.ta: ṉakku naṉṟāka nāṉ kaṇṭa tirukkāttikai taṉma-
ḷḷaiyaṉ ceykku meṟkum naṭuvuḷpaṭṭa nilam araikkā-

inscription.head:
No. 156.
(A.R. No. 148 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete at the end. It records a gift of paddy,
made by the king, to provide food daily for three ascetics (tapassiyar) of the
Tiruttoṇḍattogaiyāṉ-maṭham in the Vīrachōḻan-tirumaḍaiviḷāgam of the temple
of Vīrachōḷīśvaram-uḍaiyār in Koḻumam. It is stipulated that one tapassiyar
would chant the Tirumuṟai and offer worship to the deity and another would
attend to the preparation of the bed-chamber of the deity. The work allotted
to the third tapassiyar is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 157.
(A.R. No. 149 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete and damaged. It records a gift of paddy,
made by the king, to a private individual named Kaṇḍaṉ Adiyaṉ alias
Vaḷaviṉāṉ Uḍaiyān, in the village of Kallāpuram alias Vīraśōḻanallūr, a dēva-
dāna of the god Vīrachōḻīśvaramuḍaiyār. It appears that this donee was
enjoined to render certain services to the god Vīrachōḻīśvaramuḍaiyār.

l.ta: ṉ uṭaiyānukku nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu ā-
kku iṭṭa manṟāṭu oṉṟukku viṭṭanilam murukkan

inscription.head:
No. 158.
(A.R. No. 150 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Parakēsarivarmaṉ alias Tribhº Vīra-chōḷadēva: Year 21 = 1202-04 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription The two other items included along with this in A.R.S.I.E., 1909, are missing.is incomplete. It refers to the king as one who ruled
the two Koggu together, and records a gift of land, made by the king, to
provide for offerings to the god of VīrachōḻIśvaramuḍaiyār who was set up
and consecrated in Koḻumam for the merit of the king.

vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ koṅkiraṇṭum
oṉṟāka āṇṭa śrī viracoḻa

inscription.head:
No. 159.
(A.R. No. 151 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South base of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

This inscription Stones are stated to be out of order in A.R.S.I.E., 1909.contains an order of the king addressed to the servants
etc., of the temple of VīrachōḷIśvaramuḍaiyār in Koḻumam recording a gift
of paddy, made by the king, to the deity Piḷḷai Perumpiḷḷai Āḻvār for providing
offerings on the occasion of the durbār function of the deity held on the day of
the asterism Bharaṇi in the month of Chittirai for the merit of the king. It is
attested by Vairādarāyaṉ.

koṉeriṉmai koṇṭān karaivaḻināṭṭu koḻu[ma*]ttu āḷuṭai-
yār viracoḻaī
