inscription.head:
No. 160.
(A.R. No. 152 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same base.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records a gift made by the
king of the village of Umāparamēśvarinallūr in Kāvāḍikkā-nāḍu as dēvadāna,
together with its existing tenants, to the goddess in the temple of Virachōḻayīś-
varamuḍaiyār. It is stipulated that the various taxes, paid in the form of
kaḻañjus (gold coins) by the tenants of the gift village, should be paid as the
villagers of the dēvadāṉa Dēvanpāḍi did. It is further stated that the taxes
should be received and remitted to the temple treasury by the temple tānattārs
(trustees) and not by the members of the temple executive council.
It is attested by Vīrarājēndra-Piramap-Pallavaraiyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 161.
(A.R. No. 153 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same base.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It contained also other
particulars of date but owing to the damage suffered by the record, all that
remains of these is the 5th tithi of the bright half of the month.

It records the sale of land by public auction held at Kēraḷakēsarinallūr in
Karaivaḻi-nāḍu. It states that the land belonged to maṉṟādi Vilāḍattaraiyaṉ
of the above-mentioned place and that the sale by auction was intended to pay
off the fine imposed on him for the destruction he caused to the treasury of the
god Tiruvālanduṟai-uḍaiyār. It is stated that the land was purchased for
10(1/2) achchus by a certain Kaṇṇabhaṭṭan, a servant of the temple of Vīrachōḷīś-
varamuḍaiyār, on behalf of the temple treasury.

miyum kiḻamai ••• ••• poḻutu mu ••• •••• nāḷitu paru-

inscription.head:
No. 162.
(A.R. No. 154 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

It records an order of the king addressed to the servants of the temple of
Vīrachōḻīśaramuḍaiyār, remitting the taxes such as polivu, poṟpū, elavai,
yugavai, and svāmibhōgam in favour of the inhabitants living within the temple
premises defined by four specified boundaries.

inscription.head:
No. 163.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Kōṉērinmēṉkoṇḍāṉ: Vīra-chōḷa ?: No date: accn. 1183 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged slightly. It registers the gift, made by the
king of paddy in favour of the Piḍāri temple of the ugachchar in a village (name
lost) in Vaikāvi-nāḍu and the Kāḷā-piḍāri temple of Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, the gift
being entrusted to a certain Kaṇṇabhaṭṭaṉ, a dēvakanmi of the temple of
Vīrachōḻīśvaramuḍaiyār. It is stipulated that this person should render certain
services in lieu of the gift-paddy. It is attested by Uttaramantiri.

inscription.head:
No. 164.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the endorsement of the king to the gift of land,
made by Jayaṁgoṇḍaśōḻi alias Ulaguḍaiya-mukkōkkiḻāṉaḍigaḷ, the daughter
of Vīrachōḻadēva who ruled the two Koṅgus as one. The land appears to
have been situated in a place Irājarāja •••• in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu. The gift is
stated to have been entrusted to one Kaṇṇabhaṭṭaṉ, a servant in the temple of
Vīrachōḷīśvaramuḍaiyār, and it was for doing certain services to the deity
Nittaniṉṟāḍuvār (i.e., Nat6arāja).
The record is signed by Uttaramantiri.

kaṉmi kaṇṇapaṭṭaṉukku namolai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu āḷuṭaiyār nitta niṉṟāṭu-

inscription.head:
No. 165.
(A.R. No. 156-A of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription in two pieces is damaged and does not seem to be complete.

It appears to record the gift of money to provide oil for some lamps
given to the temple at Koḻumam.

inscription.head:
No. 166.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the gift, made by the king, for the merit of
māmaḍi (uncle), of the village of Dēvanpāḍi alias Vīranārāyaṇanallūr in Kāvā-
ḍikkā-nāḍu, with all its taxes, in favour of the image of the deity Tribhuvana-
sundarar setup in the temple of Vīrachōḻīchchuramuḍaiyār and for the celeb-
ration of the marriage (of the deity). It is stated that the village was gifted
as a dēvadāna to provide for lamps in the temple. It is further stated that
the supervision of the gift was not to be done by the sāmantars and the executive
authorities but only by the Śrīmāhēśvarar and the temple servants.

l.note: These letters seem to have been wantonly erased.
l.ta:
••• ••• •••• nam olai kuṭuttaparicāvatu māmaṭikku naṉṟākat tiripu-
vanacuntararai eḻuntaruḷuvittu tirukkalliyāṇam paṇṇukiṟapotu kāvāṭikkā
nāṭṭu tevanpāṭiyāna viranārāyaṇanallūril iṟai puravu

•• peṟu antarāyappeṟu maṉṟupāṭu teṇṭakuṟṟa maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭutum kiḻpuravu-
mayiṟkāṇamu maṟṟum epperpaṭṭatum ikkoyilukku cantiyā tipattukku uṭalāka
koṇṭu varuvatāka ivvūr tevatāṉamāka nīrvārttukkuṭuttom ivvūr
ārāyumiṭattu cāmantaratikārañ ceyvārkaḷ ārāyakkaṭavarallavākavum •••••
ṇṭattu ikkoyi

rāñcamutal tiruviḷakkukkuṭalāka kuṭuttom nantamāranār pāṟpeṭuttukkuṭukka
ivai viranārāṇa viraciṅkatevan eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 167.
(A.R. No. 157-A of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: Vīranārāyaṇa (?): No date: c. 1139-49 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the grant made by the king, to the servants of the
deity of the temple of Vīrachōḻīśvaramuḍaiyār, of paddy from a village in
Oṉpadukarai-nāḍu to provide for the food-offerings to god Sundaraperumāḷ
Vināyakappiḷḷaiyār and for the images of the god Tirubhuvanasundarar and
to the temple of the goddess Nāchchiyār which were set up for the merit
of the king's māmaḍi (uncle) and for taking out in procession (of the images
of the above deities) on the day in the month of Puraṭṭāśi when the asterism
Sadaya, the star of nativity of the king, occurs.

l.ta:
vakanmikaḷukku nam Olai kuṭu[t*]tapaṭiyāvatu oṉpatukarai nāṭṭu
tava ••• ••• •••• māṭakkoyil pūvaḻi oṉṟukku civap

inscription.head:
No. 168.
(A.R. No. 157-B of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: Vīranārāyaṇa (?): No date: c. 1139-49 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription, which is slightly damaged, records a grant of land in
Tiruvālanduṟai alias Kēraḷakēsarinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, made by the
king and entrusted to a certain Kaṇṇabhaṭṭaṉ, a servant of the temple of Vīra-
chōḻīśvaramuḍaiyār at Koḻumam to provide for food-offerings to the deity
Dakshiṇāmūrttī, set up in the above-mentioned temple (for his own merit).
Also records an order stipulating that the maṉṟāḍis should measure out oil for
the sandhyā-dīpam and provide for the procession of Śrībali.

l.ta:
koneriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ karaivaḻināṭṭuk koḻumattu āḷuṭaiyā[r*]
viracoḷiccaramuṭaiyār koyilil tevakaṉ-

mi kaṇṇapaṭṭaṉukku nam olai kuṭuttapaṭiyā[va*]tu na ••• •••• eḻun
taruḷuvitta

vaṟku amutupaṭikku viṭṭanilam iṉṉāṭṭut tiru

tukku meṟkum poypilukku vaṭakkum peruvāykkālukku kiḻakkum teṟkum

inscription.head:
No. 169.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base of the maṇḍapa in front of the Naṭarāja shrine in the same temple.

p.b: No king: Ś. 1267, wrong for 1167 (?) Pārthiva.

inscription.p:
The Śaka year is apparently wrong for 1167 It may be assigned to c.
13th century.

Some of the stones seem to have been lost, and consequently some passages
of this record are missing.

It refers to a certain Kettaya-daṇḍanāyakkaṉ, stated to be the eldest
son of one ••• ••• •••• tappa-daṇḍanāyakkaṉ who was perhaps a feudatory
of the Hoysaḷa king (name lost).

It records that Kettaya-daṇḍanāyakaṉ, for the victory and prosperity
of his younger brother Siṅgaya-daṇḍanāyakkaṉ and for achieving all his desired
objectives, granted lands, together with all the associated things, as sarvamānya,
in Oṭṭaikkumīṇḍāṉ in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu in Koṅgu-maṇḍala, to the mahājanas
of Śrīmādhava-chaturvvēdimaṅgalam and of Teṉṉvadaraiya-chaturvvēdi-
maṅgalaṁ. A certain Kaṇṇappaṉ Teṉṉavadaraiyaṉ, the uḍaiyāṉ of Śeḻu-
vaṉūr is stated to have established the village of Teṉṉavadaraiya-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam. It is also stated that on the occasion of the makara-saṁkrānti
in the Uttarāyaṇa of the last Subhānu-saṁvatsara, the mahājanas of Śrī-
mādhavachaturvvēdimaṅgalam were given some lands as iṟaiyili.

dayanimittamākavum nammuṭaiya sakalābhiṣṭasiddhyarttham ••• śrīmādhavacaturvvēdi-

vataraiyaṉ koṅkumaṇṭala

inscription.head:
No. 170.
(A.R. No. 159 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same base, in the same place as above.

p.b: [H2oysaḷa] Sōmēśvaradēva: Śaka 1[2]65, wrong for 1165 Subhānu = c. 1243-44 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged in some places. It is dated Śaka 1265, Śubhānu
Tai-śu, 11 Kārttigai is wrong for 1165 Śaka year.

It refers to the Mahāpradhāna Kettaya-[daṇṇāyakkaṉ] son of the Mahā-
pradhāna Mādappa-daṇṇāyakkaṉ who was probably a feudatory of the Hoysaḷa
king [Vīra-Sō]mēśvara. It records the grant of land to Brāhmaṇas by Kettaya-
daṉṉāyakkaṉ probably for the victory and prosperity of his brother Siṅgaya-
daṇṇāyakaṉ and for the achievement of his own desired ends. The gift land
was probably situated in Oṭṭaikkumīṇḍāṉ in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu in Koṅgu-maṇḍala.
A certain Kaṇṇappaṉ Teṉṉavadaraiyaṉ, the uḍaiyāṉ of Śeḻuvaṉūr is
mentioned.

l.gr:
ditha davya śrīpādapatmārāyaka muṭīkulayakamalamāttāṇḍa nilagiri
sādhāra

m ceḻuvaṉūruṭai-
yāṉ kaṇṇappaṉ teṉṉavataraiyaṉ koṅkumaṇṭalattu karaivaḻi [amara]nāṭṭu
teṉ muṟ ••• ••• ••••
na āvaṇakkaḷariyile kāṭam kaiccilavaṟac cilavaṟuttu inta

kavum koṇṭuṭaiyaṉāṉa itteṉ-

pavippārkaḷākavum inta nilattālluḷ

inscription.head:
No. 171.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the floor in front of the same maṇḍapa.

This is a fragmentary inscription. It records the name of the ruler and the
regnal year and mentions Pāṇḍi-maṇḍ[alam].

inscription.head:
No. 172.
(A.R. No. 161 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Second slab in the same place.

p.b:
Sundara-pāṇḍyadēva (Ruler of Koṅgu ?) Year 37 = c. 1302 A.D.
(contemporary of Vīra-pāṇḍya and Vikrama-chōḷa)

inscription.p:
This is also a fragmentary record. It appears to record a gift of paddy and
mentions Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 173.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third slab in the same place.

This inscription is incomplete at the end. It mentions a certain Vīdiviṭaṅka-
nayiṉār who imposed a fresh tax on the tānattār (trustees) inhabiting the
premises of the temple of Aḻagiya-Tiruchchiṟṟaṁbalamuḍaiyāṉ in Koḻumam.
The imprecatory passage at the end says that those who do not obey would be
deemed to have wronged the deity Nittaniṉṟāḍuvār (i.e., Naṭarāja).

inscription.head:
No. 174.
(A.R. No. 163 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Agnīśvara shrine in the same temple.

This inscription records a gift of land as iṟaiyili, made by the king, in
Tiruvālanadurai alias Vikramachōḻach-chaturvvēdimaṅgalam to those who
recited the Mahābhārata in the village. A certain Villavadaraivan figures as
signatory.

inscription.head:
No. 175.
(A.R. No. 164 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

This inscription records a gift of land, made by the king, to provide for
food offerings and other services to the deity Chokkaṉār represented by an āśraya-
liṅga set up by a certain Tillaivināyakaṉ in the temple of Vīrachōḷīśvaramuḍ-
aiyār at Koḻumam. The gift-land is stated to be situated in the village Ulagu-
ḍaippirāṭṭi-chaturvvēḍimaṅgalam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu. The signatories were
Villavadaraiyaṉ and Toṇḍaimāṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 176.
(A.R. No. 165 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is a copy of No. 175 above, addressed to tānattār (trus-
tees) of the Vīrachōḻīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Koḻumam.

l.ta: lām paṟṟukku kiḻakku nilam oṉpatu māva-
yam oṭṭaccu ārāṭci elavai yukavai kā-

inscription.head:
No. 177.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the central shrine in the same temple.

p.b: No King: (Vīrarājēndra): Vīḷambi, Āṉi 3 = May 29, 1238 A.D.

inscription.p:
This is a late record. It registers the gift of the six villages, viz., Vīra-
chōḻapuram, Irraṭṭayaṁpāḍi, Mūlaikkallāpuram, Śiṉṉākkalaimaṟi, Mēlaik-
kaṇṇāḍipputtūr and Tēṉi in favour of (the deity) Nittiyappar, by Āṇḍaperu-
māḷayyaṉ, the agent of a certain [.]shamaṇappa-nāyakkar-ayyaṉ. The record
was written by Chidambaranāthan on the orders of Śevandinātha-piḷḷai, the
temple accountant.

inscription.head:
No. 178.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the gōpura of the same temple.

p.b: No King: (Vīrarājēndra): No date: c. 1207-52 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the soldiers must protect the temple of
Vīrachōḻa-īśvaramuḍaiyār, its gōpuram and its premises.

inscription.head:
No. 179.
(A.R. No. 168 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the floor of the Varadarāja-perumāḷ temple in the same
village.

This inscription records that this door-frame (probably the one on which
the record is engraved) and the door-frame of the inner shrine were the gifts
of Uḍaiyāṉ Arumaṇidēvagaḷāśiriyamuḍaiyāṉ of Oramalaichchēri in Mīṉam-
alai-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 180.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1909.)
SAṄGRĀMANALLŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock at Kōviltuṟai in the same village.

p.b:
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Sāvaṇa-Uḍaiyār: Siddhārthi, Dhanus-ba. 5:
Tuesday, Maghā = 1379 A.D. November 29, f.d.n. ·10.
The cyclic year falls in Śaka 1301.

inscription.p: This inscription is damaged in some places.

It records a settlement arrived at amongst themselves, relating to the
enjoyment permanently of certain lands already in the possession of the
Mahājanas belonging to Agaramputtūr alias Śrīmādhavach-chaturvvēdi-
maṅgalam which was seperated from Teṉmūṟ Oṭṭaikkumiṇḍāṉ in Karaivaḻi-
nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 181.
(A.R. No. 170 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the temple of Avanāśiśvara.

This inscription records the endowment of three śalākai of Śrīyakki achchu
with the temple to provide for food-offerings during one sandhi to the deity
Kshētrapālappiḷḷaiyār by a certain Vīrapperumāḷ Edirilālādāṉ, a Piḷḷai of the
king.

l.ta: ••• muṭpaṭa śrīpaṇṭārattil oṭukkina accucciriyakki muṉṟu calākai ••• •••

koṇṭu celvatāka kalveṭṭuviccen etirilātāneṉ itu cantirātittavaṟ
celvatāka

inscription.head:
No. 182.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete. It records the undertaking given by a
certain [••••] Aviṅāśi alias Śīkāḻiyān of Kāśyapa-gōtra, a Śivabrāh-
maṇa having a right in the temple, to burn 32 sandhyādīpas in the temple with
the oil endowed during his grandfather's time as well as oil and other things
endowed now. Also records the undertaking given probably by the same person
to feed ascetics during the midday worship in the temple. With the help of
the amount of ten kaḻañju of gold endowed by Āṟṟūruḍaiyāṉ Tiruppuṟampiya-
muḍaiyāṉ.

l.ta:
kovirācakecaripaṟmarāṉa tiripuvanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvirarācentiratevaṟku yāṇṭu
patiṉaiñcāvatu ikkoyiliṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya

nāciyāna cikāḻiyānen [e?]n pāṭṭanmār munpu koṇṭa eṇṇai upaiyattinālum
••• ••• •••• eṇṇai upaiyattinālum palavakaiyālum nān erikka
kaṭava canti-

yā tipam muppattiraṇṭum eṟāḷapurattu viyāpāri āṟṟūruṭaiyān ttiruppuṟampiya
muṭaiyān te ••••• vaitta paṭṭaippoti coṟṟukkup pon tuḷai patin
kaḻaiñcukku nā-

mattiyaccantiyile paratecikaḷukku ippaṭṭaippoti coṟukuṭuppatāka ippon
nuṅkaikko-

inscription.head:
No. 183.
(A.R. No. 172 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the gift of one sandhyā lamp to the deity Avināśi
Āḷuḍaiyār and an endowment of some Paḻañjalākai achchu to provide for burn-
ing the lamp by Marudanmalaiyaṉ alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻa-Vikkiramāditta-
dēvan, a sāmanta (feudatory) of the king.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ cirikulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu
munṟāvatuk ketirāvatu peru-

māḷ cāmantaril marutanmalaiyanāna kulottuṅkacoḻa vikkiramātittatevanen
āḷuṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 184.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records the gift of one
sandhyā lamp to the deity Aviṉāśiyāṇḍār and an endowment of one kaḻañju of
poṉ to provide for burning the lamp, by a certain Uyyavandāṉ Avināśi alias
Viratamiṭṭamālai, probably a sāmanta (feudatory) of the king.

l.ta:
[rumāḷ] ••• ••• aviṉāciyāṉa viratamiṭṭamālaiyeṉ āḷuṭaiyār aviṉāci-
yāṇṭāṟku vaitta ca-

ntiyātipam onṟukkup pon kaḻaiñcum kuṭaṅkoṇṭu koyil pukuvāṉe celuttu-
vāṉāka kuṭut •••••

•••• veṭṭivicceṉ uyyavantāṉ aviṉāciyeṉ itu cantirātittavaṟ celva-
tākap paṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 185.
(A.R. No. 174 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

A portion of this inscription is lost. It records the gift of one hundred
achchu (coins) to provide for food-offerings (probably to the deity Avināśi
Āḷuḍaiyār), by a person (name lost). It mentions a certain brāhmaṇa named
Śivallan.

l.ta:
ṇṭārattil oṭukkiṉa accu nūṟu ituṅkaikkoṇṭu amutupaṭi cāttupaṭi pūccu-
paṭiyum ••• ••••

pp[ā*]ṉ pātan talaimelaṉ itu cantirā-
titta celva-

inscription.head:
No. 186.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ Vīrarājēndra: No date: c. 1207-52 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records an order of the king addressed to the servants of
the deity Āḷuḍaiyār, granting the village of Pāppārpūṇḍi alias Vīrarājēndra-
nallūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu, as a dēvadāna to the temple. It is stated that
the village was made habitable then.

l.ta:
koneriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ āḷuṭaiyār tevarkaṉmikaḷukku nammolai kuṭutta-
paṭiyāvatu vaṭapa-

vatāṉamākak
kuṭuttamaiyil ivvūṟkellai noyyaṟkaraikku vaṭakkum kaḷḷiṭṭaikku kiḻakkum
ceṅkuḷanālikkut teṟkum īṉkoṭṭuttāḻvukku meṟkum innāṉkellaik
kuṭpaṭṭa nilamum ittāl vanta iṟai

puravu ciṟṟāyam elavai yukavai maṉṟupāṭu taṇṭakuṟṟam cantatiyillāpporuḷum
cumai cuṅkam cārikai veṭṭi muṭṭāvā •• maṟṟumeppeṟpaṭṭatum

paṇṭāramāvutākuvum ippaṭi cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka nammolai kuṭuttom
ippaṭi cempiluñ cilaiyilum veṭṭikkuṭuttom [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 187.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete. It seems to record the gift of certain taxes
to provide for certain services to the god Avināśi Āḷuḍaiyār.

l.ta: śrīviracoḻatevaṉeṉ emakkuccellāniṉṟa yāṇṭu nālāvatu avināciyāṇṭār tā-

ṉattilum maṟṟum innāyanār tevatāṉamāy uḷḷa ūrkaḷilumirukkum civap-
pirāmaṇar tevaraṭiyār ••••• kku iṟuttu

iruntavaruntavanaṭai kāṉappeṟu avināci āḷuṭaiyāṟku tirumeṟpūccukku utaka-
pūrvamākak kuṭuttom [nintamā]eppeṟpaṭṭā
l.note: This is written in continuation of the end of another inscription.

inscription.head:
No. 188.
(A.R. No. 177 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

It records that the maṇḍapa was caused to be constructed by a certain
Vāṇādarāya Vaḍamalaiyappar.

inscription.head:
No. 189.
(A.R. No. 177-A of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A second pillar in the same place.

This inscription records the perpetual obeisance of a certain Vāṇādarāyaṉ
Vaḍamalaiyappar.

inscription.head:
No. 190.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar of the verandah round the same shrine.

This inscription records a gift of land made by a certain merchant named
Marudanmudali alias Vaṇikachakkaravartti of Muṭṭam alias Amarabhuyaṅ-
karanallūr in Peru-nāḍu to provide for food-offerings to the deity Avināśi
Āḷuḍaiyār Kūttāḍuvār.

l.ta: ṉaṭikku yiṭṭamutukku arici kuṟuṇikku viṭṭanilam kola •• ṟaiyil
manṟāṭṭu nilam kāṇik kellai nam viṭṭu nilattukku-
m maṇalivaṇika nilattukku vaṭakku vāykkālukku kiḻak-

inscription.head:
No. 191.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another pillar of the same verandah.

This inscription records that a certain Avināśi Śilaiśeṭṭi alias Kaṇḍiya-
dēvaṉ Vaṇḍutoṭṭi of a nāḍu (name lost) caused the making and erection of a
stone pillar to the Ishabha-maṇḍapa in the temple of Avināśiyāḻuḍaiyār. It is
stated that this work cost 1-1/2 achchu of Paḻañchalākaī.

inscription.head:
No. 192.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Bṛihatkaruṇāmbikā shrine in the same temple.

p.note: The year has been read as 22 in A.R.S.I.E., 1909.

inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged. It records an endowment of 45 paṇam, of
which 35 paṇam was for providing food offerings and 10 paṇam was for the
procession of the image of the deity (name lost), probably of the temple of
Avināśi Āḷuḍaiyār. The name of the donor is lost but it seems that he hailed
from Māḍakkuḷakkīḻ-Madurai (i.e., Madurai) in [Pāṇḍi]-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: ṅkaḷukkum ivarai eḻuntaruḷuvikka uṭaiyār ci paṇṭārattil
upayamākak kuṭutta paṇam pattum ākap paṇam nāṟpattaiñ-
tāka itu cantirātittavarai celvatāka itu panmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 193.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same shrine.

This inscription is also damaged. It records a gift of money made by
Perumāḷ Aḻagiyanāyaṉ alias Aḻagiya Pāṇḍiyadēvar, a merchant of Aṟaṉaiyūr
alias Śīvallapuram in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu in [••• •••] maṇḍalam, to
provide for food offerings to the deity Sundaraṉāyanār to whom a temple was
caused to be constructed on the bank of the tank at Pukkoḷiyūr, by the same
merchant. It is stated that the gift was found necessary because the sabhaiyār
and the ūrār of Kañjappaḷḷi alias Kulōttuṅgachōḻach-chaturvēdimaṅgalam
in Vī ••• ••••• chōḷa-vaḷanāḍu, were not able to pay something the details
of which are lost. It is further stated that the above-mentioned sabhaiyār and
ūrār had undertaken to carry out the object of the endowment.

l.ta: karaiyil eḻuntaruḷi •••• ṉa cuntaraṉāyanā[r]kku amutupaṭikkum pala-
ra •• ka ••• vi caṅkara •••• paṭṭaṉ ciṟṟampa-

inscription.head:
No. 194.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete. It records an order of Ādi-Chaṇḍēśvara of
Āvināśiyāḷuḍaiyār directing that on the date mentioned above the following
among the dēvaraḍiyārs of the temple, viz., Nāgapāśa-naṅgai, Śāntāḷ,
Eḻukarai-nāṭṭū-naṅgai, Nāchchi, Āḷuḍaiyanāchchi, Vaḷattāḷ and Irāguttarāya-
naṅgai, should render the service of tiruvantikkāppu.

l.ta:
nāyaṉār koyiṟ tevaraṭiyāril nākapācanaṅkaikkum cāntāḷukkum eḻukaraināṭṭu
naṅkaikkum

nāccikkum āḷuṭaiyanāccikkum vaḷattāḷukkum irākuttarāya naṅkaikkum nam
ōlaikuṭuttapaṭi-

tiyati nāḷ ivarkaḷukku ikko-
yiṟ ti[ruva]ntikkāppeṭuk-

inscription.head:
No. 195.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

This inscription is very much damaged and is incomplete. It appears
to record a gift of land as kuḍinīṅgā-dēvadāna and a gift of paddy to provide
for food offerings and to do some services probably to the deity Avināśiyāḷu-
ḍaiya-nāyanār. Mention is made of the premises of the temple of this deity
and of a street called Vikkiramachōḻan-tiruvīdi.

inscription.head:
No. 196.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

This inscription records a settlement concluded by the members of the
VeḷḷAḷa community and the members of the PUluva community belonging
to VaḍapariśAnakanāḍu Read Vaḍapariśāraka-nāḍu., with the members of the two communities belonging
to ŚēvUr alias Śembiyaṉ-kiḻAnaḍinallUr in the same VaḍapariśAnaka-nāḍu Read Vaḍapariśāraka-nāḍu., a
dēvadāna of the god Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār but with the proprietory right over
it resting with these people. The settlement related to the removal of two
VeḷḷAla and two PUluva families from this village, without, however, stating
the reason of this action. Several signatories, each hailing from a different
village, figure in this record.

l.ta: rai eṇpaṭṭakkuṭi irukkiṟa kuṭikaḷile veḷḷāṉ kuṭiyile
m āka nālukuṭi eṭṭāvatiṉ etirāmāṇṭu mutal nikki
rkaḷākavum ippaṭi[k*]ku cammatittu piṭipāṭu kuṭuttom vaṭa-
ḷā(ṟ)ṟkum pūluvar ūrāṟkum ippaṭikku ottanūril a[vi*]-
miṇṭan eḻuttu ippaṭikku ilattūril veḷḷāḻaṉ k[o]-
ḻannakkaṉ cella[ṉ*] e[ḻu*]ttu ippaṭikku kaiñcappaḷḷiyil veḷḷaḻa-
n mutali pottikai māṭṭāmaiyum karuvalūril veḷḷāḻa[paṭṭi]-
lūr veḷḷāḻaṉ cokkaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku erumai-
yaṉ kūvalūr veḷḷāḻa nakkaṉ ma[pa]ṉ kai[y]mmāṭṭāmal

inscription.head:
No. 197.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records an order of the deity Ādi-Chaṇḍēśura. It pertains
to the matter of colonising from the 8th year in the reign of the king of the
village of Śēvūr alias Śembiyan-kiḻāṇaḍinallūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu, a dēvadāna
of the deity Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār, but owned by the ūr and the urāḷis of the
village. It also records that in the next year (i.e., the 9th year in the reign
of the king) the village people themselves would enjoy the lands; but from
the year after that (i.e., from the 10th year), the villagers should pay paddy
and several other taxes (specified), besides 10 achchu of money annually.

l.ta: ttāṉ pātam tiṟampāmaṟ cenṉimel vaittu āticaṇṭecura āte-
lūr śrī virapāṇṭiyatevaṟki yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatu paṅku-
ṟai oṭṭac cuntavari ēla[vai] ukavai tirumun kāṭci u-
ciṟṟāyam taṟiyiṟai cekkiṟ[ai*] taṭṭārpāṭṭam yiḻam puṉcey
yokam cumai cuṅkañ cā •• kai maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum

inscription.head:
No. 198.
(A.R. No. 186 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a grant of the village of Taṉavāśi alias Vīrachōḻa-
nallūr in Kīṭpā-nāḍu, made by the king, on the occasion of his coronation, to
the goddess Peruṅkaruṇaichchelviyār of the Tirukkāmakōṭṭam in the temple
of the god Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār. The gift is stated to have been made to a certain
Mādēvāṇḍār. Also states that the taxes levied from the village should be
received by the servants of the goddess only and the king's officials like the
sāmantas and others should not enter the village. The record further states
that in regard to the colonising of the village, the above-mentioned Mādē-
vāṇḍār would decide on the families to be brought into the village, and
that the inhabitants of the village themselves should measure out oil, etc., in
the office of the goddess's shrine.

l.ta: m pūcaikkum mutalāka nām kuṭutta ūr kiṭpānāṭṭut taṉavā-
ciyāṉa viracoḻanallūr mātevāṇṭār kaiyyile nām utakam
puṉceyyāluḷḷa iṟai [puravu] ciṟṟāyam elavai yukavai maṉṟupāṭu
teṇṭa kuṟṟam cumai cuṅkam maṟṟum eppeṟpaṭṭiṉavum innācci-
yār tevakaṉmikaḷe koṇṭu varuvārkaḷākavum nam cāmantar kaṉmika-
ḷ pukkak kaṭavarkaḷallavākavum ivvūril paṭappai aṇainta paṭṭitta-
kkaḷ makkaḷ aṉupavittu varuvārkaḷākavum ivvūṟku uriya kuṭiyeṟu-
vatākavum uriyakuṭi eṟittilakaḷākil mātevāṇṭār tamakku veṇ-
vum ivvūril eṟiṉa kuṭikaḷukku iṟaikuṭiyāl kāṇaṅkalak kaṇa[p*]pum
tuḷaikālpponnum kā •• irunāḻi e[ṇ*]ṇalaiyumāka ivviṟai kuṭi-

tu ||§ ivai cetiyarāyaneḻuttu ||§ ivai muṉaiyataraiyaneḻuttu ||§ i-

inscription.head:
No. 199.
(A.R. No. 187 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is slightly damaged and is incomplete at the end. It
records a gift of 12 kalam of paddy from the 4th day of the month of Āṉi in
the year quoted above, to provide for food offerings to the deity Sundaranā-
yanār at Teṉpaḷḷi, by the ūr and the ūrāḷis of the village of Pāppārapūṇḍi
alias Vīrarājēndranallūr a dēvadāna of the deity Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār. It is
stated that these people would deliver the paddy to the śrī-bhaṇḍāra of the
temple of Avināśiyāḷuḍaiya-nāyaṉār, annually.

inscription.head:
No. 200.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This incomplete inscription records an order of the deity Ādi-Chaṇḍēś-
vara, granting the right called tachchāchāriyakkāṇi in the temple of Avināśi
Āḷuḍaiyanāyaṉār, to two artisans named Uḍaiyāṉ Nāyakapiḷḷai alias Aḻagi-
yaśēmāchāri of Tirumarudantuṟai and Uḍaiyān Uḍaiyāṉ alias Eḻukara-
ināṭṭu-āchāri, and enjoining upon them to attend to the ten kinds of repairs
in the temple in lieu of the right given to them.

l.ta:
karumamārāyka paṇṭe aṟañceytāṉ ceytāṉ aṟaṅkāttāṉ pātantiṟampāmaṟ
ceṉṉivaittu aruḷāḷa āticaṇṭe

uṭaiyāṉ uṭaiyāṉāṉa eḻukaraināṭṭu ācārikkum nam olai kuṭutta paricāvatu
tāṅkaḷ

taṅkaḷukku kuṭukkaiyil ikkoyilukku varum

tāṅkaḷe ceytu
ittāl varum aṭaṅkal peṟupeṟṟu

inscription.head:
No. 201.
(A.R. No. 189 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Vīra-Vallāladēva: Śaka 1204, Dundhubhi, Rishaba (Vaikāśi)-ba-5,
Thursday, Uttirāḍa = 1282 A.D., May 6. The Śaka year was mistake for 1244.

inscription.p:
It records a gift of the village of Teṉpaḷinattam alias Śitakaragaṇḍanallūr,
which was carved out of the dēvadāna land belonging to the deity Avināśi-
yāḷuḍaiyanāyaṉār of Tiruppukkoḷiyūr, by Mādappa-daṇṇāyaka, son of
the mahāpradhāna Immaḍi Irāhuttarāyaṉ Perumāḷ-daṇṇāyakaṉ of the
Maṭigōlaya-kula. The gift is stated to be to provide for food offerings and
other services during the Immaḍi-Irāhuttarāyaṉ-sandhi instituted
in the temple by the donor, for conducting the Vaikāśi-viśākham festival
and for doing repairs to the temple.

edition.l:
yāṇṭu āyirattiruṉūṟṟunālil tuntumi samvatsarattu ṛṣabhanāyaṟṟu abharakubha Read aparapakṣattu •-

inscription.head:
No. 202.
(A.R. No. 190 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 10 paṇam by a certain Padari of the
Veḷḷāḻa community, named Nalliśaiyāḷaṉ Parākkiramaṉ of Pālatoḻuvu in
Kuṟuppu-nāḍu for a sandhyā lamp given by the donor to the god Aviṉāśiyā-
ḷuḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

l.ta:
ttu āvaṇi mātam kuṟuppu nāṭṭil pāla
toḻuvil veḷḷāḻaṉ patarikaḷil nallicaiyāḷaṉ parākkiramaṉeṉ aviṉāciyā-
ḷuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku nāṉ vaitta cantiyā tipam oṉṟukku iṟṟai

ippaṇam pattukkum inta viḷakku oṉṟum
cantirātittavarai celvatāka ivai paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 203.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription seems to be incomplete. It records a gift of a tax called
kuḍikāṇam consisting of one kuṟuṇi and four nāḻis by the villagers of Veḷḷāṉūr
in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu to the goddess Peruṅkaruṇaichchelviyār of the Tiruk-
kāmakkōṭṭam in the temple of Āḷuḍaiyār Avināśiyāṇḍār.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅka coḻatevaṟku tiruveḻut-
tiṭṭuc cellāniṉṟa tirunalli

yāṇṭu patinoṉṟāvatukku etirāvatu vaṭaparicāranāṭṭu veḷḷāṉūrom āḷuṭaiyār
avināciyāṇṭār koyilil

tirukkāmakkoṭṭattu nācciyār peruṅkaruṇaiccelviyāṟku nāṅkaḷ amaiñcu
kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu orukuṭi kāṇaṅkuṟuṇi

nāṉāḻi kaṇṭa āṇṭuvaraitoṟum kuṭuttuvaruvomākavum itu caṉtira ātittavarai
āṇṭār mahātevāṇṭār ceyvi

inscription.head:
No. 204.
(A.R. No. 192 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records an endowment of 14 Paḻañjalākai achchu in the
temple of Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār, by the prince Nachchināṟkkiniyar alias
Kaṇḍiyadēvar, for oil for bathing the image of the god Āvināśiyāḷuḍaiyār
and of the goddess Peruṅkaruṇaichchelviyār of the Tirukkāmakkōṭṭam on
Saturdays and also for giving to the people suffering from diseases. It is stipu-
lated that the servants of deity, on receipt of the endowment, should measure
out every Saturday the specified quantity of oil at the shrine of the goddess.

l.ta: kāppu nāyanāṟkuc cani onṟukku eṇṇai uriyum
nācciyāṟku uriyum ālaṟkku vaḻaṅka eṇṇai iru
nāḻiyum ākaccanti onṟukkup parakecariyāl eṇṇai mun-

inscription.head:
No. 205.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.note: The date has not been given in A.R.S.I.E., 1909.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift, made by the ruler, of the village of Attipaḷḷi
in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu, to the deity Avināśiyāḷuḍaiyār.

l.ta: nāciyāḷuṭaiyar tevakanmikaḷukku nam ōlai kuṭutta paricāvatu vaṭa-
[r]kku ellaiyāvatu nāppupāḻikku meṟkum teṟkum pilapāḻikku vaṭa-
kkum meṟku paṉaiyoṭu kompukku kiḻakku vaṭakku karaiyum ināṉ-

cilaiyilum veṭṭi[k*]koḷ[vā]rāka [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 206.
(A.R. No. 194 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 10 paṇam by Piḷḷai alias Śiṟukkoḍukkaṉ, a
Veḷḷāḻaṉ Kuṇḍeli of Ādavūr in Kuṟuppu-nāḍu, for a sandhyā lamp given by the
donor to (the temple of) the deity Avināśiyāḷuḍaiya-nāyāṉar.

l.ta:
kuṟuppunāṭṭu ātavūr
veḷḷāḻaṉ kuṇṭelikaḷil piḷḷaiyāṉa ciṟukkoṭukkaṉeṉ uṭaiyār aviṉāci-
yāḷuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku nāṉ vaitta canti tipam oṉṟukku cipaṇṭārattil

ippaṇam pattukkum inta viḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittavarai
celuttuvatāka itu paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 207.
(A.R. No. 195 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This incomplete inscription seems to record a gift of one achchu to the
temple for burning a lamp there.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• pam oṉṟukku śrī paṇṭārattil oṭukkiṉa accu oṉṟu
inta accu oṉṟuṅ

inscription.head:
No. 208.
(A.R. No. 196 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This is an incomplete inscription. It records the gift, made by the king,
of the village of Vāḷaippāḍi in Nallūrkkā-nāḍu, to provide for daily food
offerings and other services to the deity, for doing repairs to the temple, for the
Avaṉivēndarāmaṉ-sandhi instituted in the name of the ruler and for the festival
in the temple of Avināśi Āḷuḍaiya-nāyaṉār in Śevvūr-nāḍu.

l.ta:
koneriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ cevvūrnāṭṭu
nāyaṉā[r*] aviṉāci āḷuṭaiya nāyaṉār koyilil tānattārkkut taṅkaḷ
nāyaṉārkku namperāl kaṭṭiṉa aviṉiventarāmaṉ canti-

kkum tirunāḷukkum amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷḷiṭṭu veṇṭum nittanimantaṅkaḷuk-
kum tiruppaṇikkum uṭalāka nallūrkkānāṭṭil vāḷaippāṭi nāṉku ellaikku
uṭpaṭṭa naṉ-

inscription.head:
No. 209.
(A.R. No. 197 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

This incomplete inscription records a gift of one achchu by a certain
Śattiyanāyakkaṉ, a Veḷḷāḻaṉ Kāri of Āraṅgūr in Kīḻkarai-Pūnduṟai-nāḍu for a
lamp given by the donor to the deity Aviṉāśiyāḷuḍaiyār.

l.ta:
tiyati nāḷ kīḻkarai pūntuṟaināṭṭil āraṅ-
kūril veḷḷāḻa-

ṉ kārikaḷil cattināyakkaṉeṉ uṭaiyār avināciyāḷuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku vaitta
cantiyātipam oṉṟukku śrī paṇṭārattil oṭukkiṉa accu oṉṟu itukoṇṭu
ca

inscription.head:
No. 210.
(A.R. No. 198 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Kalyāṇa-maṇḍapa in the same temple.

This inscription records a gift of 3 paṇam, made by a certain Oṉṉakkaḷa-
chcheṭṭiyār, son of Nandagaṇacheṭṭiyār, one of the merchants of Emmaṟkāl
in Teṉakkaḍambai-nāḍu for food-offerings daily during the mid-day worship
to the deity Avināśi-iliṅgaṉ in Dakshiṇa-Vārāṇasi. It is stated that the amount
of 3 paṇam was the monthly interest of 15 poṉ lent by the donor to
some persons including Tānikar Āvaḍaiya Nambiyār.

l.ta: kku mel piṅkaḷa varuṣam kāttikai mātu mu[la]-

inscription.head:
No. 211.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another slab set up near the same maṇḍapa.

p.b:
Vīra-Chikkarāya-uḍaiyār: Śaka 1430, Vibhava, Kārttigai 15 = 1508 A.D.,
November 13 (Monday).

inscription.p: This inscription is much damaged in its middle and end parts.

The ruler is stated to be the son of Vīra-Nañjarāya-uḍaiyār. The inscrip-
tion records a gift of 4 paṇam, being the monthly interest on the capital of 20
poṉ, made by a certain Ōduvanañjayan, son of Nañjaṇa-cheṭṭiyār, a merchant
of Emmaṟakāl in Tiṉaikkaṇambai-nāḍu (Terkaṇāmbai-nāḍu), to provide for food
offerings during a daily periodical worship instituted in the donor's name to
the deity Avināśi-liṅgam in Dakshiṇa-Vārāṇasi. The amount of 20 poṉ was
taken on loan by some persons who were enjoined to give the interest of 4 paṇam
monthly to carry out the object of the endowment eternally.

l.note: The rest of the inscription containing 17 lines is fully damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 212.
(A.R. No. 200 of 1909.)
AVANĀSHI, AVANASHI TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third slab set up in the same place.

p.b:
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Nañjarāya-uḍaiyār: Śaka 1421, Siddhārthi, Kārttigai
17 = 1499 A.D., November 16 (Saturday).

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of land to the god Aviṉāśi-iliṅgaṉ of Dakshiṇa-
Vārāṇasi, by a certain Oṉṉakkaḷach-cheṭṭiyār, son of Nandagaṇach-cheṭṭiyār,
a merchant of Emmaṟkāl in Tiṇaikkaṇāmbai-nāḍu (Terkaṇāmbi-nāḍu),
who is described as the Pradhāna of the king. The land is stated to
have been purchased from the temple lands in Avanāśi by paying 15 poṉ
to the temple treasury. It is stated that one thousand coconut trees
should be grown in the land and the income accruing thereform in due course
should be used to provide for certain services to the deity mentioned above.
It is further stated that the land was entrusted to some people including
Kōḻiyāṇḍār, Sūriyadēvaṉ and Masāṉ who were sons of a certain Āṇḍi of Śēvūr
in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu and that they and their successors should perpetually
continue to carry on the above-mentioned services to the god.

l.ta: m paṇṇi aruḷāniṉṟa kālattu nañcaṟāya uṭai-
yavar nilattil uṭaiyavar paṇṭārattukku kiṟaiyam patinañcu po-
ṉ kuṭuttu nām koṇṭa nilam cal[ā*]kai muṉṟu inta nilam mu-
ṉṟu cal[ā*]kaikkum āyiram teṉṉam paḻam poṭṭu toṭṭa-
ku tiru aviṉāciyil kaṇakku timmaiyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the gōpura of the same temple.

p.b:
Kṛishṇarāja-Vaḍeya of Mahisūru ruling at Śrīraṅgapaṭṭana. Ś. 1678,
Kaḷi 4857, Dhātṛi = 1756 A.D.

inscription.p:
This incomplete inscription records that Śaṅkarayya, son of Madanayya
and grandson of Mādayya who is the son of Vīrayya and an officer
(gurikāra) at Coimbatore and a kāryakarta of daḷavāyi Dēvarājayya, an agent
of the king in this part of the country, is stated to have carried out the renova-
tion of the temples at Aviṇaśi including the maṇḍapas, the gōpuras, the prākāra
walls, the paved flooring (nelakaṭṭu) and the tank for the floating festival (teppa-
koḷa). He is also stated to have set up a Pañchaliṅga called Śaṁkarēśvara in
the south-east corner of the prākāra of Aviṇaśīśvira temple here. Also it regis-
ters a grant of land made after purchase, to the above deity for the maintenance
of lamp and worship. The details of grant land are not given in the record.

edition.l:

inscription.head:
No. 214.
(A.R. No. 202 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Tāṉtōṉṟīśvara temple.

This incomplete inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp to the god
Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār by a certain person (name lost), a Veḷḷāḻaṉ Mēṉmaṇi
of Nambipērūr in [••• •••]-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 215.
(A.R. No. 203 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This is another incomplete inscription which records a gift of lamp to the
same deity mentioned No. 214 above, by a certain Kāmiṇḍaṉ a Veḷḷāḻaṉ
Kaḻañjipari of Nambipērūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 216.
(A.R. No. 204 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

This incomplete inscription records a gift of two sandhyā lamps and probably
money to provide oil for them, to the god Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār by a certain
Mukkaṇ Āṇḍāṉ, a Veḷḷāḷa of Nambipērūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu. A Śiva-
brāhmaṇa named Āṇḍāṉ Śella-bhaṭṭaṉ alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala-nambi of
Bhāradvajagotra and another (name lost) who had rights (of worship) in the
temple seem to have accepted the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 217.
(A.R. No. 205 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same temple, left of entrance.

This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It records a gift of a
sandhyā lamp to the god Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār by a certain Dēvaṉ Irājarājaṉ
a Veḷḷāḻaṉ Śōḻa of Nambipērūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas
figuring in No. 216 above, figure here also.

inscription.head:
No. 218.
(A.R. No. 206 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same temple.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp and a sum of 10 paṇam
to provide oil for it, to the deity Tāntōṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyār of Nambipērūr by
the wife of the Brāhmaṇa Pūman Sōman of Kāśuva (Kāśyapa)-gōtra belonging
to Paṭṭāli in Kāṅgaiya-nāḍu. The temple-right-owning Brāhmaṇas Āṇḍān
Śella-bhaṭṭan alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala-nambi of Bhāradvāja-gōtra and Āḻvān
Vañjagaṟkāyan alias Vañjanāyaka-bhaṭṭan received the gifts and agreed
to do the service.

l.ta: ivviruvom yivar pakkal upaiyamākak koṇṭa paṇam pattum cantiyā tipa[m*] o-
nṟum kuṭaṅkoṇṭu koyil pukuvar cantirātittavarai celuttuvo[r*] itu pan-

inscription.head:
No. 219.
(A.R. No. 207 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records a gift of a sandhyā
lamp and a sum of 10 paṇam for (providing oil for) it, to the same deity by a
certain Śiṅgakaramiṇḍaṉ Dēvaṉ (second name lost), a Veḷḷāḻaṉ Kaḻañjaya of
Nambipērūr in Vaḍapariśāra-nāḍu. The gift was received by the same
Śivabrāhmaṇas who figure in No. 218 above, etc.

inscription.head:
No. 220.
(A.R. No. 208 of 1909.)
NAMBIYŪR, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two broken pieces of a slab set up in Pudūr, a hamlet of Nambiyūr.

p.b:
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Nañjaṇṇa-uḍaiyār: Īsvara, Chittirai =
1517 A.D., April (?)

inscription.p:
This fragmentary inscription now contains only the name of the ruler and
the few details of the date given above.

l.note: The rest of this face is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 221.
(A.R. No. 209 of 1909.)
SATYAMAṄGALAM, GOBICHETTIPALAYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East and north walls of the temple of Āṇḍavarkōyil on the Dauḷagiri hill.

p.b:
Vīrapratāpa-Chikkadēvarāja, ruling at Maisūr: Ś. 1598, Naḷa, Chittirai 1 =
1676 A.D., March 29 (Wednesday).

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the ruler as well as his feudatory Daḷavāy
Nañjanāthayyaṉ, son of Kuḷalē Mallarājayyaṉ who is described as the ruler's
sarvva-kāryya-dhurandharar and Śivabhakt-āgrēsarar, ruling at Satyamaṅgalam.
It records that a temple for the god Kumārasvāmi was caused to be constructed
on the Dhavaḷagiri hill which was known as the Durvāsa-kshētra and was
situated near the confluence of the rivers Chintāmaṇi and Bhavānī and which
was situated on the northern bank of the Bhavāni in Satyamaṅgalam included
in Oḍuvaṅga-nāḍu, by Raṅganātha-cheṭṭi, the son of Appi-cheṭṭi and grandson
of Appaṉā-cheṭṭi of the Kāvērīrishi-gōtra and Śiṉṉā-cheṭṭi, the son of Tirivi-
cheṭṭi and grandson of Kuṟuppu-cheṭṭi of the Kabandhamaharishi-gōtra,
who were nagarattārs of the place called Viṇṇappaḷḷi. These and all other
nagarattārs are stated to have built the tank at Malaḍipputtūr at a cost of 300
poṉ. It is also stated that these nagarattārs purchased two śalāgais of nañjai
land from the palace and gifted one śalāgai to provide for worship to the deity
Kumārasvāmi and the other śalagai to the Mahādēvarmaṭha at Haradanahaḷḷi.

l.gr:
śvara rājamāttāṇḍa rājagambhīra vīra-
pratāpa śrīcikkadēvarājabhūpālar maisūr siṁhāsanatti

inscription.head:
No. 222.
(A.R. No. 210 of 1909.)
PAṬṬAṆAM, PALLADAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the village.

p.b:
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Vīra-Nañjaṇa-uḍaiyār: Piravava (Prabhava), Chittirai, 1
= 1570 A.D. ? March 28 (Sunday). The cyclic year falls in Śaka 1429.

inscription.p:
This inscription is damaged at the end. It records that a certain
Maḍukkāḍu in Vāyiṛaikkā-nāḍu was in ruins for a long time and that six
merchants (names specified) belonging to the group of Ādicheṭṭis of Śrīnātha-
paṭṭaṇam of the king, arranged to recolonise the place to make it into a
Nānādēśippaṭṭaṇam as well as an Añjiṉāṉ-pugal-iḍam. It also mentions
Irājarājapurachchāvaḍi, Eḻukarai-nāḍu, a temple and Śuvarmaḍi.

l.note: Lines 28-29 are in Kannaḍa but illegible.

inscription.head:
No. 223.
(A.R. No. 211 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Kailāsanātha temple.

This inscription records a grant of land made by a certain Śiṅgaṉśōḻaṉ
alias Anuttarap-Pallavaraiyan, a Tenkuḍuba, for daily food-offerings to the
goddess Tribhuvanasundara-nāchchiyār in Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

l.ta:
viracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu patine[ḻā]vatu karaivaḻināṭṭu kaṇṇāṭi

inscription.head:
No. 224.
(A.R. No. 212 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

p.note: The year has been doubtfully read as [2nd in A.R.S.I.E., 1909].

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp to an Īśvara temple
(name lost) and a sandhyā lamp to the temple of Ammaḍēśvara and two achchu
of money (to provide oil for the lamps) by a certain merchant named Kaṇḍan
Āṇḍammai of Kaṇṇāḍiputtūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu eṭṭāvatu karaivaḻināṭṭu kaṇṇāṭip
puttūr viyāpārik kaṇṭa[n] āṇṭammai pe ••••

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 213 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged and the date portion is lost. It seems to
record a gift of 7 achchu of [Śrī]yakki-Paḻañjalāgai with which to feed some
people (details lost), by a certain person. It also mentions a Śivabrāhmaṇa
who had the right of worship in the temple of the god Tiruv-Anantīśvaram-
uḍaiyār.

edition.l: kāṅkeyar pakkal nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa ciriyakki paḻañcalākai accu 7 ivvaccu 7 ••••

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 214 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the gift of two [achchu ?] of money for burning
a sandhyā lamp to the deity Tiruv-Anantīśvaramuḍaiyār, by a certain Vīra-
śōḻak-kulaśēkharapanmaṉ, a sēnāpati of the king. It is stated that the
amount was entrusted to Karikālaśōḻa-bhaṭṭar who had the right of worship
in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 215 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same temple.

This inscription is also damaged and the date portion is lost. It appears
to record a gift of money for burning two sandhyā lamp in the temple of Tiruv-
Anantīśvaramuḍaiyār in Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr, by a certain Ulaguḍaimukkōkki-
ḻāṉaḍi, for his own merit.

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 216 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged and incomplete and the date portion is lost.
It seems to record some gift made by a certain Vīrarājēndra-Aṇittirap-pallavarai-
yaṉ, to the image of Dakshiṇāmūrtti set up by some maṉṟāḍis and priests.

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 217 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

This inscription seems to have been damaged and some passages are
missing. It appears to record some gift to the god Vināyakappiḷḷaiyār in the
temple of Tiruv-Anantīśvaramuḍaiyār, by a certain person (name lost) who
was a sēnāpati of the king.

l.ta:
ccaramuṭaiyār koyilil viṉāyakap piḷḷaiyāṟku perumāḷ
ceṉātipatikaḷil [kuṭut •]

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 218 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the entrance into the same temple.

This inscription is damaged at the end. It records a gift of paddy made
by the residents of Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr, for offerings to the deities Tiru-Anantīś-
varamuḍaiyār, Sundarar and Nāyanār, and for three sandhyā lamps.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 219 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the entrance into the Tirunandikēśvara temple in the village.

This inscription records a gift of 3 achchu of money for hot food-offerings
to the god Tiru-Anantīśuramuḍaiyār in Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu,
by a certain Mūttavāḷ Kaikkōḷa Kalkōvan-Vīran. The money is stated to have
been entrusted to Karikālaśōḻa-bhaṭṭan, a Śivabrāhmaṇa having right in the
temple. It is stated that the investment of the money would yield two kalam
of paddy at every crop-season.

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the Kaliyuga-Varadarāja-Perumāḷ temple in the village.

p.b:
Trībhº Kōṉēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ: Vikramachōḷadēva (?) Year 3, 257th day =
1276 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records an order of the king confirming as iṟaiyili, a piece
of land which was situated at Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu and was
originally granted as iṟaiyili by Sundarapāṇḍya to the temple of the god
Śokkanārāyaṇapperumāḷ at Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr alias Vīrapāṇḍyachchutur-
vēdimaṅgalam in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu. The order is addressed to the Nambiyār
and the Śrī-Vaiṭṭinavar of the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 221 of 1909.)
KAṆṆĀḌIPUTTŪR, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the Śelva-Vināyaka temple in the village.

This fragmentary inscription seems to record a gift of 3 kāśu to provide
for food-offerings. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 222 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Kulaśēkharasvāmin temple.

This inscription records a gift of land by a certain Kāḷi Aṟaiyan Irunedik-
kiḻavan, a maṉṟāḍi of Śōḻaṉmādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, for offerings to the
god Kulaiśēkhara-Īśvaramuḍaiyār of the same village, at the service of
tiruttāṉakkūṭṭi. Mention is made of a piece of land among those forming
the boundaries of the gift-land stated to be the śrīdhana land of the donor's
elder sister (tamakkai).

l.ta:
ttaṉmattukku viṭṭa nilamāvatu meṭṭuppākkālil leṉ maṉṟāṭṭukkāṇi nocci-
yoṭu vitai

tūṇippatakkilit taṉmattukku viṭṭanilam [pata]kkukku nāṉkellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟ-

kellai peruṅkavarukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟke-

kellaikkuḷ paṭṭanilam patakku-

r[ā*]tittavaṟ celvatākak kalveṭṭik kuṭutteṉ iru
netikkiḻavaneṉ itupanmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 223 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.

This inscription is much damaged. It records a gift of land in Śōḻaṉ-
mādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu made by a certain individual named Śiṟai-
mīṭṭaperumāḷ to the same god figuring in No. 234 above, for the festival con-
ducted on the Tiruvādirai days in the month of Mārgaḻi and for conducting
the procession of the image on those days. Amongst the boundaries of the
gift-land is mentioned a land belonging to the deity Koṅguviḻaṅkā-Īśvaramu-
ḍaiyār of Kaḍiṟṟūr given to it by Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻadēvar.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• yāṉa ciṟaimiṭṭaperumāḷen innāyanāṟku tirumārkaḻit-
tiruvātiṟai ••• ••• eḻuntaruḷukaikkum-

••• •••• yam tiraviyam ••• ••••• mutupaṭi tūṇippatakkāka
amutu ceytaruḷi ūrile eḻuntaruḷavum nāṉku ••• ••• •••

• ṭik kuṭutta nirnilamāvatu ivvūr vayalil kāṇippoḻiyil eṉ maṉṟāṭṭuk-
kāṇi kuṭi iraṇṭiṉāl vitai tūṇippatakkum nāṉkellaiyāva-

tu kiḻpāṟkellai ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻatevar nilamāy kaṭiṟṟūrāḷuṭaiyār koṅku
viḻaṅkā ī

• kāṇi amutupaṭipuṟattukku meṟkum ••• ••••• yāṉa maṉ-
ṟāṭṭukkāṇikku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai ••• ••• ••••

kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ūrkkāṇi ni ••• ••• •••• ttukku te •
••• ••••• [i]nnāṉkellaikkuṭ •••••

•••• kuṭipāṭṭanellu oṉpati ••• ••• ••••• tūṇip
patakkum āka [ne]llu [pū] oṉṟu ••• ••• ••••

kku irupatto[ru*]kala nellu itti ••• ••• ••••• tuvā[r*]-
kaḷāka kalve[ṭṭi]k kuṭu ••• •••••

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 224 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the gift of the eastern portion of the patti (enclo-
sure ?) of the temple by Periyadēvaṉ, an ascetic of the Mēlaimaṭham (western
maṭha), of Śōḻamādēvinallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 225 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records a gift of 15 achchu
of money by Puyaṁgan Irāśā ••• ••• •••• (second name lost), a maṉṟāḍi,
and Śōḻan Araiyan alias Vīraśōḻavaṇigairāyan, both belonging to Śōḻamādēvi-
nallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, to provide for the Tiruvotta-sāmam (ardha-yāma)
service. A certain Daṉmapālan also figures as another donor (?).

l.ta:
viracoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu pat[toṉpatāvatu] karaivaḻināṭṭu ••
coḻamātevinallūr manṟāṭi puyaṅkan irācā • ta nāṉa i •••••

nenum coḻan araiyanāṉa viracoḻavaṇikai[rā]yanenum ivviruvom tiru-
vottacāmattukku oṭukkiṉa āṉai •••• kattiyanapa ••••

pālan oṭukkiṉa accu panniraṇṭum vaṇikaiyrāyaṉ oṭukkiṉa accu muṉṟum āka
accu patiṉaiñcuṅkoṇṭu accu oṉṟukku upaiyam pūvaḻi kalane tūṇippatakku
••••

ka ippaṇṭārattile oṭukkinom tiruvottacāmattukku tirup ••• pattukku
nirvātta poṉṉukku cilavāka ittaṉmattukku oṭukkineṉ taṉmapālaneṉ
paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺĀMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is also damaged. It records an gift of an achchu of money
by Kōnūri alias (second name lost), a Veḷḷāṭṭi, 2 achchu of money by
Purushamāṇikka-śilaiya-śe ••••• (?) another Veḷḷāṭṭi, of Śōḻamādēvi-
nallūr, to conduct the service called the vaḻinaḍaichchaṭṭi in the temple of the
god Kulaśēkhara-īśvaramuḍaiyār of the same village.

l.ta:
vatu coḻamātevi na[llūr]
āḷuṭaiyār kulacekara īcuvaramuṭaiyāṟku ivvūr veḷḷāṭṭi kon ūriyāṉa
pāṇṭi • •

kka ••• [mu]nṟum ivvūr veḷḷāṭṭi puruṣamāṇikkaccilaiyace •••••
yen oṭukkiṉa accu iraṇṭum āka accu aiñcukkum vaḻinaṭaicaṭṭi onṟum
canti[rātittavaṟ]

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 227 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same temple.

This inscription records the gift of land in Āloḍūr for the tiruvottaśāma
service to the goddess of the holy bedroom of the god Kulaiśēkharaīśvara-
muḍaiyār of Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, by the king. It is stated
that a request for a gift of land was submitted personally to the king by Araiśan
Paṟaiyan alias Poyyāttamiḻ-nambi, a maṉṟaḍi-pūjaka of the same village.
The service included food-offerings and a vāyvaḻinaḍai-śaṭṭi. Amongst the
boundaries Kaṇṇāḍipputtūr is mentioned.

l.ta:
viracoḻatevaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellāni[ṉ*]ṟa tirunalli āṇṭu
•• vatu karaivaḻināṭṭu coḻamātevinallūr āḷuṭaiyār kulaicekara īcuvara-
muṭaiyār tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai nācciyāṟkut tiru-

vottacāmappaṭṭi veṇṭumenṟu ivvūr manṟāṭi pūcakari laraican paṟaiyanāṉa
poyyāttamiḻnampi pakkale vantu namakkuc colli viṭuvitta niṟān nāiṭu
āloṭuril talaivayal maṉnaṟai nila-

ttukku kalane tūṇikkum ellaiyāvatu coḻamātevip peruvākkālukku meṟkkum
kaṇṇāṭipputtūr ellai kaṇṇāṟṟukku vaṭakkum aiṅkalappaṟṟil naṭuvuccey
nilattukku kiḻakkum koṇapoṭikkavarukku

teṟkum innāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa nellu vitai kalaṉetūṇiyum amutupaṭi vāy-
vaḻinaṭaicaṭṭi onṟuñ celvatākavum innilattāl vanta iṟai puravu cit-
tāyam elavai ukavai maṟṟum

eṟpeṟpaṭṭatuvuṅ koḷḷātomākac cantirāticcaṟ celvatāka na ••• māruṭai
poṟpeṭuttukkuṭukka nam olaiyil eḻuttuyiṭṭuk kuṭuttom itu paṉmāyecura
i

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 228 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged and incomplete and the date portion is lost.
It appears to record another gift of land to the same deity as figures in No. 239
above, by the king on the request of the same person figuring therein, whose
full name, however, was Araiśan Paṟaiyan alias Śūttira[rāyaṉ] Poyyāttamiḻan.

l.ta:
viracoḻat(o)vaṟkkut tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc cellā niṉṟa tiru
••••• vatu ka ••••• coḻamātevi ••• •••••

cciyār[n]eṟiyiruntamaiyil tiruppaḷḷiaṟainācciyāraiye ••• •••••
•• paṟaiyanāna cūttirarā ••••• ta • maḻana ••• •••

vantu colli pūṅkataṉ kuṭimaiyil viṭuvitta nilattukku ellaiyāvatu vi •
tā ••• ṟpukkāṇikku meṟkum puṅkāperuṅkavarukku vaṭakkum peruva •••
