inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 229 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged. It records a gift of land for the service of
tiruvottasāma to the god Kshēttira-pālappiḷḷaiyār in the temple of Kulaiśēkhara-
īśvaramuḍaiyār of Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, by the king on the
request of the same individual who figures in Nos. 239 and 240 above. Among
the lands forming the boundaries of the gift-land is mentioned one which belonged
to a certain Danmapālaṉ of Dēvadāṉakkuḍi.

l.ta:
vatu karaivaḻi
nāṭṭuc coḻamātevi nallūr ••• yār kulaicekara īcuvaramuṭaiyār koyi-
lil kṣettirapālappiḷḷaiyāṟ ••• tacāmappaṭikku i-

vvūr manṟāṭi pūcakarilaraicaṉ paṟaiyanāṉa cūttirarāyanāṉa po ••• tamiḻaṉ
nam pakkale vantu amu •••• yillaiyeṉ yeṟṟu uḷvāykkāl piṟa-
vāykkālāka viṭukiṟa kā •••• nilattukku el-

laiyāvatu tevatāṉakkuṭi taṉmapālaṉ kāṇikku meṟkum kārai •• ḻippoya-
lukku vaṭakkum meṟ ••• ••• yāṉa ṟe vāykkoṭṭattukkuk kiḻakkum
piṇam pokiyiṭa •••• teṟkum innāṉ-

kellaikkuṭpaṭṭa vitai aiññāḻi nelvitai uṭpaṭa viṭṭa namolaiku ••••
intavitai aiṅkuṟuṇikku miṟai puravu cittāyam me •••• vai eppeṟpaṭṭatu-

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 230 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records a list of lands belong-
ing to the god Kulaśēkhara-īśvaramuḍaiyār of Śōḻamādēvinallūr. It includes a
land where 6 kalam of seeds could be sown, for food offering, a land, where I kalam
of seed could be sown, for nimantappuṟam, a land where padakku of seed could
be sown for a service (name lost), a land where 2 kalam of seed could be sown,
for tirununtā-viḷakkuppuṟam, a land where 3 kalam of seed could be sown for
food offerings to the deity and another land where[•••••]ṇippadakku of
seed could be sown for the poṉvilai.

l.ta:
coḻamātevinallūr āḷuṭaiyār kulacekara īvacuramuṭaiyāṟku
ivvūril amutupaṭipuṟamāy puravi niṅku vitai aṟukalamum iṉṉāyaṉār uvacca-
puṟa vitai aṟukalamum nimanta[p]puṟam vitai kalamum ••• ••• puṟam vitai
patakkum tirununtāviḷakkupuṟam vitai irukalamum ittevarkku ••• •••
kaṟkkum nāya[n*]āṟkku amutupaṭippuṟam vitai mukkalamum poṉvilait te ••
••••• ṇippatakkum paṉmāye

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 231 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
It records a gift of land. See Text in the Appendix.

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 232 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records an order of the king addressed to the servants of
the deity Kulaśēkhara-īśvaramuḍaiyār of Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-ṉāḍu.
It states that a certain Kāḍavarāyaṉ, a maṉṟāḍu of the village, became a
traitor to the king and that his maṉṟāḍu land was forefeited to the king, and
that this land was sold to the temple servants. It mentions a gift of land for
rearing a flower-garden for the use of the temple.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ koṉerimelkoṇṭāṉ karaivaḻiṉāṭṭu
coḻamāteviṉallūr ā(ḷ)ḷuṭaiyār kulace

kaṉmika(ḷ)ḷukku ṉammolai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu i[v*]vūr ma[ṉ*]ṟāṭṭuka(ḷ)ḷil
kāṭavarāyaṉ namakku turokiyarāy ivaṉ maṉṟāṭṭu nammutāṉamaiyil itteva
kaṉmikaḷukku viṟṟuk kuṭut-

ta maṉṟāṭṭu nilam araikku tam carakkukku vaiytta accu muppatum naṅkaṉmikaḷ
peṟum caṉ[ti]vikkirakiraka(r)peṟum cāmaṉtarpeṟum olaikaraṇamum olaicciṉ

vaccu muppatiṉāluṅkoṇṭu immaṉṟāṭṭu nilam araiyiṉāl vanta vitai kalaṉe muk-
kuṟuṇiyum iṉṉā(ṉa)yanāṟku [ti]ruṉaṉtavāṉamākaviṭṭa toṭṭa vitai patakkum
āka vitai kalaṉe-

•• ṟuṇikkum nāṉkellaiyāvatu taṭiyiraṇṭil vitai irutūṇippatakkukku
ellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai maṇṭakappuṟattuk kuṭakāṭamāṭitevar nilattukku
meṟkkum teṉpāṟkel-

lai tiruviṭaiyāṭṭattukkum cāmaṉtappaṟṟukku vaṭakku melpāṟkellai viraṉārā-
ṇappallavar maṇṭalattukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vayicciyakkāṇikkut teṟkum
āka iṉṉāṉkel-

laikkuṭpaṭṭa vitai irutūṇippatakkum taṭiyiraṇṭi(la)ṉāl vitai aiyṅkuṟuṇikku
ṉāṉkellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai kāṭamparam vāykkālukku meṟkum teṉpāṟ
kellai kumaraṅkayi

ramuṭaiyār nilattukku
kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai vaiyiccakkāṇikkut teṟkum āka iṉṉānkellaikkuṭ
paṭṭa vitai ai(ñ)ṅkuṟuṇiyum melliṭṭalil karuṅ-

koṭṭaiyaṭutta toṭṭa vitai patakkum āka iṉṉāṉkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa vitai kalane
aiyṅkuṟuṇi[yi*]l ••• ••• yilil ṉāyakaṟku amutupaṭikku yiyāvutāka iṉ
nilattukku iṟai puravu ciṟ[ṟā*]yam elavai ukavai naṅkaṉmi-

kaḷpeṟum cāmantappeṟu nam olaikkaraṇamum maṟṟum meppeṟpaṭṭatuvum iṟukkak
kaṭavatallavāka ••• ••• •••• veṭṭikkoḷvārkaḷāka namolai kuṭut-
tom ivai ellāvalla coḻamuventa veḷ[ḷā]n eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 233 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged and incomplete. It seems to record a gift of
land, and mentions Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Kaṛaivaḻi-nāḍu.

l.ta:
etir karaivaḻināṭṭu[c] coḻa-
m[ātevinallūr] ••• ••• •••• melpāṟkellai perumatiḷukku
kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai iṭṭikkut teṟkum •••

toṭṭattukku ••••• melpāṟkellai ••• kku kiḻakkum ••• pāṟ-
kellai muṭiko nāṭṭu ••• •••• nāṉkellaikkuḷpaṭṭa toṭṭamum
teṟkil vācalil toṭṭattu ••• ••••^1^

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a grove by Perumāḷ alias Perumāḷ-
tōḻan to the Mēlaittirumaṭham (the holy monastery on the western side) of
the temple of Kulaśēkhara-Īśvaramuḍaiyār in the village (name omitted).
It is stated that the gift was intended for a lamp and for providing vegetable
dishes in the said maṭham.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī virarācentiratevaṟku tiruveḻuttiṭṭuc
cellāṉinṟa tirunalliyāṇṭu irupattoṉpatāvatu perumāḷḷāṉa perumāḷ
toḻane-

ramuṭaiyār koilukku melaittirumaṭat-
tukku ūṟpuṟampākaviṭṭa toṭṭattu teṟkil vācalil toṭṭattu •••••
pavilaikoṇṭuṭaiya toṭṭat-

tukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai ••• mipatta toṭṭattukkum kāttāṇṭi
toṭṭattukkum vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai iṭṭalukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟke ••
•• ṭṭatukku ṉāṉkellaiyāvatu kiḻpāṟkellai

vaṇikiḻāṉtār nila[t*]tukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai peruvāykkālukku
vaṭakkum melpārkellai ayyaṉār koyi[ṟ]kum muṭikoṇṭā toṭ[ṭat*]tuk
kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkel ••• •••• immaṭattukku tiruviḷakkum kaṟiamu-
tukkum ceṣamāka-

viṭṭu kalveṭṭi kuṭutten perumāḷ toḻaneṉ itu caṉtirātittavaṟ celva-
tāka śrīmāke

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 235 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.note: The second 1st year has not been given in the A.R.S.I.E., 1909.

inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It states that a certain Veḷḷāṭṭi named
Ūrankōvi alias Kaḷabhamānachchay donated 5 Paḻañjalāgai achchu to provide
for the services on Śivarāttiri days to the god Kulaiśēkhara-īśvaramuḍaiyār
of Śōḻamādēvinallūr. This money was utilised for doing repairs to the maṇḍapa,
in the temple. In lieu thereof the king made a gift of paddy at the rate of 6
kalam per year.

l.ta: ḻamātevinallūr āḷuṭaiyār kulaicekara īcuramuṭaiyāṟku civarāttirait tanma(m)-

ttukku meṟpaṭi ā • [ve]ḷḷāṭṭi periyakaṉal ūrankoviyāna kaḷapamāṉaccayen
oṭukkina paḻañcalākai a-

ccu nālun tirumaṇṭapat tiruppaṇikku iṭṭamaiyāl innāyanāṟkut tevatānattil
veṭṭivicca [nila] upaiyam

aḷakkunellile ā[ṭṭai]kku aṟukala nel upaiyattukkuc celuttivaruvatākavum
ittanma cantirātittava[rai]yum

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 236 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same temple, right of entrance.

This inscription records a gift of a garden as maḍappuṟam made by the
residents of Śōḻamādēvinallūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, to the Kīḻaimaṭham (the
monastery on the eastern side) for burning lamps in the maṭham.

l.ta:
rom eṅkaḷḷūr kiḻaimaṭattil mutaliyār •• lla ñānām pata(ta)kku nāṅkaḷ
i[m*]maṭattukku maṭappuṟamā-

ka tiruviḷakkiṭaviṭṭa toṭṭattukku nāṉkellaiyāvatu kiḻpākkellai vaṭakku
nokkipoṉa kar

kku meṟkum teṉpākkellai nāṅkaḷ ellai nāṭṭiṉakallukku vaṭakkum mel-
pākkellai iṭṭalu-

kku kiḻakkum vaṭapākkellai nāṅkaḷ ellai nāṭṭiṉakallukku teṟkum iṉnāṉ
kellaikuḷuḷ-

ḷiṭṭa toṭṭam maṭa[p*]puṟamāka viṭṭukkuṭuttom coḻamātevinallūrvarom
imaṭappuṟam caṉtirā-
tittavaṟ celvatāka kalveṭṭikkuṭuttom coḻamātevinallūr ūrom ippaṭikku

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 237 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar within the same temple.

This inscription records that the pillar, on which the epigraph is inscribed,
was the gift of a certain Mōḍan Tirunaṭṭapperumāḷ, a Śāliya of the village of
Śōḻamādēvi.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 238 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another pillar in the same place.

This inscription records that the pillar was the gift by a Veḷḷāṭṭi (name not
clear) of Śōḻamādēvinallūr, to the temple of Āḷu[ḍaiya]-ṉāyanār.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Third pillar in the same place.

This inscription records that this pillar was the gift of a certain Śivirikūttan,
a Kaikkōḷar of Śōḻamādēvinallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the eastern entrance into the same temple.

This inscription records the gift of two door-posts and a couple of steps
to the maṇḍapa (of the temple) by a certain woman (Veḷḷāṭṭi) named Adiyaśōḻan
Araiśan alias Maṇikaiyarāyan, a Tuḻāiyar, of Śōḻamādēvinallūr, for her own
merit.

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 241 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the southern entrance into the same temple.

This inscription records a gift of two door-posts and two steps to the
maṇḍapa (of the temple) caused to be made and erected, by two women of
Śōḻamādēvinallūr, one named Paṟaiyan Āḻuḍaināchchi, a Pūsakar and her
younger mother.

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 242 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the entrance into the Tambirāṭṭiyammaṉ temple in the village.

This inscription records a gift of two Śrīyakki-Paḻañjalākai-achchu, for
conducting the Śivarāttiri festival to the god Irājarāja-Īsvaramuḍaiyār of
Kāḍappāḍi alias Vīraśōḻapuram, by a certaiṟn Kuṟūṉjēriyāṇḍār, alias Śirut-
toṇḍan, an ascetic (tapasvin) of the same place. The money was entrusted
to the Śivabrāhmaṇa Dēvaṉ Śivadavanapperumāḷ alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-
bhaṭṭaṉ who had the right (of worship) in the temple and who had agreed
to see the festival conducted perpetually in the temple.

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1909.)
ŚŌḺAMĀDĒVI, UDUMALAIPET TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone built into the wall of the same temple.

The beginning of this inscription is built in. It records a gift of two Iyakki-
Paḻañjalāgai for burning a sandhyā lamp in the temple of the god •••••
tavanamuḍaiyār of Kaṇṇāḍipudūr in Karaivaḻi-nāḍu, by a certain [•••••]
alias Koṅganāḍu-kiḻavan of the same place. The money is stated to have
been entrusted to the Śivabrāhmaṇa Karikālaśōḻa-bhaṭṭan who had the right
(of worship) in the temple.

l.ta:
vatu karaivaḻināṭṭuk kaṇṇāṭi
putūr manṟāṭi kā ••••• nāna koṅkanāṭu kiḻavannen ivvūr āḷuṭaiya

pam erippitāka i[k*]koyil kāṇi
yuṭaiya civa

ṇan karikālacoḻa ••• ṭanvacam oṭukkina iyakki paḻañ
calākai iraṇṭum ko-

•••• ppānākavum • evan oruvaṉ kuṭaṅkoṇṭu koyil pukkān ericcuvaruvān
ākavum ittanma

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 244 of 1909.)
DĒSŪR, WANDIWASH TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the Karivaḍa-īśvara temple.

p.b:
No king: Ś 1450, Sarvadhāri, Chittirai 29, śu. 6, Friday,
Puṇarpūśam, Kaṟkaṭaka-lagna = 1528 A.D., April 24.

inscription.p:
This inscription is in verse. It records that a temple to the god Śeṅgai-
Vēlāyudha (i.e., Subramaṇya), with the help of the mercy of Īśurat-
Timmaiyaṉ, was caused to be constructed at Dēsūr by a certain Uṇṇā-
mulaiyaṉ described as one who patronised the poet of Karidurga, the ruler of
Kampai, Kaṉṉaṉ, Poṉṉaināḍaṉ and one who favoured a certain Pāriyāḻvan
who (suddenly) appeared like a cloud.

l.ta:
m āyiramu[m*] nāṉūṟṟaṉpattiṉ meṟce[l*]lāniṉṟatornaṇ
caṟuvatāri cittiraiyilirupattoṉpatāntiyati

nalcukkiṟavāramuṅkoṇṭa [teḷpā]r puṇarpūcattu ṉāḷ paṇpāṉa kaṟ-
kaṭakala

tirumaruvumicurattimmaiyaṉ kiṟupaikoṇṭu cirmaḻavar kulam viḷaṅkat tecūrile
ceṅkai velāyutaṟkoru tirukkoyil kaṭṭuvittāṉ

kariturka nāvalaṟkaruḷ kampaiyātipaṉ kaṉṉaṉ miku poṉṉaināṭaṉ kārpolavanta
pāriyāḻvāṉukantatoru karuṇai uṇṇāmulaiyaṉe

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 245 of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar in the Śelliyammaṉ temple.

This inscription is damaged and the date does not seem to be genuine. It
records a gift of a nandā lamp to the image of the god Aḻagiyama[ṇa]vāḷa set
up in the temple of the god Mahāvishṇu Kalikēsariviṇṇagar-āḷvār of the
village of Vikkiramachōḻa-charuppēdimaṅgalam, by a certain Brāhmaṇa
woman, wife of Prayāgai-Sūryyadēvan, a member of the executive council
(Āḷuṅgaṇam) of the place. The record also makes mention of the sabhai of the
village, who had undertaken to burn the nandā lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 246 of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar in the Vēṇugōpālasvāmin temple.

p.b: No king: Ś. 892 (probably mistake for 982 ?) = 1060 A.D. (?)

inscription.p:
This inscription, the date of which does not seem to be genuine, records
a gift of money for burning daily a sandhyā lamp in the maṇḍaga (maṇḍapa)
in the temple of Kalikēsariviṇṇagar-dēvar by a certain bhaṭṭaṉ (name not clear)
belonging to Tirukkaḍavūr. The money was received by the sabhai (assembly)
of Kaḷamūr, who had undertaken to burn the lamp accordingly. At the end of
the record there is a passage which states that the same sabhai had undertaken
to burn a lamp during day-time in the sanctum sanctorum (of the same temple ?).

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 247 of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A fragment built into the wall of the Kāśiviśvanāthasvāmin temple.

This fragmentary inscription forms part of a long record of the king a part
of whose praśasti is preserved here.

It refers to an image of the god Karumāṇikka ••• ••• in standing posture,
in the Vishṇu temple called [••••] ṇagar-Āḻvār temple. Mentions a certain
Śirunaṁgaichchāṉi, a Brāhmaṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 247-A of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another fragment built into the same wall.

This inscription is also fragmentary. It, however, appears to record a
gift of (1/2) a nandā lamp and some money to provide for this, by a donor (name
not preserved), in the temple of Śirikaiyyillāyam (Śrīkailāyam) Amudīśvara-
muḍaiya ••• ••• at Kaḷamūr alias Vikkiramachōḻachcharuppēdimaṅ-
galam in Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu. The gift is stated to have been accepted by the
sabhai of the place who had also agreed to carry out the object of the gift.

l.ta:
raiyum āvapaṭaiccaṉaiyaṉ kaṟpulaṅkuḻi eḻupattunālum kuravacāri aṅkāṭi u •
••••

inscription.head:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 247-B of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A third fragment built into the same wall.

This piece of the inscription is extremely fragmentary in nature and contains
only a part of the passage referring to the boundaries of a certain land.

l.ta: kavum ivvūr yāḷuṅkaṇattāruṭ kuṇṭur naṉti aiyakkirama ••• •••
ḻikkut teṟkum iṉnāṟpālellaikku naṭuvu kānilamum naṉ ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 262.
(A.R. No. 247-C of 1909.)
KAḶAMBŪR, ARNI TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A fourth fragment built into the same wall.

This piece of an inscription is too fragmentary, and the passage preserved
here contains only the last part of the record. It refers to certain privileges
like the viḷakkeṇṇai-bhōgam, tiruvamudu-bhōgam, mañjaḷ-iñji, etc.

l.ta: kumpilāvum mañcaḷḷiñci maṟṟum ivarkaḷ tāṅkaḷ veṇṭu ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 263.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the central shrine in the Brahmēśvara temple.

p.b:
Vīra-Vijayabhūpatirāya, son of Vīra-Dēvarāya (I): Ś. 1331, Virōdhi,
Vṛiśchika (Aippaśi)-śu. 10., Uttiraṭṭādi = 1409 A.D., October 18 (Friday). The
nakshatra Uttiraṭṭādi comes on the next day.

inscription.p:
This inscription is built in the middle. It records a gift, made by the king,
of one half of the revenue from some puñjai land, garden, and ponds and one
half of all items of revenue except the tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam, from the four villages
namely Pāchchal, Paṉaikkaṟai, Kuṟumbaṟai and Virundāṇḍāṉputtūr to the god
Brahmīśvaramuḍaiyār of Tīruppeṟṟūr alias Śrī-Mādhava-chchaturvēdimaṅ-
galam in Eyiyil-nāḍu in Nikarilichōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttiraṭṭātināḷ nikarilicoḻamaṇṭalattu eyiyil
nāṭṭu tiruppeṟṟūr āṉa

tirāya ••• ••••• ḷḷatevatāṉamum kollaikāṭum āṇṭār
kuṭṭai kūttāṭuntevar kuṭ(ṭai)taḷittoppu eḻuventaṉ kuṭṭaiyil oṉṟu-
pātiyum pāccal paṉaikkaṟai kuṟumpaṟai viruntāṇṭāṉ puttūr āka innā-
lūrilum tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam nīṅkalāka tevatāṉam oṉṟupātiyum ittāluḷḷa
naṉcey puṉcey nāṟpāṟkellaikku uṇṭāṉa

yam e • tiṟai maṉaitiṟai
kāṇikkai kaṭṭāyam caṭi

maṭakkuvari nattavari ••• ••• ••• āyam yiṭai kammātam maṟṟum epper-
paṭṭa

upātikaḷum vipūtikāṇikkai uḷḷūr ūrviṉiyokamum uṭpaṭa
iṉṉāyaṉārkkup pūcai tiruppaṇikkum tirunāḷukkum terkkum
aikkaraiyiṟ kurāṟpacukkoṉṟāṉ pāvattiṟ paṭuvāṉ itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 264.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
[Hoysaḷa] Vīra-Vallāḷadēva III: Ś [12]56, Bhava, Mīṉa-ba. 12, Wednesday,
Sadayam = 1334 A.D., April 1. The weekday was Friday.

inscription.p:
This inscription is very fragmentary and refers to the king as Śārv-
vabhauma. Only the passage containing the particulars of date is preserved.

inscription.head:
No. 265.
(A.R. No. 250 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
[Hoyasaḷa] Viśvanāthadēva: Year 3, Siṅga (Āvaṇi)-ba. 11,
Saturday, Punarpūśam = 1299 A.D., August 22.

inscription.p:
This inscription is built in the middle. It records that from the year
quoted the dēvadāṉa villages belonging to the temple of Brahmīśvaramuḍaiya-
nāyiṉār were made sarvamānya (rent-free).

l.ta:
vatāṉa ūrkaḷu ••• ••••• muṭpaṭṭa tiruṉāmat-
tukkāṇi muṉṟāvatu mutal

viṭṭom ittaṉmattukku ilaṅkaṇam ••••• ṉ piramakattik[o]ḷḷak-
kaṭavaṉ §

inscription.head:
No. 266.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
North wall of the central shrine in the Varadarāja-perumāḷ
temple.

p.b:
Vīrapratāpa-Dēvarāya-mahārāya I: Ś. 131[8] for 1338, Durmukhi,
Pushya-ba. 13, Friday = 1417 A.D., January 15.

inscription.p:
This inscription records an order of the king exempting the maṉṟāḍis of
a number of nāḍus like Tagaḍa-nāḍu, Eyyil-nāḍu, and Puṟamalai-
nāḍu and several paṟṟus like Kaliṅgaṟai-paṟṟu, Vārūrp-paṟṟu, Mukkaṉūrp-
paṟṟu, Ārppūrp-paṟṟu, Paiyūrp-paṟṟu, Kuḍivūrp-paṟṟu and Tenśīrmaṅgalap-
paṟṟu in Muḷvāyrājya from the payment of the tax called puṟakūli, for
grazing their sheep, goats and cattle in the forests of the above-mentioned
places, because, the māṉṟāḍis had been paying a certain tax called taḷaiviṟai
to the king.

l.ta: ttu nāḷ śrīmatu[kāvaṉṉatāta] uṭaiyar muḷvāya i-

rācciyattukkuc cellum takaṭanāṭu eyyilnāṭu kaliṅkaṟaippaṟṟu vārurppaṟṟu
mukkaṉūrppaṟṟup puṟamalaināṭu ārppūrppaṟṟu paiyūrppaṟṟu tā ••• ppaṟṟu patu
•••• koṅkāṭa • ṭṭai kuṭivūrppaṟṟum tencirmaṅkalappaṟṟu cantirapa ••
ppaṟṟu uḷḷiṭṭa ••• pala maṉṟāṭikaḷukkuk kuṭutta [olai] nam tāṅkaḷ paṭaitta
āṭu māṭu

intakkāṭukaḷ • meyntatukku araimaṉaikkut taḷaiviṟai iṟukkayiṉāle tāṅkaḷ
iṉṉāṭukaḷil āṭumāṭukaḷ meyntatukku [puṟ] kūlikku [paṉṉi] • ṉṟu taṅkaḷaik
koḷḷa[ppaṭai]alla inta nāṭṭilavarkaḷum taṅkaḷaippuṟkūli tarakkaṭavatu eṉṟu
kuṭutta

nattu iyātu oruvarahitam paṇṇiṉavaṉ keṅ-
kaikkaraiyil kurāl pacuvaik koṉṟāṉ pāpattile pukakkaṭavaṉākavum
l.de: aṁ na dā ta na ba ra ha
l.note:
This expression means that the writing is of Annadātā. It is engraved in Kannaḍa
characters.

inscription.head:
No. 267.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Four detached stones built into the platform round the flag-staff in front
of the same temple.

This inscription is very much damaged. It contains part of the praśasti
of the king. It appears to record the remission of tax on some land belonging
to a certain Rājarājaśanman by the sabhai (assembly) of Śrī-Vīranārāyaṇa-
chchaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēya in Rājēndraśiṅga-vaḷaṅāḍu.

l.ta:
•••• ṅkuṭamalaināṭuṅ kollamuṅ kaliṅkamu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamum
iraṭṭapāṭi ēḻarai ilakkamu tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟi taṇṭāṟkoṇṭa taṉṉeḻil

vaḷaruḻiyuḷellā yāṇṭuntoḻutaka viḷaṅkum yāṇṭe ceḻiñrait tecukoḷ
śrīkovi
ṉ kuḷa ••• ṇṭu ••• •••• l vilaikkuk koṇṭa

inscription.head:
No. 268.
(A.R. No. 253 of 1909.)
TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar set up in front of the same temple.

This inscription is damaged much. It refers to a certain Mahāpradhāna
Śiṅgaya-daṇṇāyaka and to another Daṇṇāyaka (name not clear) alias Vala-
dēvar. It records the gift probably by the above-mentioned Daṇṇāyaka of a
nandā lamp to be burnt from the month of Kārttigai in the above-mentioned
cyclic year in the temple of Āṉaikāttaperumāḷ in Śrī-Mādhavachchaturvēdi
maṅgalam.

inscription.head:
No. 269.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1909.)
PAṬṬUKAṆĀMPAṬṬI, KARUR TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock near the ruined Śiva temple.

This inscription begins with the praśasti of the king. It records a gift
of land as iṟaiyili for offerings at the service called tiruchcheṉṉaḍai to the god
Mahādēva at Nelvāy by Naṉṉikāmuṇḍaṉ, son of Erumaiya-nāḻ-Kāmuṇḍaṉ
alias Vīraśōḻakāmuṇḍaṉ of Ariyūr in Puṟamalai-nāḍu, while Pañchavaṉ
Brahmādarāyar, a peruntaram of the king, was ruling from Tagaḍūr-nāḍu
which was granted to him for maintenance (by the king). It also makes
mention of the villages of Pāppāṉpāḍi, Kaṉṉāḍu, and Puttūr among the
boundaries of the gift-land.

At the end of the inscription, a grant in this year of a nandā lamp to the
god Mahādēvar (of Nelvāy) made by the people of Puṟamalai-nāḍu is also
recorded.

l.ta:
tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyun taṉakkeyurimai pūṇṭamai maṉak-
koḷak kāntaḷūrccālai kala-

maṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu nuḷampapāṭiyun taṭikaivaḻiyuṅ kuṭamalai-
nāṭuṅkollamuṅ kaliṅ-

kamu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara īḻamaṇṭalamu miraṭṭapāṭi yeḻaraiyilakkamun tiṇṭiṟal
veṉṟit taṇṭāṟ-

koṇṭa taṉṉeḻil vaḷaruḻiyuḷellā yāṇṭiluṅ toḻutakai viḷaṅkum yāṇ-
ṭeceḻiñaraittecukoḷ śrīko-

jīvitamāḷāniṟka puṟamalaināṭṭu ariyūruṭaiya erumaiyaṉāḻkāmuṇṭāṉāṉa viracoḻa-
kāmuṇṭaṉ makaṉ naṉṉi-

ṟkellai pāppāṉpāṭi meṟpoṉa vaḻiyiṟ kaṉṉāṭṭukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai
puttūrellaikkut-

ppāṉ śrīpātam aṭiyeṉāyattalaimelaṉa ||§ ivvāṇṭu puṟamalai
nāṭṭu nāṭṭomivvū-

viḷakkoṉṟiṉukku iṉṉāṭṭu ciṟṟūr perureṉ-
ṉāteyūrā • tūmameṇṇai

titteval [ma]ṭuvatākacceytu kuṭutto mitaṉṟeṉpā ṉeḻāṉarakattukkuk
kiḻāṉarakam pukuvāṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 270.
(A.R. No. 255 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Perumāḷ-kōyil tank.

p.b:
Vīrapratāpa Śrīraṅgadēva Mahārāya: Śaka. 1501, Bahudhānya, Makara(Tai),
Mahōdaya = 1576 A.D., January 26. The week-day was Monday.

inscription.p:
The inscription is damaged at the end. It states that the king was ruling
from Vidyānagara, and records grant of the village Kuṉṟattūr on the holy
occasion of Mahōdaya, to the god Talaśayanapperumāḷ of Tirukkaḍalmallai
(i.e., modern Māmallapuram), for worship, offerings, festival and for rearing
gardens, and for all other services, including the Sahasranām-ārchana. The
gift-village is stated to be situated in Peruṁbūkka-śirmai in Tirukkaḻukkuṉ-
ṟappaṟṟu, in Mugandūr-nāḍu in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam in Paḍaivīḍu-rājya in Gangai-
koṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍala. The gift was made for the merit of Vēṇkaṭapatidēva-
mahārāja, the brother of the king, by the former's agent Gōppa[ṇa] Daḻavāy-
Tirumalai-nāyakkar.

inscription.head:
No. 271.
(A.R. No. 256 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the Nādānandēśvara temple at Maṇamai, a hamlet of the
same village.

This inscription is damaged and its end portion is not well-preserved. It
describes the king as ‘who was pleased to take Madurai and the crowned head
of the Pāṇḍya’ It records a gift of 3 cows for burning a nandā lamp in the
temple of Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiyār at Maṇamai alias Jananāthanallūr in
Āmūr-nāḍu in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by a certain
Tirunaṭṭapperumāḻ a pākkiḻāṉ of Vaḷavaśikhāmaṇinallūr.

inscription.head:
No. 272.
(A.R. No. 257 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the floor at the entrance into the Īśvara temple in the same
hamlet.

This inscription is incomplete. It narrates a hunting accident. A certain
Śeṭṭi Peruṅgāḍaṉ, a paḷḷi of Maṇamai in Āmūr-nāḍu in Āmūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅ-
goṇḍachōḻa-maṇḍalam, and another paḷḷi of the same place named Māṟaṉ Āḍa-
valān went hunting. There, Śeṭṭiperuṅgāḍaṉ shot an arrow at a virugam (a
wild animal like a leopard). The arrow, however, unfortunately hit Māṟaṉ
Āḍavalān and caused his death. Here the inscription stops.

inscription.head:
No. 273.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two detached fragments built into the shrine of the same temple.

p.b:
Sundarapāṇḍyadēva: Year 13, Mithuna śu ••••• = 1264 A.D.
(Jaṭāº Sundara-pāṇḍya-I?)

inscription.p:
These pieces are too fragmentary in nature. However, the record men-
tions a Vishṇu temple called Śendāmaraikkaṇṇaviṇṇagar ••• ••••• , and a
village called Iḍaiyūr.

l.ta:
maṭum iṭaiyūril nirnilat •••• appaṉ eṉṟu peruṭaiyaceṟu •••••
••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 274.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the shrine of the goddess in the same temple.

This inscription is also slightly damaged. It records a gift of two cows
for burning a sandhi lamp in the temple of Tiruvagattīśuramuḍaiyār of Maṇamai
alias Jananāthanallūr in Āmūr-nāḍu in Āmūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam, by a woman named Peruṇkaruṇaiyāṭṭi alias Dēvarkaḷabhaiyam, a
nyāyattār of Uttaraṉmērūr. The cows are stated to have been received by a
number of people including Gavutaman Naṅgai-bhaṭṭaṉ Vaḍugapiḻḻai a
dēvakaṉmi having right of worship in the temple, and Ammai Appaṉ Samaiya-
mudali alias (second name lost), who had agreed to burn the sandhi lamp
accordingly.

l.ta: paṭṭaṉ vaṭukappiḷḷai uḷḷiṭṭārum ammai appaṉ camaiya mutaliyāṉa • ṉam •
• paṭṭar uḷḷiṭṭārum ivvaṉaivaroṅkaikkoṇṭu canti viḷakku erikka ka-
ṭavom ivvaṉaivom ivai vaṭukappiḷḷai eḻuttu ivai •• kai •
rapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ivai āṇṭāṉ ••• eḻuttu itu ••• •••

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 260 of 1909.)
KUṈṈATTŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same shrine.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same deity of the
same Jananāthanallūr occurring in No. 274 above, by a woman stated to be the
daughter of a certain Sāttuvarappu Puṟṟiḍaṅgoṇḍār of Peruṁpāchchēri. It
is stated that the gift was accepted by the temple Śivabrāhmaṇas Gauvutavan
(Gautaman) Ammai Appaṉ alias Paṉpāyēśvara-bhaṭṭaṉ and Naṅgai-bhaṭṭaṉ
Vaḍuga-bhaṭṭaṉ, who had agreed to burn the lamp accordingly.

l.ta:
••• ttu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu āmurkkoṭṭattu āmurnāṭṭu maṇa-
maiyāṉa jaṉaṉāta • •

ti

ka perumpācceric cāttuvarappu puṟṟiṭaṅkoṇṭār makaḷuñ cā • ṭai inṉāya-

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 261 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Kachchāpēśvara temple.

p.b: Tribhº Rājādhirājadēva II: Year 8, Tai = 1171 A.D. (?)

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of the tax paid in oil as well as one paḻaṅkāśu
per oil-press from the oil-presses established on the premises of the temple
of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tirukkachchūr to provide for the expenses of the
temple connected with the burning of two nandā lamps and ten sandhi lamps,
for food-offerings on every Amāvāsya day and for oil for the torches used
on the occasion of taking out (the images) in procession. The gift was made
by the assembly consisting of the oil-mongers, who went by the name of
Mānagar, of Śri-Kāñchimānagar and the people of the 24 Nagaras
of the Kāñchī-maṇḍalam, who had gathered in full strength and with no
absentees, in the temple of Tirukkāṭṭuppaḷḷiuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Kāñ-
chimāṉagar. This endowment is stated to be a Jāti-dharmam. One sama-
iyaṉārāyaṇat-tamiḻavēḷān is stated to have written this on the orders of the
Nagara (i.e., the members of the above-mentioned assembly).

l.ta: r tirumaṭaiviḷākattu iṭṭu āṭukiṟa cekku i-
n nāyaṉārkku nāḷ oṉṟukku eriyun tirununtā viḷakku ira-
ṇṭukku eṇṇai uriyum cantiviḷakku pattukku eṇṇai uḻakkum
amāvāci oṉṟukku tiruvamutu arici patakkum kaṟiyamutu niṟai
ṉāṟpatiṉ palamum neyamutu āḻākkum miḷakamutu oru cevi-

m appamutukku paccai arici kuṟuṇiyum ney nāḻiyu[m*]
kku eṇṇai ṉāḻiyum āka ippaṭiyāl vanta nimantat-
tukku cekkāl vanta kaṭamai eṇṇaiyum cek-
kukku iṟukkum paḻaṅkācu onṟum ittaṉaiyum nitta nimanta-

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 262 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of land for a sandhi lamp to the temple of Tiru-
vālakkōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Tirukkachchūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷattūr-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Śendāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ Tiruvālak-
kōyiluḍaiyāṉ, an oil-monger of the same village. The land is stated to have
been received by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Kāñchikkuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Ēkaṁ-
biradēvaṉ of Bhāradvāja-gōtra who had right (of worship) in the temple,
and four others of the group of Uḍaiyapiḷḷai, namely Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyār
Ēkāmbiradēvar, Uḍaiyapiḷḷai and Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār. It is stated that
a few incomes from the land are to be enjoyed by the donor himself.

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 263 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription seems to be incomplete. It records a gift of some
bronze vessels to the temple occurring in No. 277 above by a private individual
named Kuppai Śōṟuḍaiyāṉ, the kiḻavaṉ of Āyanallūr and the Mūvēndavēḷāṉ
of Ōymānāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-[maṇḍalam*].

l.ta:
āyanallūr kiḻavaṉ kuppai coṟuṭaiyāṉ āṉa jayaṅkoṇṭa-
coḻa oymānāṭṭu muve

ḷikai āyiravanāl niṟai eṇpattaiympalam tarā aṭṭaṇaikkāl eṇpattaiym-
palam taṇṇir amutu ceykiṟa veṭkulā

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Rājarājadēva: Year 14, Dhanus (Mārgaḻi)-śu. 1, Uttirāḍam = 1229 A.D.,
December 18.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that a certain Kirandai Mālaippāgamvaittāṉ
(Skt. Harihara) Śērikkūttaṉ donated a sandhi lamp to the temples of Tiru-
vālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tirukkachchūr alias Nittavinōdanallūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-
nāḍu in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍśōḻa maṇḍalam. The donor is stated
to be a local person. It is also stated that in order to provide for this gift, he
endowed three naṟpaḻaṅkāśu which was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇa
Umaiyōḍuṅkūḍaṉiṉṟāṉ Uyyakkoṇḍāṉ alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala-bhaṭṭaṉ who
had right to worship in the temple and who had undertaken to burn the sandhi
lamp once daily.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tribhuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīrājarājadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 14- vatu
dhanunāya-

ṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrk koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūrāna nitta-
vinotana-

llūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyārkku ivvūr kirantai mālaippākam-
vaittā-

oru cantiyile oru cantiviḷakku erikkak kaṭavanākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ
uyyakko-

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 265 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same shrine.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pūmēvu-
vaḷar and mentions his queen Bhuvanamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ. It records the gift of a
nandā lamp to the temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of the village
occurring in No. 279 above, by an individual named Kirandai Śelvan
Sūriyadēvaṉ of the same place. It is stated that to provide for the gift, he
sold a piece of land for a sum of 5 anṟāḍu-naṟkāśu to the ūr of the place.

l.ta: mevukalaimakaḷ nalamperitu ciṟappa vijaiya māmakaḷ vel-
puyattiruppa icaiyiṉ celvi eṇṭicai viḷaṅka niruparva
ceṅkolocci vāḻi pallūḻi āḻinaṭappa cempoṉ vira-
lattuk kaḷattūr koṭṭattu ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūrāna nittavinotanallū-

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete. It records the gift of a nandā lamp and 90
sheep to meet its expenses, to the temple of the deity of the place occurring
in No. 280 above, by a certain Eyinaṅguḍi-kiḻavaṉ Nambi [••••] of Kaḷḷi ••••
pākkam in Veḷi [.]-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.
It appears that the sheep were left in charge of some one belonging to [•••]
maṇimaṅgalam in Neḍuṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Śeḻumūr-kōṭṭam.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu nāṟpattāṟāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭa-

eyinaṅkuṭi kiḻavaṉ nampika • ti • •

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 267 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king as in No. 280 above.
It records the sale of a land in the same Tirukkachchūr by the ūr of the place
for a sum of 10 gold anṟāḍu-naṟkāśu to a certain Śatturu-kālan Marundan
of Tirukōvalūr in Naḍuvi-nāḍu; who endowed the money with the same
ūr who had agreed to burn a nandā lamp in the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār
of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Rājanārāyaṇaṉ Śambhuvarāyar: Year 13, Tulā (Aippaśi)-ba. 2, Sunday,
Kārttigai = 1350 A.D., October 17, f.d.t. 11.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a garden of Puṉṉai trees, 170 kuḻis in
extent, belonging to the estate of a certain Kāśivan (Kāśyapan) a samaiya-
mudali of the Jīyar of Pushpagiri, to the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of
Tirukkaichchūr (Tirukkachchiūr), probably by Śaṅkaraśiva, the Jīyar.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 269 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with
Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, and mentions his queen Bhuvanamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ. It
records the gift of 90 sheep to provide ghee for nandā lamp in the temple of
Tiruvālakkōyilāṇḍār of the same Tirukkachchūr, by a certain [••••]ṉ
Tiruvaraṅgadēvaṉ alias Virudarājabhuyaṁkarachchōḻa-vāṇiyaṉ of Iruppāka-
ḷakkuḍi in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is stated
that the 90 sheep were divided amongst three or four persons for maintenance,
who had agreed to measure out the ghee to the priests of the central shrine of
the temple.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka cayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ vaḷara malarmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭi cūṭit teṉṉavar nilaikeṭa miṉavar kulaitara

yum ta-

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 270 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

p.b:
Tribhº Rājarājadēva III: Year 3, Makara (Tai) śu, 4, Sunday, Mūla:
Irregular = c. 1218-19 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of two sandhyā lamps to the temple of the
god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tirukkachchūr occurring in No. 284 above, one by
Kālaimālai-pāgamvaittāṉ Villavanāḻvāṉ and another by Kaḷariyakkaḷattuḻāṉ
Oṟṟikoṇḍāṉ Irāśarāśadēvaṉ of the same village. A sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu to provide
for burning of a lamp is stated to have been received by an owner of right of
worship in the temple, the Śivabrāhmaṇa Āḻvān-dēvagaḷ Suttaṉ alias
Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭan of Bhāradvāja-gōtra and another a sum of 3 paḻaṅ-
kāśu for a similar purpose was received by Āḷumpirān Uḍaiyapiḷḷai alias
Alaitāni-bhaṭṭaṉ and his younger brother Maśakaipukkāṉ of Kavuśika
(Kauśika)-gōtra who had agreed to carry out the object of the gift.

l.ta:
turttiyum mulamum peṟṟa nāyaṟṟukkiḻamaināḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa-
coḻamaṇṭalattuk kaḷa-

ttūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu tirukkaccūṟkālai mālaipākam vaittāṉ
villavāḻvāṉum kaḷariyak-

kuḷattuḻāṉ oṟṟikkoṇṭāṉ irācarācatevaṉum uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyi-
luṭaiyārkku vaitta ca-

vakaḷ cuttaṉnāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇāyirapaṭṭan kaikkoṇṭa viḷakku 1- kku
kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu 3m kavucikakottira-

kaikkoṇṭa cantiviḷakku 1-

l.ta: kaṭavatākac cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭuttom ivvaṉaivom itu

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It describes the king as one ‘who
was pleased to take Madurai and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’. It records
the gift of a sandhyā lamp to the temple of the same god and place occurring in
No. 285 above, by a certain Pañchanadivāṇan alias Irāśarāśa Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ
Dēvaṉ [••• •••]. A sum of two paḻaṅkāśu to provide for this, is stated to
have been entrusted to the Śivabrāhmaṇa Kāñchikku •••• bhaṭṭaṉ Uḍaiyapiḷḷai
alias Ānanda[k]kalliyāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ, who had right of worship in the temple,
and who had agreed to carry out the object of the gift.

ttikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyuṅkoṇ-
ṭaruḷiṉa kulottuṅkacoḻa

vatu tirukkaccūr tiruvālakkoyil ṉāyaṉāṟkku pañcanativāṉaṉāṉa irāca-

yāṇa

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp to the same temple in the
same place as in No. 286 above by Koṟṟaṉ Koṟṟaṉ Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyāṉ
Śelvappiḷḷai of Piḷḷaippākkam alias Kaiytavakaiytava-nallūr in Māgaṉūr-
nāḍu in Śeṅgāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. A Śivabrāhmaṇa
who had the right of worship in the temple, named Āḷumpirāṉ Uḍaiyapiḷḷai alias
Sadāśiva-bhaṭṭaṉ of Kavuśika(Kauśika)-gōtra received a sum of three
paḻaṅkāśu and had agreed to burn the lamp.

l.ta:
ttūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭut tirukkaccūr tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāya-
nārkku immaṇṭalattuc ceṅkāṭṭuk koṭṭa-

ttu mākaṉūrnāṭṭup piḷḷaippākkamāṉa kaiytavakaiytavanallūr koṟṟaṉ koṟṟaṉ
tiruvekampamuṭaiyāṉ celvap-

ippaḻaṅkācu muṉ-
ṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭu ivviḷakku onṟum canti-

rātittavarai erippeṉākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuteṉ uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyāṉa
catācivapaṭṭaṉeṉ itu paṉmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the gift of some utensils like parikalam probably to
the same temple at Tirukkachchūr as occurs in No. 287 above, by a certain
worshipper Āṇḍār Ambaruḍaiyān Yirakoḍuvantān.

l.ta: vatu tirukkaccūril kumpiṭṭirukkum āṇṭār amparuṭaiyān yirakoṭuvan-
tiruparikalam oṉṟiṉāl eṭai nūṟṟumuppattiraṇṭum parikalam onṟiṉāl

edition.l: eṭai eḻupattoṉṟarai [va]ṭivamum āka [ 202 ṉāl] eṭai irunūṟṟumu-

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 274 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription refers to the failure of crops in Tirukkachchūr in the 10th
year in the reign of the king and the consequent troubles of the people.
It records the grant of 15 kāśu in lieu of the land given by the ūr in the
Kīḻakkārāṇai of the piḍāgai of Tirukkachchūr, made by a certain Kuppai-
śōṟuḍaiyān alias Oymānāṭṭu-vēḷāṉ, the kiḻavaṉ of Āyalūr, for food offerings
to the deity Tiruvālākkōyil-uḍaiyār and for the eleven sandhyā lamps gifted by
the ūr itself. The ūr, receiving the amount from the donor, had agreed to
perform the services accordingly. The villages of Kūḍalūr, Śiṟumaṇūr,
Mēlviḷimalli and Pērāmaṇūr are stated to form the boundary of the said land.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu muppatteḻāvatu kaḷattūrk
koṭṭattu ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu tirukkaccūrāṉa nittavinotanallūr ūrom yāṇ-

namikukaiyil āyalūr
kiḻavaṉ [kuppai]coṟuṭaiyānāṉa oymānāṭṭu veḷāṉ tiruvālakkoyi ••
• •

ṉāṟku kaṟi amurtu nālukkum ney amurtukkum taiyir amutukkum aṭaikkā
amurtukkum arici kuṟuṇikkum ūril eṅkaḷāl viṭṭuvarukira

viḷakku patinonṟukkum uṭalāka eṅkaḷūrpiṭākai kiḻkkārāṇai kūṭalūr
ellaikku meṟkum tenpāṟ ciṟumaṇūr ellaikku vaṭakku

melviḷimallikkuk kiḻakkum perāmaṇūr ellaikku teṟkkum iṉṉāṉku ellaik-
kum naṭuvupaṭṭa nilam kāṭu veṭṭikko-

ḷakkaṭavanāna ivaṉ pakkal nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa kācu patinaiñcu ikkācu patinaiñ-
cukkum poliūṭṭāka kuṭuttom innilattukku i •••••
nam paṇṇi kuṭuttom tirukkaccūr ūrom itan

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 32 cows and a bull to provide daily one
uḻakku of ghee for burning a nandā lamp in the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍai-
yār at Tirukkachchūr as occurring in No. 289 above, by one Kulōttuṅgaśōḻak-
kaṇṇappaṉ-Ādināyaṉ Pañchanadivāṇaṉ alias Rājarāja Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ of
Tiruchchuram in Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Puliyūr-koṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa
śōḻamaṇḍalam. The cows and the bull were received by some Śivabrāhmaṇas
of the temple, who had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually.

A postscript at the end of the inscription states that in the 18th (year of
king ?), a standing lamp weighing 132 palam was given to enable the burning
of the nandā lamp mentioned above.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉpāka maturaiyumiḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭit-
talaiyuṅ koṇṭaruḷiṉa

āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūṟkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟa-
nāṭṭut tirukkaccūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyāṟku immaṇṭala-

ttup puliyūrkoṭṭattu kulontuṅkacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu tiruccurattu kulottuṅka-
coḻakkaṇṇappaṉātināyaṉ pañcanativāṇaṉāṉa rājarāja nilaṅkaraiyaṉ tiru-

vālakkoyiluṭaiyāṟku vaitta tirununtā viḷakkoṉṟukku nāḷoṉṟukku ney-
yuḻakkukkup poliyūṭṭu

ṭṭanum ivvaṉaivom ivar pakkal
kaikkoṇṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭum

edition.l:
erikkakkaṭavom ivvaṉaivom itu paṉmāheśvararakṣai ||— ittevarkku pati
ṉeṭṭāvatu ittirunantāviḷakku erikka iṭṭa ittirukkuttiviḷakku oṉṟiṉāl
tarā

inscription.head:
No. 291.
(A.R. No. 276 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The inscription refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai
and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’. It records a gift of land for burning
a nandā lamp in the same temple at the same Tirukkachchūr as occuring in
No. 290 above, by Kirāndai Amararkaṇāyakkar Kākkunāyakkan and his
younger brother Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyān of the same Tirukkachchūr. It is
stated that the gift-land was sold to one Kirandai Ārumaṇa-nāyanār by the
many Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who had undertaken to carry out the object
of the gift with the money thus obtained. At the end of the inscription, there
is a passage which refers to the 15th year of Perumāḷ (king) Sundarapāṇḍya-
dēva.

l.ta:
kaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ-
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu

maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār
tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku ivvūrk kirāntai amararkaṇāyakar kākku-
nāyakaṉum ivaṉ tampi

tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyāṉum ivviruvom iṉṉāyaṉāṟkuc cantirātittavarai
celvatāka vaitta tirunu
iṉṉilattukkuk kiḻtalaiyiṟ kiṇaṟoṉṟum vaṇṇakkaṉ kuṇṭil rūpāy

kiṇaṟoṉṟum iṉṉilattāl vanta iṟaiyuñ cilviṟai coṟupāṭṭum veṭṭi
muṭṭe āḷu • ḷiṭṭa cilliṟai kaḷiṟai iḻiccik kuṭukkak kaṭavomākac cilā-
lekai paṇṇik kuṭu-

ikkalveṭ[ṭi] ippa[ṭi] kuḻi
aiññūnṟu irupattaiñcum kiṇaṟu ikkoyiṟ kāṇi uṭaiya civa-

ppirāmaṇar palarum ivvūr kirantai ārumaṇa nāyan[ā*]rkku kāṇiyāka viṟṟuk
kuṭuttu poruḷuk kaikkoṇṭu cantirā[titta*]varai ittirununtā viḷakku oṉ-
ṟum tirunaṭaimāḷikai vaṭapār •• ttile perumāḷ cuntarapāṇṭitevaṟku

inscription.head:
No. 292.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp and three paḻaṅkāśu for
it by Koṟṟaṅ-Koṟṟaṉ Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyāṉ Tulaiyādaśelvaṉ alias Aṉanta-
paṉmaṉ of Piḷḷaippākkam alias Kaitava-Kaitavanallūr in Māgaṇūr-nāḍu in Śeṅ-
gāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam, in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, to the same deity of the same
place as occurring in No. 291 above. The money of three kāśu was accepted by
the Śivabrāhmaṇa who had the right of worship in the temple, named Āḻvāṉ
Dēvargaḷ Sundaran alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra].

l.ta: ruvekampamuṭaiyāṉ tulaiyāta celvaṉāṉa aṉantapaṉmaṉ vaitta cantiviḷak-

••• ••• ṉak kuṭutteṉ tevarkaḷcuntaranāna nāṟpatteṇṇāyirapaṭṭeṉ
itu panmā

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 278 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

The inscription is built in at the beginning. So, the name of the king, the
date and some other important details are lost.

It appears to record a gift of lamp and the undertaking given in this regard
by four persons. Neḍuṅguṉṟa-nāḍu, Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-[maṇḍalam] and [••• •••]
Perumpūdūr are mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete at the end. It seems to be a copy of No. 289
above. However the 10th year in which the failure of crops is stated in
No. 289 to have occurred is not clear in the present record.

l.ta: yamurtukkum arici kuṟuṇikkum ūri eṅkālāl viṭṭu varukiṟa
llaikku vaṭakkum melpāṟ pāḻikkiṇaṟṟukku mel vi-
ḷimpukku kiḻakkum peramaṇūr ellaikku teṟkum iṉnāṉku e-
payir ceyvittuk koḷḷakkaṭavatāka ivan pakkal nāṅkaḷ ko-
ṇṭa kācu patiṉaiñcu ikkācu patiṉaiñcukkum poliyūṭṭāka-
k kuṭuttom iṉnilattukku iṟai ivaṉaṟuvatu epper-

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhyā lamp to the temple of the god
Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya Mahādēvar of Tirukkachchūr alias Nittavinōdanallūr,
by Amarkaḷa-nāyakan, a Vēḷān. Three cows gifted by him to meet the expenses
of the lamp were accepted by four Śivabrāhmaṇas, having right to worship in
the temple, who had agreed to carry out, perpetually, the object of the gift.

l.ta: tiviḷakku onṟukku inta śrīkoyilil nālopāti kāṇi uṭaiya
koṇṭa pacu muṉṟum kaikkoṇṭu cantirātittavarai celuttak kaṭa-

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 281 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of one nandā lamp to the same temple
of the same Tirukkachchūr occurring in No. 295 above by the same person who
figures as the donor in No. 290 above, but who is stated, here, to belong to
Mullaipāḍi, in the same nāḍu, kōṭṭam and maṇḍalam. It is stated that 32 cows
and one bull which he donated for the purpose of burning the lamp, were
entrusted to maṉrāḍi Āṭkoṇḍak-kōṉ, his son Āḍavallāṉ and the latter's
younger brother Śiṟṟi of Śeṅguṉṟam in the nāḍu of the same name, who had
agreed to measure out daily one uḻakku of ghee by the measure called Irāśa-
kēsari.

l.ta:
vattikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉai muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīku-

ttūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyi-
luṭaiya nāyanāṟku

innāṭṭuc ceṅkuṉṟattilirukkum maṉṟāṭi āṭkoṇṭakkoṉūm ivaṉ makaṉ āṭa-
vallāṉum iva-
ṉ tampi ciṟṟiyum ivvaṉaivom innāyanāṟku erippatāna tirununtāviḷakku

nāḷ oṉṟukku irācakecariyāl aḷappatāna ney uḻakkukku poliyūṭṭāka in
nāyanārkku imaṇṭalattu ikkoṭṭattu i-

m iṣapam kam pacu muppattiraṇṭum iṣapam oṉṟum
kaikkoṇṭu
ṟ celuttak kaṭavomākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom ivvaṉaivom itu

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 282 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is incomplete. It describes the king as one ‘who was pleased
to take Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya, and
‘who had a hero’s and a victor's coronation’. It records an order of a certain
Nandipaṉmaṉ and mentions Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
vattikaḷ maturai yumiḻamuṅ karuvūrum pāṇṭiyaṉ
muṭittalaiyuṅ ko-

ṇṭaruḷi virā apiṣekamum vicaiyā apiṣekamum paṇṇi aruḷiṉa śrīkulot-
tuṅka coḻatevarā-

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 283 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍān, Kulōttuṅga III (?) Year 36 = 1213-14 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift made by the king of 48 vēli of land in
Śeṅguṉṟam alias Arumoḻidēvanallūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, and 67 vēli of land in Vellāḷa Maḍaiyār
Puḷḷiyam in Valla-nāḍu as dēvadāna iṟaiyili to the temple of the same deity in
the same Tirukkachchūr occurring in No. 297 above, to provide for offerings
to the deity therein. The gift was made on the request of Chēdiyarāyaṉ.
The signatories were Irāsanārāyaṇa Mūvēnda-vēḷāṉ, who was the tiru-
mandira-ōlai, Pallavarāyaṉ, Viḻiṇattaraiyaṉ, Toṇḍaimāṉ, Śiddharāyaṉ,
Chēdikularāyaṉ, Śiṅgarāyaṉ, Aṅgarāyaṉ, Vijayarāyaṉ and Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ.

l.ta:
tti koneriṉmaikoṇṭāṉ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa
maṇṭalattu kaḷattūr ko-

ṭṭattu ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyāṟ koyiliṟ teva-
kaṉmikkum śrīmā-

rakaṇkāṇi cevārkaḷukku śrīkāriyañceyvāṉūkkum ittevarkku veṇṭum
nilan taṅkaḷukku iṟuppatā-

ka iṉṉāṭṭuc ceṅkuṉṟamāṉa arumoḻitevanallūrile nāṟpatteṇ veli
nilamum vallanāṭṭu ve-

ḷḷāḷa maṭaiyār puḷḷiyam nilam arupattu eḻuveliyum muppattu āṟāvatu
picāṉa mutal antarāyam

pāṭṭa[m*] uṭpaṭa tevatāṉa iṟaiyili āka vikkaṭavatāka peṟaveṇumeṉṟu ceti
yarāyaṉ namakku coṉṉamai-

yil ippaṭi ceyakkaṭavatākac colli kaṇakkilum iṭṭuk koḷḷakkaṭavarkaḷāka
varikku kūṟu cevārkaḷukkum coṉ-

ṉom inilam muppattu āṟāvatu picāṉamutal antarāyam pāṭṭam uṭpaṭa it-
tevarkkut tevatāṉa iṟaiyili ākak ko-

ṇṭu nivaṉtañ celutta paṇuka eḻutiṉa tirumantiraolai irācanārāyaṇa muventa
veḷāṉ ivai pallavarāyaṉ eḻuttu ivai viḻi-

ṇattaraiyaṉeḻuttu ivai toṇṭaimāṉeḻuttu ivai cittarāyaṉeḻuttu ivai
cetikularāyaṉeḻuttu ivai ciṅkarā-

yaṉeḻuttu ivai kāṭavarāyaṉeḻuttu ivai aṅkarāyaṉeḻuttu ivai vicaiya
rāyaṉeḻuttu ivai nilakaṅka-

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 284 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Tribhuvanavīradēva Kulōttuṅga III: Year 37 = 1214-15 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription is incomplete. It records a gift of a sandhi lamp and a
sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu to provide for that, to the same temple as occurs in
No. 283 above, by a certain Palakaṇṇan Vīdiviṭaṅkaṉ Vaḍuganāthaṉ of
Iraiyūr in Kuṉṟa-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam.
The money was accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇa who had right of worship in
the temple, named Vināyaka-bhaṭṭaṉ, son of Kūtta-bhaṭṭaṉ of Gautama-
[gōtra], who must have agreed to carry out the object of the gift, as usual.

l.ta:
tiruvālakkopiluṭaiya nāyaṉāṟku immaṇṭalattu ūṟṟukkāṭṭukkoṭṭattuk
kuṉṟaṉāṭṭu iraiyūr pala-

kaṇṇan vitiviṭaṅkaṉ vaṭukanātaneṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyiṟ
kāṇi uṭaiya civa

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 285 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

The king is described as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam, and
the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’. This inscription records a gift of two sandhi
lamps to the same temple in the same Tirukkachchūr, made by a certain
Āyiluḻāṉ Āḷuḍaiyāṉ Kākkunāyakkaṉ of Uṟattūr in Kōṉādi-nāḍu in Śeṅgāṭṭuk-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍāśōḻa-maṇḍalam. It is stated that he gave 24 sheep
to provide ghee for the lamps to the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple and that
these sheep were received by a certain Śāmik-kōṉāṉ Kākkunāyakka-kōṉāṉ,
son of maṉṟāḍi Māḍak-kōṉāṉ of Tirukkachachūr itself, who had agreed
to measure out two nāḻi of ghee monthly.

The inscription also records that the same Śāmik-kōṉāṉ Kākkunāyakka-
kōṉāṉ had previously received 12 sheep from the temple treasury to provide
ghee for a sandhi lamp, and that he had agreed to measure out one nāḻi of ghee
monthly in this connection. The measure used to measure out the ghee is
called Irāśakēsari.

l.ta:
ttikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalai
yuṅ koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulot-

tuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu irupatteṭṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk
kaḷattūṟ koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉ-

ṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūrt tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkku immaṇṭalattuc ceṅ-
kāṭṭukoṭṭattuk koṉā[tinā]-

ṭṭu uṟattūrāyiluḻāṉ āḷuṭaiyāṉ kākkunāyakkaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku iraṇ-
ṭukkum ikkoyir kāṇiyuṭaiya civappirā-

maṇar vacam viṭṭa āṭu irupattunālum ittirukkaccūrilirukkum maṉṟāṭi māṭattāṉ
makaṉ cāmikkoṉāṉ kākkunāyakka-

koṉāṉ ivarkaḷ pakkal nāṉ kaikkoṇṭu mātam oṉṟukku cela virācakecari-
yāle irunāḻi ney can-

tirātittavarai muṭṭāmal aḷakkakkaṭavenākac cammatittuc cilālekai paṇṇik-
kuṭutte-

ṉ maṉṟāṭi māṭattāṉ cāmikkoṉāṉ kākkunāyakkakoṉeṉ nāṉ muṉṉāḷil
iṉṉāyaṉār cipaṇṭārattu

ṉṟukkuk kaikkoṇṭa āṭu paṉṉiraṇṭukkum mātam oṉṟukku iṉṉāḻiyāl
aḷakkum ney nāḻiyāka viḷakku muṉ-

ṟum cantirātittavarai celuttuveṉākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ itu
paṉmākecura

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 286 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a royal order drafted by tirumandira-ōlai
Mīṉavaṇ-mūvēndavēḷāṉ and issued through his officers, to the dēvakaṉmi,
śrīmāhēśvarakaṅgāṉi-cheyvār and śrīkāryam-cheyvār of the temple of Tiru-
[vai]yār-uḍaiyār of kuttaṉpākkam in Māṅgāḍu-nāḍu in Puliyūr-kōṭṭam alias
Kulōttuṅgaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam granting the lands of
25 vēli from Pāripākkam, 40 vēli from Arumpākkam and 10 vēli from
Māttūr, all belonging to the same nāḍu ḍēvadāṉa-iṟaiyili for the offerings to
this god. This document was attested by Toṇḍaimāṉ, Nigariliśōḻa-pallava-
rayaṉ, Villavarayaṉ, Kaliṅgarāyaṉ and Mūvēndarayaṉ, who figure as
signatories.

l.ta:
koneriṉmai koṇṭān jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻa
maṇṭalattu puliyūrkoṭṭamāṉa

kulottuṅka coḻavaḷanāṭṭu māṅkāṭṭu nāṭṭu kūttaṉ pākkattu uṭaiyār tiru
[vai]yār uṭaiyār koyilil tevaka-

ra kaṅkāṇi ceyvārkaḷukkum śrīkāriyañ ceyvāṉukkum it-
(ta)tevarku veṇṭu nivaṉtaṅ[ka*]ḷukku iṟuppa-

tāka innāṭṭu pāripākkam pottakappaṭi nilam irupattai veliyum arumpāk-
[ka*]m nilam nāṟpatiṟṟu veliyum māttūr nilam

patiṟṟuveliyum ittevaṟkku tevatāna iṟaiyiliyāka iṭakkaṭavatākap peṟa
veṇumeṉṟu vaṟca

ṭṭi ippaṭi namakku toṇṭaimānum namakkuc co[ṉ*]ṉavāṟe ippaṭi ceyyak-
kaṭavatāka colli kaṇakkiṉum iṭṭuk koḷḷakkaṭa-

va[rkaḷ] varikkūṟu ceyvārkaḷukkuc coṉnom iṉnilam irupattoṉṟāvatiṉ
etirāmāṇṭu mutal ittevarku tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyākak-

kaik koṇṭu nivaṉtañ celuttap paṇṇuka eḻutiṉāṉ tirumantira olai mīṉa-
vaṉ muveṉtaveḷāṉ yāṇṭu irupattoṉṟāvatu

ivai toṇṭaimāṉ eḻuttu ivai nikarilicoḻa pallavaraya-
ṉeḻuttu ivai villavarāyaṉ eḻuttu ivaik kaliṅkarāyaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The king is described as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai and the crow-
ned head of the Pāṇḍya’. The inscription records a gift of two sandhi lamps
to the same temple in the same Tirukkachchūr as occurring in No. 300 above,
one by a local man named Karandai Chērikkūttaṉ Mālaipāgamvaiṭṭān (Skt.
Harihara) and another by a certain Kumaraṉ Śāttaṉ Vāraṅgāttāṉ Tiruchchi-
ṟṟambalam-uḍaiyāṉ of Vēlūr in Damaṉūr-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam.
A sum of 2 paḻaṅkāśu from the former donor was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇa
Kāṅchikuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Uḍaiyaliḷḷai alias Nityakalāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ of
Bhāradvāja-[gōtra] and a sum of 2 paḻaṅkāśu from the latter donor was received
by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Āḻvāṉ Dēvappiḷḷai alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ
of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra]. Both were owners of right of worship in the temple,
and they had agreed to carry out the object of the gift perpetually.

l.ta:
ttikaḷ maturaiyum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅ
koṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkaco-

āvatu tirukkaccūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭai
yārkku ivvūr karantai cerikkūttaṉ

ṭukkoṭṭattut tamaṉūrnāṭṭu velūr kumaraṉ cāttaṉ vāraṇaṅkāttāṉ tiruc-
ciṟṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku o-

mmatittu cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom ivviruvom iti panmā-
he

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 288 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Kulōttuṅga-chōḷadēva III: Year 3, Āvaṇi 3 = 1180-81 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of two sandhi lamps to the temple of the same
deity in the same Tirukkachchūr as occurring in No. 302 above, by a certain
Puduppākkiḻān-Ādan of Uḻalūr alias Rājarājanallūr in Kaḷattūr-nāḍu in
Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam. It is stated that the donor entrusted 4 paḻaṅkāśu to provide
for the lamps with the Śivabrāhmaṇa Sṛishṭīśvaramuḍaiyān Iṟaṇindān alias
Tillaināyaka-bhaṭṭan of Kāśyapa-[gōtra] belonging to Kānaṟaikuḍi, who had
right of worship in the temple and who had agreed to carry out the object of
the gift hereditarily.

l.ta:
ṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūrkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr tiru
vālakkoyiluṭaiyārkku ikkoṭṭattu kaḷattūr nāṭ-

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The inscription records a gift of two sandhi lamps to the temple in the same
Tirukkachchūr as occurs in No. 303 above, by two local persons named
Nochchikkiḻān Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyān Tillaikkūttaṉ and his younger brother
Tiruvāvaḍutuṟaip-perumāḷ. It is stated that a sum of 4 paḻaṅkāśu endowed
by them to provide for the lamps, was received by two Śivabrāhamaṇas-
having right of worship in the temple, named Tiruvuḍaiyāṉ alias Tillaināyaka,
bhaṭṭaṉ of Gautama-[gōtra] and his younger brother Sūryadēvaṉ alias
Paṉmāhēśvara-bhaṭṭaṉ, who had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] tribhuvanaccakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻadēvaṟku yāṇṭu 5-
āvatu tirukkaccūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakko-

l.ta:
yiluṭaiyāṟku ivvūr noccikkiḻān tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyān tillaikkūttaṉum
ivan tampi tiruvāvaṭutuṟaipperumāṉum

paṭṭaṉu mivviruvo mivarkaḷ pakkal kaik-
koṇṭa paḻaṅkācu

mmatittu cilālekai paṇṇik
kuṭuttomivviruvom itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 290 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The king is described as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam, Karu-
vūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records an order of Nandipaṉmaṉ. Its contents are as follows. Two
persons Ambar-kiḻavaṉ and Neṟkuṉṟakkiḻāṉ owning lands in Maḍaiyār-puḷḷi-
yam in Valla-nāḍu in Kaḷattūrk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam
represented that the dēvadāna of the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tiruk-
kachchūr of Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu, were getting depleted and that the temple trustees
(tāṉattār) were saying that the services in the temple and repairs to it were not
conducted. To retrieve the situation the dēvadāna and the pallichchanda lands,
in Maḍaiyār-puḷḷiyam, measuring 67 vēli in extent together with garden-land
were granted to the temple for services to the deity from the 280th day of the
35th year in the reign of the king. The two persons mentioned above should
meet the temple trustees and pay the tax-incomes and arrange for further
transactions in the matter.

l.ta: yaṉ muṭittalaiyuṅkoṇṭu virā apiṣekamum vicaiya apiṣekamum paṇ-
tūrkkoṭṭattu vallanāṭṭu maṭaiyār puḷḷiyaṅkāṇi uṭaiya amparkiḻavaṉum
neṟkuṉṟaṅkiḻāṉum kaṇṭu ikkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyā-

tillaiyeṉṟu tāṉattār collukaiyāle taṅkaḷūr uḷḷāt tevatāṉa-
p paḷḷiccantamāṉa nilaṅkaḷ nikki niṉṟa nilam aṟupatteḻuveliyum kollainila-

mum innāyaṉārkku muppattañcāvatu nāḷ iṟunūṟṟu eṇpatu mutalveṇ-
ṭum niva-

ntaṅkaḷukku iṟuppatākat tevatāṉamāka iṭṭatu tāṅkaḷ ikkoyiliṟ tāṉat-
tārai-
kkaṇṭu kaṭamaiyum iṟuttuk kuṭimaiyuñ ceyyap paṇṇuka ivai nantipaṉma-

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 291 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The inscription is badly damaged. The king is described as ‘who was
pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.
It appears to record a gift of 3,000 (three thousand) Aṉṟāḍu naṟpudukkāśu
for a certain service (probably lamp) in the same temple in the same place, as
occurs in No. 305 above. A certain Tiruchchiṟṟambala-bhaṭṭaṉ is mentioned.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 292 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This fragmentary inscription appears to record a gift of some sandhi lamp.
The endowed material (money or sheep) is stated to have been received by
Uḍaiyapiḷḷai alias Tiruchchiṟṟambala-bhaṭṭaṉ who had agreed to measure one
uḻakku of ghee.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• ṉa uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyāṉa tirucciṟṟampalapaṭṭaṉeṉ iṉ-
ṉāyanā[r*] cipaṇṭārattu nāṉ kaikkoṇ-

••• ••• •••• ruva • inney uḻakkum cantirātittavarai vi •
ḷaṭṭāmai aḷakka kaṭaveṉākac cammatittu cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ
uṭaiyā-

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 292-A of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This is too fragmentary a piece. However, it records a gift of a lamp
(detail lost) to the temple of the god. Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya-nāynār of the
place (name lost, but evidently the same Tirukkachchūr occuring in No. 306.
etc., above), by a certain Porudapiḷḷai alias Abhimānabhūshaṇamāṇikkam,
daughter of Ariyapillai, a dēvaraḍiyāḷ of the same temple. It seems that the
gift was accepted by Āḻvāṉ •••••] alias Kaṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭan of Bhāradvāja
[gōtra], who had right of worship in the temple.

l.ta: r āḷuṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyanār koyiliṟ tevaraṭiyāḷ ariya-

vālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉā ••• ••• •••• viḷakku oṉṟukku ik
koyiṟkāṇi uṭai-

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 292-B of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

Only the last line of the inscription is all that is preserved. Mentions a
certain Viṉāya[ka*]-bhaṭṭaṉ who was probably the receipient of the gift to the
temple.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• vaṉākac cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutteṉ viṉāya[ka*]paṭṭa-
nen itu ci māhe

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 293 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This incomplete inscription records that a certain person (name not clear
but probably reads as Iṉaṉ) of the same Tirukkachchūr had obtained 32 cows
and a bull from the bhaṇḍāra of the temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār
and in lieu thereof had agreed to burn a nandā lamp in the temple by measuring
one uḻakku of ghee perpetually.

l.ta:
āvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrkkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟa-
nāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiru-

pacuvil i[v]vūril iṉeṉ ināya-
nārkku oru tirununtā viḷakku cantirātittavarai celuttuvenāka nāṉ
kaikkoṇṭa naṟ-

kku cela virācakecariyāl aḷakkak-
kaṭave ••• uḻakku ittirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukkum cantirātittavarai
nāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 293-A of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription too is extremely fragmentary, and contains only its last
few lines. It appears to refer to the gift of 5 paḻaṅkāśu for sandhi lamp (other
details lost). The person who accepted the endowment is stated to be Dēvagal
Sundaram alias Nāṟpatte[ṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ] (Cf. No. 292 above.)

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 294 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is very much damaged. It appears to record a gift of
land made by the king, to provide for all the services in the same temple and in
the same place as occur in No. 307, etc., above. A number of signatories, includ-
ing Aṉapāya Mūvēndavēḷāṉ, the tirumandira-ōlai, are mentioned.

l.ta:
tirumukappaṭi ••• ••• •••• kaḷattūrkoṭṭattu ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tiruk-

•••••

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 295 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same maṇḍapa.

This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai,
Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records the provision of irrigation facility to the lands in Āmbaṟpākkam
alias Irandiḍutiyāginallūr which was a hamlet of Śeṅguṉṟum and a dēvadāna
of the temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyār of Tirukkachchūr, also occurring
in the above records. It is stated that this dēvadāna hamlet was owned by
some individuals such as Śeṅguṉṟaṅ-kiḻāṉ Oṟṟikoṇḍāṉ Taḻuvakkuḻaindāṉ of
Śeṅguṉṟam, his younger brother Kārāṇaiviṭaṅkaṉ and the latter's younger
brother Perumānambi, and Kumaran Śāttan Vāraṇaṅgāttāṉ-Aṟamuḍaiyāṉ of
Vēlūr in Damaṉūr-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam, his younger brother Tiruch-
chiṟṟambalamuḍaiyāṉ, his younger brother Chērikkūttaṉ and his younger
brother Tiruvēgambamuḍaiyāṉ, who, with the help of paddy provided by the
temple, permitted the irrigation facility consisting of 240 kuḻi of land and kalli
tuṁbu (probably a sluice) through the tank called Periyadēvapputtēri, which
irrigated their estate Veṇśiṉṟiyampākkam.

koṇṭaruḷiya śrīku-

ttavarai nilaviḻukkāṭṭile nir pāyak kaṭavatākac cilālekai
paṇṇik kuṭuttom ivva-

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 296 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhi lamp and a sum of 3 paḻaṅkāśu
for it, to the same temple in the same Tirukkachchūr village by Perumpēṟuḍaiyāṉ
Nambiyāḻvāṉ of Mūlaṅguḍi in Uyyakkoṇḍa-vaḷanāḍu in Śōḻamaṇḍalam.
The money was accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Dēvargaḷ Sundaran alias
Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra], who had right of worship
in the temple, and who had agreed to carry out the object of the gift.

l.ta:
r koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālaṅkoyiluṭaiyāṟku
coḻamaṇṭalattu uyyakkoṇṭavaḷa-

nāṭṭu mulaṅkuṭi kāṇiyuṭaiya perumpeṟuṭaiyāṉ nampiyāḻvāṉ vaitta canti-
viḷakku

tevarkaḷ cuntaranāna nāṟpat-
