erikkak kaṭavenāka cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭutte-

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 297 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription describes the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai
and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same temple of Tirukkachchūr
occurring in the above-discussed inscriptions, by Kulālaṉ Kākkunāyakkaṉ, a
karaṇattāṉ and owner of property in the village. It further records that a sum
of 3 paḻaṅkāśu was endowed by him for this purpose, and this money was
accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Kāñchikuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Uḍayapiḷḷai
alias Nityakalyāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra], who was the Śivāchārya
of the temple and who had (1/4) share in the right of worship in the same temple.
It is further stated that this bhaṭṭa had agreed to burn the sandhi lamp per-
petually.

l.ta:
āvatu tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyi-
luṭaiyārkku ivvūrkkāṇi uṭaiya karaṇattāṉ kulālaṉ

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 298 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This incomplete inscription describes the king as one ‘who was pleased to
take Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya and who
was pleased to anoint the heroes and the victors’.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same temple occurring in No. 315,
etc., above by a certain Palakaṇṇaṉ Vīdīviṭaṅkaṉ Vaḍuganāthan of Iṟaiyūr.
in Kuṉṟa-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkaṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍasōḻa-maṇḍalam,
He endowed 3 paḻaṅkāśu for this purpose and the same was accepted by the
Śivabrāhmaṇa Dēvappiḷḷai alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ, son of Āḻvāṉ-
bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra], who had right of worship in the temple.
Further details are missing.

l.ta:
vaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum karuvūrum pāṇṭi-
yaṉmuṭittalaiyuṅkoṇṭu virāvapiṣe

ṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟaṉāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāya-
ṉārkku immaṇṭalattu ūṟṟukkāṭṭukkoṭ-

ṭattuk kuṉṟaṉāṭṭu iṟaiyūṟ palakaṇṇaṉ vitiviṭaṅkaṉ vaṭukaṉātaneṉ vaitta
cantiviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyil kāṇi uṭaiya civa-

ṇan pārattuvāci āḻvāṉpaṭṭaṉ makaṉ tevappiḷḷaiyāṉa nāṟpatteṇṇā-
yirapaṭṭaṉeṉ kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take Madurai
and Īḻam and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records a gift of two sandhi lamps to the same temple as occurs in No. 316,
etc., above, by a certain Śendāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ, son of Kumāraṉ Śāttaṉ Tiru-
vēgambamuḍaiyāṉ of Vēlūr in Damaṉūr-nāḍu, in Ūttukkāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam. He
also endowed a sum of 4 paḻaṅkāśu for the purpose. The money was accepted
by the Śivabrāhmaṇa Uḍaiyappiḷḷai alias Nittakalyāṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ alias son of
Kāñcikkuṟi Śrīkaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ Dēvappiriyaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra] who had
right of worship in the temple and who had agreed to carry out the burning
of the lamps.

l.ta:
varttikaḷ maturaiyum īḻamum pāṇṭiyaṉ muṭit
talaiyuṅkoṇṭaruḷiya śrīkulottuṅkacoḻa

mutal tirukkaccūr tiruālakkoyil uṭaiya
nāyaṉārkkuc cantirātittavarai celvatāka uttukkā-

ṭṭuk koṭṭattut tamaṉūr nāṭṭu velūrk kumāraṉ cāttaṉ tiruvekampamuṭaiyāṉ
makaṉ centāmaraikkaṇṇaṉ vaitta cantiviḷakku

kāñcikkuṟi śrīkaṇṭa-
paṭṭaṉ tevappiriyaṉ makaṉ uṭaiyappiḷḷaiyāṉa nit-
takalliyāṇa paṭṭaṉeṉ ivaṉ pakkal nāṉ kaikkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu

nāluṅ-
kaikkoṇṭu iccantiviḷakku iraṇṭum cantirātittavarai e-

rikka kaṭaveṉākac cammatittuc cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭutten uṭaiya piḷḷaiyāṉa
nittakalliyāṇapaṭṭaṉeṉ itu paṉmā

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the outer prākāra of the same temple.

This inscription records that from the above-mentioned year in the reign
of the king, each trader, each loom of a weaver, each loom of Śāliyar, and each
oil-monger of Tirukkachchūr should pay annually 6 paṇam and of the total
income thus derived one half should go to the Śrībhaṇḍāra (of the temple).

l.ta:
ccūr āyampuṟakkalaṉai kaṭamai koḷḷumiṭattu śrīpaṇṭārappāti uḷpaṭa viyāpārikaḷ
per oṉṟukku āṇṭu oṉ-

ṟukku āṟupaṇam koḷḷavum kayikkoḷar taṟi oṉṟukku āṇṭu oṉṟukku āṟu
paṇam koḷḷavum cāliyar taṟi oṉṟukku ā-

ṇṭu oṉṟukku āṟupaṇam koḷḷavum vāṇiyar per oṉṟukku āṇṭu oṉ-
ṟukku āṟupaṇam koḷḷavum ippaṭi kaṭamai

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 301 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription appears to be incomplete.

It states that a certain Perunambigaḷ, belonging to the santāna (lineage
or family) of certain Śāttaṉār who had the titles of Muttamiḻ Āchāryar and
Tamiḻ-karaikaṇḍa and who was a member of the Gaḍikai (Skt. Ghaṭikā, a learned
assembly) of Kāvaṉūr alias Poyyāmoḻimaṅgalam in Kōṉāsura-nāḍu in
Śeṅgāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam owned Poyyāmoḻimaṅgalam as his kāṇi.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva: Year 13+1,
Kaṟkaṭaka (Āḍi)-ba. 10, Monday, Kārttikai = 1290 A.D., July 3.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a nandā lamp and a standing lamp to the
temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyanāyaṉār of Tirukkachchūr in
Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Śiṟṟēṟi-
uḍaiyāṉ Tulaiyāḍa selvaṉ alias Chittiramēḻi-vēḷāṉ, the karaṇattāṉ of Maṇanallūr
alias Vīraśōḻa-chchatuṟuvēdimaṅgalam in Śembūṟ-kōṭṭam in the same Jayaṅ-
goṇḍaśōla-maṇḍalam. He gave 30 cows and a bull to provide for this
which were accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who had agreed to
burn the sandhi lamp accordingly.

l.ta:
ṟ kirttikkumel koccaṭapanmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntara-
pāṇṭiyate[va]rkku yāṇṭu

miyum tiṅkaḷkkiḻamaiyum peṟṟakārttikai
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattuk kaḷattūṟ-

koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya
nāyaṉārkku immaṇṭalattu cempūṟkoṭṭattu(m)

maṇanallūrāṉa viracoḻaccatuṟuvetimaṅkalattu karaṇattāṉ ciṟṟeṟiuṭaiyāṉ
tulaiyātacelvaṉāṉa cittirameḻiveḷāṉ

vaitta tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukkum tirukkuttuviḷakku oṉṟukkum ittirununtā-
viḷakku oṉṟukku viṭṭa pacu muppatu iṣapam oṉṟum

ikkoyil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarom kaikkoṇṭu ittirununtāviḷakku
oṉṟum cantirātittavarai erippatāka cilāle-
kai paṇṇikkuṭuttom ikkoyil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇarom itu

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 303 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva (I): Year 7, Mīṉa
(Paṅguṉi)-śu. 10, Sunday, Hastam = 1257 A.D., Feb. 25. But the nakshatra
was Punarvasu.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same temple of the
same place occurring in No. 320, above, by Vikkirama-śōḻadēvar alias (name
lost), a mudali of the king. The gift of a (cow or buffalo) and a heifer to
provide for this by the donor, was accepted by the Śivabrāhmaṇas belonging
to the 44 circles of the temple who had agreed to burn the lamp.

l.ta: oṉṟum kiṭāri oṉṟukkum innāyaṉār koyil nāṟpattunālu
vaṭṭattu kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmmaṇarom kaikkoṇṭu ic-
cantiviḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittavarai erikka kaṭavomākac cilāle-
kai paṇṇikkuṭuttom ivvaṉaivom itu śrīmākecura ra

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged. It records the gift of land, etc., in the village
of [Ip]puram a dēvadāna of the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār of
Tirukkachchūr but owned by Maṇaṟkuḍaiyāṉ Dēvagadēvaṉ alias Vayirādarāyaṉ
for worship and offering to the deity Dēvar-Ādīśuramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of [Ip]-
puram. The gift was made by the trustee (tānattār) of the temple of Tiruvālak-
kōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi muṉṉāka koṟcaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravatti-
kaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiyatevarkku

vatu tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉār tevatāṉam
maṇaṟkuṭaiyāṉ tevaka-

tevaṉāṉa vayirātarāyaṉ kāṇiyāṉa [ip]purattu uṭaiyār tevarāticuramuṭaiya
nāyaṉāṟku pūcaikkum amutupaṭikkum te-

••• ••• •••• ṉṉāḷil tāṉakkaṭaiya •••• kuṭuttu ••
• [va]ṉamum paṇṇiṉa nila ••••• oru ••• kkum nāṅkaḷ

••• •••• āyamum uḷḷiṭṭa aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum [u]ṭaiyār tevarāti-
curamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkke iṟai iṟuppatāka viṭṭo ••••
kkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉār koyiṟ tāṉattom itu śrīmākecu

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 305 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Sundarapāṇḍayadēva (I): Year 8, Mithuna (Āṉi)-ba.
(tithi omitted). Monday, Uttiraṭṭādi = 1258 A.D., June 24. The tithi was
Saptami.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of a nandā lamp placed over a brass figure
of a women (pāvai-viḷakku), to the same temple of Tirukkachchūr mentioned
in No. 320 above, by a woman Uṟṟānāchchi, the daughter of Bhōgamāttār,
a dēvaraḍiyār of the temple. It is stated that including a cow previously given
by her, she gave 30 cows and a bull to the temple to provide for the lamp
and these were accepted by two groups of people one group consisting of the
maṉṟāḍis Vaḍugakōṉ's son Tāḻi and his younger brother Atti and the other
group consisting of Puṅgakōṉ's son Maṇiyaṉ and his younger brother Puṅgaṉ,
each group undertaking to be responsible for half of the lamp and both
groups together agreeing to measure out daily one uḻakku of ghee, by the
measure called Irāśakēsari, till eternity.

l.ta:
tiruvāykkeḻvi ••• ••• ••• [• koccaṭaipaṉ]marāṉa tiri
puvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntarapāṇṭiya tevark-

mituṉa nāyaṟṟu aparapakṣattu tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttiraṭ-
ṭāti nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭala-

ttuk kaḷattūrkoṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālak-
koyiluṭaiya nāyaṉār-

kku iṉṉāyaṉār koyiṟ tevaraṭiyāril pokamāttār makaḷ uṟṟānācci iṭṭa
peṇṇurupamāka ceyta pitta-

ḷai pāvaimel vaitta tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukku nāṉ muṉṉāḷil viṭṭa
pacu uḷpaṭa viṭṭa pacu muppatum iṣapa-

m oṉṟum kaikkoṇṭa ivvūr maṉṟāṭikaḷil vaṭukakkoṉ makaṉ tāḻiyum
ivaṉtampi attiyum kaikko-

ṇṭa viḷakku araiyum puṅkakkoṉ makaṉ maṇiyaṉum ivaṉtampi puṅkaṉum
kaikkoṇṭa viḷakku

araiyum āka tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukku nāḷ oṉṟukkum aḷakku irācakecari
yāl ney uḻakkum cantirā-

tittavaṟ ••• ••••• māka cammatittu cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom
••• ••• itu śrī

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº [Sundara]-pāṇḍyadēva (II): Year 2 Mithuna (Āṉī) śu. 3, Sunday,
Pūśam = 1277 A.D., June 6.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of which (2/3) was made by Kuḷattuḻāṉ Tulaiyā-
daśelvaṉ Māriyappiḷḷai alias Śiṉattaraiyar and (1/3) was made by his younger
brother Aṟamāṇḍai, of two villages called Oṭṭaṅgāḍu and Śōṟaṉtiruttu, purchased
by the above-mentioned persons from the ūr of Perumpēḍu in Māgaṇūr-nāḍu in
Śeṅgāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam. The gift-villages, from which the previously granted
dēvadāna and tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam lands were excluded, were granted as dēvadāna to
the temple of the god Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Tirukkachchūr. It is
stated that from the date of the inscription the incomes, etc., from these
villages were to be given to the temple trustees and this was probably
attested to by Pañchanadivāṇaṉ Aruṇagirip-perumāḷ Nīlagaṅgaraiyaṉ.

l.ta:
yum peṟṟa pūcattunāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu puliyūrkoṭṭattuc
cāttūr-

nāṭṭu tarmapurattu kuḷattuḻāṉ tulaiyāta celvaṉ māriyappiḷḷaiyāṉa ciṉatta-
raiyar mu-

ṉṟu kūṟiṭṭa irukūṟum ivar tampi aṟamāṇṭai oru kūṟumāka ceṅkāṭṭuk
koṭṭattu mā-
kaṇūr nāṭṭup perumpeṭṭu ūravar pakkal vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya oṭṭaṅkāṭu coṟa-

ṉtiruttu uḷpaṭa nāṟpāṟkellaikku uḷpaṭṭa viḷainilattilum kollai nilat-
tilum

tūṇi nilattevatāṉam tiruviṭaiyāṭṭam nilam nikki ellātaram tirukkaccūr
uṭai-
yār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkkut tevatāṉamāka iṭṭom pa-
ñcanativāṇaṉ aruṇakirip perumāḷ nilakaṅkarayaṉeṉ iṉṉāḷ mutal
ikkoyil tāṉattārkku eṟakaṭamai kuṭimai pokkaṟuppate ippaṭikku ivai

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 307 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same Prākāra.

This inscription records that on deciding to levy the tax of āṭṭiṟaippāṭṭam
from the maṉṟāḍis of (the district) of Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu, it was fixed by the
Government that they should pay the grazing charges at the rate of one paṇam
for every 10 cows, one paṇam for every 5 buffaloes and one paṇam for every 50
sheep. It was also decided that the total tax amounts should be collected
every month without fail, and that new families (of maṉṟāḍis) agreeing to pay
this tax would be permitted. The signatory was Sundarapāṇḍya Kaṇḍaya-
dēvaṉ.

l.ta:
paṟṟil maṉṟāṭikaḷai āṭṭiṟaippāṭṭakkaṭamai koḷḷa niccayittapaṭi payirkaṇṭu
kaṭamaikoḷḷum iṭa-

ttu vaḷaikkaṉṟuṅkuṭṭiyum meya āṇṭu oṉṟukku pattu māṭṭukku oru paṇamum
añcu erumaikku oru paṇamu[m*]

aimpatu āṭṭukku oru paṇamum āka vanta paṇam mātaviḻukkāṭṭile koḷḷak
kaṭavatākavum ippaṭikku pukutu-

ṅ kuṭiyum pukutaviṭuvatākac coṉṉom ivai cuntarapāṇṭiya kaṇṭayatevaṉ
eḻuttu itu ulakkiṉa i-
vaṉ keṅkai karaikkurāṟ pacuk koṉṟāṉ pāvaṅ koḷvāṉ [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva I: Year 17, Śiṅga
(Āvaṇi) śu. (the tithi lost), Monday, Uttirāḍam = 1268 A.D., August 20.
The tithi was śu. 11

inscription.p:
The beginning of this inscription seems to be built in. It records a gift
of a sandhi lamp to the temple of the god Tirukkachchūr occurring in several
records above, by the merchant Kāḍuvēṭṭi Nār[pat]teṇṇāyirachchilai ••• •••• veṇ-
vāyiluḍaiyāṉ, resident of the street of Bhuvaṉamuḻupperundern in Śrī-Madhu-
rāntaka-chchatuṟuvēdimaṅgalam. It is stated that he gave 3 cows to provide
for this lamp. It is further stated that 1(1/2) cows were accepted by the Śivabrāh-
maṇa Mudaliyāṉ Tirunaṭṭa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-[gōtra], who had right of
worship in the temple and the other 1(1/2) cows by Agattiyaṉ Tiruvālakkōyiluḍai-
yāṉ alias Tiruvagatti-bhaṭṭan, who both had agreed to burn the lamp
perpetually.

l.ta:
••• tiruvāykeḻvimuṉṉāka kocaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīcuntara-
pāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu

••• miyum tiṅkaḷkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa uttirāṭattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa-
maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrk koṭṭatuc ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭut tirukkaccūr uṭaiyā-

••• lakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉārkku śrīmaturāntakaccatuṟuvetimaṅkalattu puvaṉa
muḻutupperunteruvil viyāpāri kāṭuveṭṭi nāṟ[pat*]teṇṇāyiraccileya-

••• veṇvāyiluṭaiyāṉ vaitta canti viḷakku oṉṟukku viṭṭa pacu muṉ-
ṟukku iṉṉāyaṉāṟkoyil kāṇi uṭaiya civappirāmaṇaṉ pārattuvāci mutaliyāṉ
tirunaṭṭappaṭṭa

••• koṇṭa pacu oṉṟaraiyum akattiyaṉ tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiyāṉāṉa tiru-
vakattipaṭṭaṉ kaikkoṇṭa pacu oṉṟaraiyum āka pacu muṉṟum kaikkoṇṭu

••• ti viḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittavarai erippatākac cilālekai paṇṇik-
kuṭuttom ivviruvom itu śrīmahecu

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 309 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Māṟavarman Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva I (?): Year 19, 1234-35 A.D. (?).

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of the village Nariyaṉpākkam belonging to
Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu by the nāḍu (inhabitans) of the paṟṟu as maṭhappuṟa-
iṟaiyili to Mudaliyār Perumāḷ-Tādar. The signatory, by order, was Chittira
mēḻivēḷāṉ.

l.ta:
nariyaṉpākkam mutaliyār perumāḷ tātarkku maṭappuṟa iṟaiiliyākak kuṭuttom
ivvūrkku varum kaṭamai poṉvari nāṭṭuvari

paḻavari putuvari maṟṟum eppeṟ[pa]ṭṭa upātikaḷum iḻantu maṭappuṟa iṟaiiliyākak
kuṭuttom iraṇṭāyiravelippaṟṟil

nāṭṭavarom inta iṟaiyilikku ilaṅkaṇañ coṉnār uṇṭākil keṅkaik-
karaiyil kurāṉpacuk koṉ-
ṟāṉ pāvaṅkoḷvāṉ ippaṭikku ivai paṇiyāl cittirameḻi veḷāṉ eḻuttu

ivūril eṟumpuṟakkal uṉaittaṟi iṟai taṭṭārppāṭṭam māvaṭai maravaṭai erimiṉ
pāṭṭam veṭṭi ari mukkaittuṇṭam āya ivaiyum iḻantu maṭappuṟa iṟai-
ili-

yākac cantirātittavarai celvatākak kuṭuttom ivai paṇiyāl cittirameḻi
veḷāṉ eḻuttu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 310 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift, made by the king, of two vēli of land in
the nañjai land of Kuṟumbaṉmēḍu in Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu to the north of
[Tirukkachchūr ?] to two priests in the temple of the deity Tiruvēgamba-
muḍaiyanāyaṉār at Kāñchīpuram viz., Kāñchai Anavaratasundara-bhaṭṭaṉ
Nallanāyaṉ of Kāśyapa-(gōtra) and Kāñchīkkuṟi Śrikaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ
Perumānakkaṉ Tiruchchiṟṟambala-bhaṭṭaṉ of Bhāradvāja-(gōtra).
The land was given as Bhaṭṭavṛitti-agaram (Bhaṭṭavṛitti-agrahāram) from the
date of this inscription. The details such as the extent of each piece of land
and its location in the different parts of the above mentioned Iraṇḍāyiravēlip-
paṟṟu, are also given. Besides the land, it is stated that the donees were given
each a house-site in the residential area. A certain Muṉaiyadaraiyaṉ figures
as the signatory.

l.ta: kku etirāmāṇṭai āṭi mātam tirumukappaṭi kā

purattu uṭaiyār tiruvekampa-
muṭaiya nāyaṉāraip pūjikkum

rumāṉakkaṉ tirucciṟṟampalamuṭaiyārkkum vaṭakkiliraṇṭāyiravelippaṟṟil kuṟumpaṉ
meṭṭil nañ-

nta kaṭamai āyam poṉvari nāṭṭuvari kācu kaṭamai paṭṭi kuṟṟam kāttikai
paccai maṟṟum epperpaṭṭa iṟai-

māka kuṭuttom inilattukku puṉṉiyamuṭaiyā-
ṉiṭṭataṭi uḻavarippaṭikku kāliyāṉa kaṇṇāṟṟi-
ṇṭaṉ kaṇṇāṟṟil maintaṉ kuṇṭil uḻappaṭa kku

m puṟakālil
kālukku kiḻai murukkaṉ kku
m itaṉ vaṭakiḻai paruttippulam teṟkaṭaiya kku

m ivarkaḷukku paṭṭavirutti akaramāka cantirātittavarai celva-
tāka cilālekai paṇṇikkuṭuttom i-
ppaṭi ceyvate ivai muṉaiyataraiyaṉ eḻuttu itu

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 311 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same Prākarā.

p.b:
Tribhº Vijayagaṇḍagōpāladēva: Year 28, Mithuna (Āṉi) ba. 5,
Wednesday = 1279 A.D., May 31.

inscription.p:
This inscription is built in at the beginning, so a few letters at the beginning
of each line are not available.

It records the sale of the village comprising the blocks of Oḍḍaṅgāḍu,
Śōṟaṉtiruttu and Āchāriyaṉtiruttu and forming a hamlet of Peruṁbēḍu in
Māgaṇūr-nāḍu in Śeṅgāṭṭuk-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by the
ūr of the same place, to two persons, one named Kuḷattuḻāṉ Mārippiḷḷai alias
Śīṉattaraiyar of Dharmapuram in Śurattūr-nāḍu in the kōṭṭam (name lost),
and the other named Aṟamaḷattār (Aṟamvaḷattār ?) of the same place. The
details of the boundaries are given. At the end a list of signatories representing
the ūr of Perumbēḍu, is also given.

l.note: Beginning of each line is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 312 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Viruppaṇa-uḍaiyār, son of Ariyaṇa-uḍaiyār: Dundubhi, Śiṅga
(Āvaṇi), śu. 5, Svāti, Wednesday = 1442 A.D., August 11.
The week day was Saturday.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the gift of food and all other upādhis due to them
from the temple, made by the maṉṟāḍis and the Śamaiyak-kaṇakkaṉ of Iraṇḍā-
yiravēlippaṟṟu, as sarvamānya-iṟaiyili to two families, one headed by Aḻagaṉ
alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyirakkōṉ and the other headed by his younger brother
Kaṇṇāḷaṉ employed as tirunundā-viḷakku kuḍi in the temple of the deity
Tiruvālaikkōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār at Tirukkachchūr in Śeṅguṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷat-
tūrkōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. These two families were
enjoined to look after the service of tirunundā-viḷakku in the temple, heridi-
tarily. The signatory was Vaṇḍuvarāpati, who was the Śamaiyakkaṇakkaṉ.

l.ta:
ariyaṇa uṭaiyār kumāra śrīviruppaṇa uṭaiyarkkuc cellā
niṉṟa tuntupi varuṣattu

peṟṟa putaṉkiḻamai nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūr koṭṭattu
ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu tirukkaccūr uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya

nāyaṉārkku tirununtā viḷakkuc cāvāmuvāk kuṭi āka iraṇṭāyiravelippaṟṟu
maṉṟāṭikaḷum camaiyakkaṇakkaṉum inta varuṣam mutal nāṅkaḷ kuṭutta aḻakaṉ āṉa

nāṟpatteṇṇāyirakkoṉum ivantampi kaṇṇāḷaṉaiyum tirununtāviḷakku kuṭi
āka yintavaruṣam mutal cātam maṟṟum [nā]ṅkaḷ koḷḷum upātikaḷ uṭpaṭa
caṟuvamā-

ṉiya iṟaiyili āka kuṭuttom ippaṭi cantirātittavaraiyum naṭatta kuṭuttom
ippaṭikku ivai nāṭil maṉṟāṭikaḷ eḻuttu ivai vaṇṭuvarāpatic camai-

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 313 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Vīra Viruppaṇa-uḍaiyār: Raktākshi, Mīṉa (Paṅguṉi) śu. 6, Friday,
Anusham = 1445 A.D., February 26, f.d.t. 66, f.d.n. 77.

inscription.p:
The inscription records a gift of the food and the upādhis due to them
from the temple, as the gift of the nāḍu made by the maṉṟāḍis of the nāḍu
of Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu, to two families one headed by Kambaṉ, son of Kōbat-
tarāyaṉ and the other headed by Periyamudali, son of Āḷappiṟandāṉ-kōṉ, who
were employed to attend to the service connected with the nandā lamps and
that connected with the taking out in procession the image of the deity of
the temple in the same Tirukkachūr mentioned in No. 330 above. These
families should render these services heriditarily. The same Vaṇḍuvarāpati,
the Śamaiyakkaṇakkaṉ who figures in No. 330 above, also figures here, as the
signatory.

l.ta:
yum peṟṟa aṉuṣattu nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa-
maṇṭalattuk kaḷattūrkkoṭṭattu-

maṉṟāṭikaḷom nāṭṭu upaiyam ākat tirununtāviḷakkukkum cipātam tāṅkavum
cākāmuvākkuṭi āka nāṅkaḷ

kopattarāyaṉ makaṉ kampaṇaiyum āḷappiṟantāṉ koṉ makaṉperiya mutaliyai
yum ivaruṣam

mutal cātamum nāṅkaḷ koḷḷum upātikaḷum uṭpaṭac caṟuvamāṉiya iṟaiyili
yākat tāraivāttuk kuṭuttom ippaṭikkuc cantirātittavaraiyum na-
ṭakka kuṭuttom ippaṭikku ivai vaṇṭuvarāpatic camaiyakkaṇakkaṉ eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 314 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is interesting as it refers to the gifts of lamp made during
the reign-periods of three or four earlier kings.

It states that an extent of 525 kuḻi of land and a well which were granted
formerly for burning a nandā lamp in the temple of Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiya-
nāyaṉār of Tirukkachchūr by a certain Kirandai Amarkaṇāyakaṉ Kākkunā-
yakar and his younger brother Tiruvālakkōyiluḍaiyaṉ in the 20th year of the
reign of Kulōttuṅgaśōḻadēva (see No. 291 above), were sold back to Kirandai
Arumaṇināyakar, one of the sons of the above-mentioned donor, for a sum of
12 Paḻaṅkāśu, 1260 Naṟkāśu and 32 paṇam, by the following Śivabrāhmanas
in the manner detailed below. Tillaikkūtta-bhaṭṭaṉ received 5 Paḻaṅkāśu in
the 38th year of Tribhuvaṉavīradēva for (1/4) lamp, Śrikaṇṭha-bhaṭṭaṉ and others
received 7 Paḻaṅkāśu in the 15th year of Irāśarāśadēva for (1/4) lamp, Dēvargaḷ-
Dēvaṉ Sundaraṉ alias Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ received 1,260 Naṟkāśu in
the 27th year of Irāśarāśadēvar for 1/4 lamp, Sundara-bhaṭṭaṉ Mādēva-bhaṭṭaṉ
and others received 12 paṇam in the 7th year of Sundarapāṇḍyadēvar for [. .]
lamp, Tiruchchiṟṟambala-bhaṭṭaṉ Bhīmaṉ alias Panmākēśura-bhaṭṭaṉ received
11 paṇam, for 1 mā araiyē-araikkāṇi mumdirigai lamp, Tiruvagatti-bhaṭṭaṉ
received 3 paṇam for 1/2 lamp, Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira-bhaṭṭaṉ and others received 3
paṇam for kāṇi-araikkāṇi mundrigai lamp, and Sādāśiva-bhaṭṭaṉ and others
received 3 paṇam for kāṇiaraikkāṇi lamp. It is stated that all these Śivabrāh-
maṇas had agreed to burn the nandā lamp accordingly.

It is interesting to note that the record of the 20th year of Kulōttuṅga-
śōḻadēva containing the original grant of land, is stated to be engraved on the
northern side of the maṇḍapa which it described as pā-kal maṇḍapa (i.e. one,
built of larger dressed stone slabs).

l.ta:
vatu nāḷil ivvūr
kirantai amarkaṇāyakaṉ kākkunāyakarum ivar tampi tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya

araiyum nāṟpatteṇṇāyirapaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār paṇam

kkāṇi muntirikaiyum catācivapaṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār paṇam 3kkuk kaikkoṇṭa
viḷakku kāṇi araikkāṇiyum ākap paḻaṅkācu 12kkum naṟkācu 1260-

m kiṇaṟum ivarkku vi[lai]kkuṟa
viṟṟup poruḷaṟak kaikkoṇṭu kāṇiyum viṟṟuk kuṭuttu tirumalaiyile
cilālekai paṇṇi-

kkuṭuppomāka nāṅkaḷ paṇṇikkuṭutta cātaṉappaṭikku eṅkaḷperile tirivu iṭṭu-
kkoṇṭu tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟum cantirā[titta*]varaikkuṅ koḷvatāka
upai-

yamāka kaikkoṇṭu cilālekai paṇṇik kuṭuttom [civa]ppirāmaṇar ivvaṉai-
vom itu śrīmā

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 315 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva: Year 13, Kumbha (Māśi)
śu. 5, Wednesday, Aśvati = 1263 A.D., February 14th. The nakshatra
ended on the previous day at ·98.

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the atrocities committed by certain Brāhmaṇar
of Uttipākkam alias Ādiṉāyaka-chchaturvēdimaṅgalam in Tamuḻa-nāḍu in
Āmūṟ-kōṭṭam in Iraṇḍāyiravēlippaṟṟu and to the punishment meted out to
them on the request of the residents of the Agara-brahmadēśa, the Vēlān-ūrs
and the Nagaras of the same paṟṟu. It is stated that the rebellious people
could not be controlled by Vikkiramaśōḻadēva Iruṅgōḷāśaniyaṉ alias Vāṇarāyar,
the previous district authority, and others and that this could be achieved
only by another officer named Piḷḷai Pottappirāyar, who after arresting the
criminals and putting them to jail, after punishing them in the manner
employed for lower caste people, ordered the confiscation of their properties
and selling them to temples, etc. Their property in Uttipākkam was thus sold
as tirunāmattukkāṇi of the temples of the place, Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya-
nāyaṉār and Śrīkayilāyamuḍaiya-nāyaṉar for 200 paṇam and this money was
deposited in the temple at Tirukkachchiūr. The sale was effected by the
residents of Agaram, Vēḷān ūr and Nagaras mentioned above, whose represen-
tatives figure as signatories.

l.ta:
śrīmaṟkiṟtikkumel śrīkoccaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ
śrīcuṉtarapāṇṭiya tevarkku yāṇṭu

ṅkaḷil uḷḷārum veḷāṉ ūrkaḷil uḷḷārum
nakara-

ṅkaḷil uḷḷārum iṉṉāṭṭu āmuṟkoṭṭattu tamuḻaṉāṭṭu uttipākkam āṉa
ātiṉāyakaccatu

timaṅkalattu uṭaiyār tiruvakatticuramuṭaiya ṉāyaṉār koyil
tāṉattāṟku

ṇarum
veḷāḷarum va[r*]ttikkum muṟaimaikaḷ oḻiya kiḻcātikaḷ ceyyum muṟaimai
kaḷile āytaṅkaḷ kaṭṭiyum

ṇikaḷait tūṣittum kaḷavukaṇṭum
kaṉṟukālkaḷai aḻittu viṟṟum ceytu poṉtapaṭikaḷāle muṉpu ṉāṭu ceyta
vikkiramacoḻa tevarāna iru-

ṅkoḷācaniyaṉ vāṇarāyar tirumalaitantār āṉa muṉaiyataraiya uḷḷiṭṭa mutali
kaḷ palarum ivarkaḷaip piṇaittum aṭittum teṇṭaṅkoṇṭum viṭukaḷ
iṭittum vaṇṇārpa ciṟaiiṭṭum

ceyta iṭattum ivarkaḷai aṟacceytu paṭātapaṭiyālum piṉpum ivarkaḷ kkuṇa-
kanaṅkaḷ •• tapaṭiyālum piḷḷai pottappiyarāyarā • oṭukkakkaṭavatāy
eḻuntiruppāṉata aḷavile iṉ-

ṉāṭṭil akara jaṉaṅkaḷum nāṭṭavarum ivarkaḷ ceytikaḷum viṇṇappañceytu
itaṉaic cātittu taraveṇumeṉṟu viṇṇappañceyta aḷavile piḷḷaipottap
piyarāyarum ivarkaḷaik ka[ṭṭik]koṇ-

ṭu varuvatāka vaḷḷuvanāṭāḷvān iruṅkoḷarum [aṭaippal]viṭai iṭṭa malaiyāḷa
raip pokaviṭṭa aḷavil poṉavarkaḷai veṭṭiyum kuttiyum ampiṭṭeytum
āytam paṟittum malaiyile eṟinavaṉ • le piṉ-

piṭittu āṭkoṇṭa vilviyaiyum pāmpa(na)ṇaiyānaiyum kaikkoṇṭuvantu
tirukkaccūr taṇṭalile [pu]vaṇattu vaittu ivarkaḷaiyum kāṭṭile palaciṟai-
kaḷaiyum kaṇṇattūrāṟ ulakuṭaiyaperumāḷ cipāta-

(ta)t teṟkkoṇṭu pokacceyte vaṭapiṭākaiyil cāṉūr aracapākkattil kāṭ
ṭile ivarkaḷ tampimār āyttappiṉa muvarum maṉittaraik kūṭṭikkoṇṭu
vantu ivarkaḷaiyum ivarkaḷ

uṭaṉ pokiṟa palaciṟaikaḷaiyum veṭṭiviṭuvittukkoṇṭu poṉapaṭiyāle ivarkaḷ
ceyti tavira ulakuṭaiya perumāḷ śrīpātattile viṇṇappañceytu ivarkaḷ
irunta iṭaṅkaḷile k[ai]kkoṇṭu

kiḻcātikaḷait teṇṭikkum muṟaimaikaḷile teṇṭippittuk koḷḷakkaṭavo
mākavum ivar[ka*]ḷukku iṉṉāṭṭil maṉaikaḷuyum kāṇiyum uḷḷavai tiruk-
koyilkaḷukkum tirumuṟṟaṅkaḷukkum tirunāmattukkāṇi-

yāka viṟṟu viṟṟapaṇam ivarkaḷait teṇṭamākak karuvulakatte oṭukkuvatākavum
aṟuti iṭukaiyil ivarkaḷ kāṇiyāy uṭaiyār tiruvakti

kollaiyum nattamu u[ṭ*]ppaṭa[kko]ṇṭu ivar-

irunūṟu ippaṇam irunūṟum tirukkacci ūrkkaruvukalatte
oṭukkuvittuk koḷkaiyil ivarkaḷukku uttipākkattil uḷḷa

paṉamum tirukkoyil-
kaḷile cilaralekaiyum paṇṇikkoṇṭu cantirātitta varaiyum tirunāmattukkāṇi-
yāka aṉupavittukkoḷḷak-

caṅkaḷiḷā[rum*] ve(ḷa)ḷāṉ ūrkaḷil[ārum*] nakaraṅkaḷilārum iv-
vaṉaivom ippaṭikku ivaikuttanalluḻāṉ

eḻuttu ulakaṅkāttāneḻuttu u[kki]rakkaṇṇan eḻuttu kaḷappāḷaṉ eḻuttu
kīraṉ kaieḻuttu itu

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 316 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Detached stone built into the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the
Añjanākshi-ammaṉ shrine in the same temple.

p.b: Tribhº Tribhuvaṉa-chōḷadēva: Kulōttuṅga III: Year lost. C. 13th century.

inscription.p:
This inscription is fragmentary. It refers to the king as one ‘who was
pleased to take Madurai, Īḻam, Karuvūr and the crowned head of the Pāṇḍya
and to anoint the heroes and the victors’. It records the setting up of an image
of the Śaiva saint Tirunāvukkaraśu (i.e., Appar) in the temple of [Tiruvālak-
kōyiluḍaiya-nāyaṉār] of Tirukkachchūr alias Nittavinōdanallūr in Śeṅ-
guṉṟa-nāḍu in Kaḷattūr-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍaḷam, by Biramaṉ
alias Chittiramē[ḻi ••• •••], son of Marudiḍaṅgoṇḍār, a reciter of the sacred
hymns (Tiruppadiyam) in the temple of the deity Biramīśuramuḍaiya-
nāyanār of a chaturvēdimaṅgalam (name lost) in the same maṇḍalam.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• [ma]ṇṭalattuk kaḷattūr koṭṭattuc ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu
tirukkaccūrāṉa nittaviṉotanallūr

••• ••• •••• yanār koyilukku tirunāvukkaracutevarai eḻun-
taruḷuvittāṉ immaṇṭalattu

••• ••• •••• jaccatuppetimaṅkalattu uṭaiyār piramicuramuṭaiya
nāyanār koyilil palapa-

••• ••• •••• patiyam viṇṇappañ ceyvār marutiṭaṅkoṇṭār
makaṉ piramaṉāṉa cittirame-

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 317 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another detached stone built into the same wall.

p.b: Sundarapāṇḍyadēva (—Jatº Sundº Pāṇḍya I): No date = C. 13th century.

inscription.p:
This inscription is extremely fragmentary. It refers to the king as one
‘who was pleased to take every country’. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South and East walls of the Marundīśvara temple.

p.b:
Narasiṅgarāya: Śaka 1406 (mistake for 1411), Saumya, Ishaba (Vaikāsi)
śu. 15, Thursday = 1489 A.D., May 14.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the foundation of a new village called Marundukku-
ṉādayapuram, on the hill at Tirukkachchūr for the merit of the king and his
foremost servant (mudappāvāḍai) Ṉāgamanāyakkar. This was arranged by
the latter's agent Chōḍa-Timmanāyakkar, and the karaṇakkar Śilumaraśar.
It is stated that the house-sites in the place were allotted to various categories
of families on condition that from the month of Āḍi in the year quoted above,
for six months, they could enjoy the sites as sarvamānya (tax-free) and from
the next year onwards each of the Kaikkōḷar (weaver) and other families should
pay (1/4) paṇam as tax. It is stated that people of other religions should not
occupy the place. It further states that the Paḷḷa families are exempted from
the payment of the tax called vāśal-paṇam.

l.ta:
yar mutappāvāṭai ṉākamanāyakkar kāryattukku kaṭavar coṭattimmu
ṉāyakkarum karaṇakkar cilumaracarum nara

iṭṭu palapaṭṭaiyāna kuṭikaḷukku celā-
lekai paṇṇikkuṭuttapaṭi ivva[ru*]ṣam āṭimātam mutal āru mātavaraikkum

varuṣam mutalāka mātam oṉṟukku kaikkoḷakkum palapaṭṭaṭaikkuṭikaḷ carvakattuk
kuṭikkum kāl(p)paṇam koḷḷakkaṭavom ākavum immatattār oḻiya aṉṉiyar
maṭa[m] paṇṇikkoḷḷa

kaṭavomalla koṇṭavarkaḷ keṅkai karaiyil kārām pacuvaik koṉṟa
toṣattile po(ka*)kaṭavarkaḷākavum inta taṉmattukku irācataram naṭakku[m*]
per tānattārum maṟṟum ca • vikkarapperum

••• •••• koṇṭāl civatturoki ākakaṭavarkaḷ vantu eṟina paḷḷak
kuṭikku vācal paṇa[m*]

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 318-A of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South and East walls of the Marundīśvara temple.

This inscription is also fragmentary. It records a certain sarvamānya
(tax-free) gift to a temple (name lost) probably for the merit of Śāḷuva-nāyakkar
(name not preserved).

l.ta:
mārkaḻi [paḷukku] cāḷuvanāyakkar taṉmamāka eṟṟiṉi
ma •• tarakoyilukku mutalmai muntiṉa mutalmai mulamattiya kaṇṭa-

yiraṇṭām mutalamai kuḻaintār ceramāṉār nallāḷ periyavaṉ āṉaippiḷḷaiyār
ā[ṟā]m mutalmai [maramar] eḻām mutalamai koṉerimakaḷ nallāḷ

muntiṉa •• nallāḷ makaṉ[t] taḻuvakkuḻaittāṉ •• ṇṭāmaṉūṭaiya eḻuva[t
tarimā] colaiyār ••• ••• yiraṇṭum caṟuvamāṉiyam mutalmaikkum

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 319 of 1909.)
TIRUKKACHCHŪR, CHINGLEPUT TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the Vighnēśvara shrine near the tank, in the same village.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva I: Year 8, Ṛishabha
(Vaikāśi) śu. 3, Thursday, Pūśam = 1258 A.D., May 9, the tithi was śu 5.

inscription.p:
This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who was pleased to take every
country’.

It records a gift of 23 vēli of land by three persons, viz., Pañchanadi-
vāṇa Nīlagaṅgaraiyar (5 vēli), Nallanāyaṉār Nīlagaṅgaraiyar (8 vēli) and
Aruṇagiri Perumāḷ Nīlagaṅgaraiyar (10 vēli) as iṟaiyili tiruviḍaiyāṭṭam
land in Brahmakuḷattūr alias Vēṭṭaikkāṟaṉ Kuḷattūr to the temple of the
god Perumāḷ Nāṟpatteṇṇāyiraviṇṇagar Emberumāṉ of Tirukkachchūr. A
certain Tirumalaitaṉtāṉ Muṉaiyadaraiyaṉ figures as the signatory.

l.ta:
koṟcaṭapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ emmaṇṭa[la]muṅ koṇ-
ṭaruḷiya śrīcuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu

yaiyu[m*] viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa pūcattu
nāḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kaḷattūr koṭṭattu ceṅkuṉṟanāṭṭu tiru

kkaccūr perumāṉ nāṟpatteṇṇāyira viṇṇakar emperumāṉukku ūṟṟukāṭṭu
koṭṭattu

ku[ḷa]ttūrāṉa veṭṭaikkāṟaṉ kuḷaṟttūr nir nilattil
paṉṉiraṭi ko-

lāl uḷḷūr tevatāṉa nila oruveliyum tiruviṭaiyāṭṭa nilam oru veliyum
piṭāripaṭṭi kālum uṭaiyār tiruvālakkoyiluṭaiya nāyaṉār tevatāṉa viḷakku
paṭṭi uḷpaṭa nālaraiveliyum nikki

uḷḷa nilattil pañcanetivāṉa nilakaṅkaraiyaṉ viṭṭa nila aiñcuveliyum nalla
nāyaṉār nilakaṅkaraiyar viṭṭa nila eṭṭuveliyum aruṇakiri perumāḷ nilakaṅ
karaiyar viṭṭa nila pat-

tu veliyum ivūru piṭākaikaḷum nālpāṟkellaikku ••• ••• mum
kollai nilamumāṉa kuḻi •••• mum kaṭamai āyam poṉvari nāṭṭu vari
kācu kaṭamaipaṭṭi •• veṭṭi arimukkai kāttikaipaccai erimi-

ṉ pāṭṭam puṟkilaṉai ācuvikaḷ peṟ kaṭamai aṉaittāyamum nām iṟaiyili
tiruviṭaiyāṭṭamāka viṭṭom ikkoyil tāṉattāṟ keṟa pokkaṟuppate
tirumalai taṉtā-

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 320 of 1909.)
MADURAMAṄGALAM, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
On a stone built into the west wall of the Uḷḷaṅgaikuḷundanāyaṉār
temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 189.

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1909.)
MADURAMAṄGALAM, SRIPERUMBUDUR TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Stone built into the floor of the maṇḍapa in front of the Vaikuṇṭhaperumāḷ
temple.

This inscription is damaged. Mentions the sabhai of Madurama[ṅgalam]
and a certain [.]lōkamahārāyar.

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Tirupurāntakēśvara temple.
This inscription is built in the middle.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the temple of the god (name lost) of
Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr, by Vēśāli Mādāṇḍāṉ Pāriśēriyāṇḍāḷ (?)
who also endowed 10 paṇam for this purpose. This money was received by
a number of Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple having right of worship there, such
as Tavarāśa-bhaṭṭaṉ, his sons [Poṉ]ṉambalakkūtta-bhaṭṭaṉ and Tāḻi-bhaṭṭaṉ
and Tiruvallanāyaka-bhaṭṭaṉ, Ulagāḷuḍaiyāṉ[bhaṭṭaṉ], and Poṟkōyil Nambi
Śōmaśrīdēvaṉ, all of whom had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvicaiyaka ••••• varkku yāṇṭu
añcāvatu kūvamāṉa tiyākacamuttiranallūr āḷuṭaiya ••• ••• ṉārkku
ivvūr vecāli mātāṇṭāṉ pāriceriyāṇṭāḷ vaitta vai

cantiviḷakku oṉṟukku paṇam pattu ippaṇa ••• ••• [k]oyil kāṇi uṭaiya
civappirāmaṇaṉ tavarācapaṭṭaṉ piḷḷai ••••• ṉampalakkūttapaṭṭaṉ tāḻip-
paṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭārum tiru-

vallanāyakapaṭṭaṉ ulakāḷuṭaiyāṉ •••• poṟkoyilnampi coma śrītevaṉum
ivvaṉaivom ippaṇam •••• tirātittavarai iccantiviḷakku erikkakkaṭa-
vom iv-

vaṉaivom ippaṭikku ivai tavarācapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai ••••
paṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai poṉṉampalakūt •••• ippaṭikku ivai
tiruvallanāyakapaṭṭaṉ ulakāḷuṭaiyāṉ pa ••• poṟkoyil nampi coma • vaṉ
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Rājādhirājadēva II: Year lost: C. 12th century.

inscription.p: This inscription is too fragmentary.

It refers to the temple of the god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyanāyaṉār (i.e.,
Pāśupatēśvara) and its sthānattār as well as the ūr.

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Rājakēsarivarman Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva II: No date:
C. 12th century.

inscription.p: This inscription is incomplete.

It commences with the praśasti of the king, Pūmēvu vaḷar tiruppoṉmār-
vu puṇar, etc. Mentions the queen Bhuvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ, and stops.

l.ta:
pūmevuvaḷar tiruppoṉmārvu puṇar nāmevu kalaimakaḷ nalam peritu
ciṟappa vicaiya māmakaḷ velpuyattiruppa icaiyiṉ celvi eṇṭicaiviḷaṅka nirupar
vantiṟaiñca niṉilamaṭantaiyait tirumaṇam puṇa-

rntu cirvaḷar tarumam aṇimuṭi kavitteṉa maṇi[mu]ṭicūṭi mallaiñālattup pal-
luyiṟkellā mellaiyiliṉpamiyal vinileyt v[e*]ṇkuṭai niḻaṟ ceṅko-
locci vāḻipallūḻiyāḻi naṭappa cempoṉ vi-

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Chakravartin Śrī-Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva I:
Year lost: C. 1090 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription is built in the middle. Its date portion is lost, but the
date must be after the 20th year as this year is quoted in the text.

It starts with the praśasti of the king, Pugaḻśūḻnda puṇari, etc. It states
that some land, probably in Rājarājanallūr, which was gifted as iṟaiyili by
purchase from Veḷippākiḻāṉ Dēvaṉ Śāttan alias Rājēndrapallavaraiyaṉ, was
not in use since the 20th year of the king owing to the disappearance and death
of certain Perumbākkiḻāṉ Aḍigaḷ Vēṅkaḍam. Therefore, some of his lands,
totalling in extent to 1339 kuḻi and 4(1/2) mā. were gifted to provide for offerings
to the temple probably of Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār.

l.ta:
pukaḻcūḻnta puṇari akaḻcūḻnta puviyiṟ poṉṉemiyaḷavun taṉ-
ṉemi naṭappa viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai iḷaṅkopparuvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramat
toḻilāṟ putumaṇam puṇar

maṇinākaṉuṭaiya ceppukkuṟaikkum neṭuñcerikkuṭaiyāṉ arayaṉ tirumukko-
ṇaṉ ceppukkuṟaikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapārkkellai uṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiyār

munṉūṟṟemuppattoṉpate ṉān māvaraimu[n*]tirikai nilamum it ••••

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same shrine.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with
Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc.

It records a gift of nañjai land and fishing taxes to provide for repairs
and drudging of the tank called Tirubhuvanamādēvippērēri of Kūvam alias
Madhurāntakanallūr in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅ-
goṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by the ūrār of the village. A number of signatories figure
in the record.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅkac cayamātu virumpa nilamaka[ḷ*] ṉilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same Wall.

p.b:
Veṅkaṭapatirāya of Āṉaigondi: Bahudhānya, Tai 3 = 1578 A.D.,
December 31 (Wednesday).

inscription.p:
It records a gift of 100 kuḻi of land in Tiṟibhuvaṉamādēvi-viḷāgam in
Kūvam, as guru-pāpattu (homage to the guru) to Śālai Appar of Elumaṭṭi by
a certain Ṉāraṇappanāyakar. The gift is stated to be for the merit of the
ūravar.

l.ta: ṉāḷil cavāmaṉāṟaṇappa ṉāyakkar elumaṭṭiyil kuruvāvattākac cālai
apparukku viṭṭa caṟuvamāṉiyam kūvattil tiṟipuvaṉamātevi viḷākattil
yinta kuḻi ṉūṟum ūravarukkup puṇṇiyamāka kaṭṭaḷaiyiṭṭom yintapaṭi

yinta māṉiyattukku vikātam paṇṇiṉavarkaḷ keṅkaikkaraiyil kārāmpacuvaik
ko-

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North and west walls of the same shrine.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with tiru-
mādu puviyeṉṉum, etc.

It records a sale of land in Kōṭṭūr alias Chōḻavichchādirach-chaturvēdi-
maṅgalam in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam
by the Mahāsabhai of the place for a sum of 160 kāśu. The land was intended
for digging through it a feeder-channel to the Tiribuvanamādēvipperiyēri of
Kūvam alias Madhurāntaka-nallūr in the same nāḍu, kōṭṭam and maṇḍalam.
It contains a detailed description of the manner and course in which the channel
should be dug.

veṇkuṭai uyarttit tiṇkalipeyarttu tanciṟutā[t]aiyākiya eṟivalik kaṅkai

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same shrine.

p.b:
Tribhº Kulōttuṅga-chōḷadēva III: Year 8, Aippaśi =
1185-86 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Puyal-
vāyppa vaḷamperuga, etc.

It records a gift of a nandā lamp to the temple of the god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍai-
yār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamuttiranallūr in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍa-
sōḻa-maṇḍalam by a certain Kuṟumuḍi Śaṅgaṉ Gōvindan alias Vāṉavaṉ
Mūvēndavēḷāṉ. It is stated that he endowed for this purpose, 16
Aṉṟāḍu naṟpaḻaṅkāśu which was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the
temple viz., Śennaṟpeṟṟāṉ Mahādēva-bhaṭṭan of Kaviśika (Kauśika)-[gōtra],
Uḍaiyapiḷḷai alias Tavarāśarāśa-bhaṭṭaṉ of Gavutama-[gōtra], his younger
brothers, Tiruvallamuḍaiyāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ of Gavutama-[gōtra], Dēvar alias
Poṟkōyilnambi of Kāśyapa-[gōtra], and his younger brothers, who had
agreed to carry out the object of the endowment perpetually.

l.ta: puyal vāyppa vaḷam perukap poyyāta nānmaṟaiyin cayāl(v)

vāypat tirumakaḷuñ ceyamakaḷuñ ciṟantu vāḻa veṇmatipol kuṭaiviḷaṅka vel
ventaraṭi paṇiya maṇmaṭantai manaṅkaḷippa maṉuniti taḻaittoṅkac ca-

kkaramuñ ceṅkoluñ tikkaṉaittuñ celanaṭappak kaṟpakālam puvi[pākka]p poṟ-
pamainta muṭi pūṉaintu cempoṉ vira

āvatu appaci mātattu
ceyaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattu kūvamāṉa tiyākacamuttira-
nallūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiyārkku kuṟumuṭi caṅkaṉ kovintanā-

na vāṉavaṉ muventaveḷāṉ vaikkiṟa tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟukku ikkoyil
civappirāmaṇaril kavucikaṉ cenneṟpeṟṟāṉ mahā

paṉ tevarāṉa poṟkoyil nampiyum tampimārum ivvaṉaivom
ikkuṟumuṭi caṅkaṉ kovintaṉāṉa vāṉavaṉ muventaveḷān pakkal eṭṭāvatu
aippaci mā

aṉṟāṭu naṟpaḻaṅkācu patināṟu ikkācu patināṟum eṅkaḷ kāṇipotiyile kaik-
koṇṭu ittirununtāviḷakku oṉṟum cantirātittavaṟ celuttakkaṭavomāka
nām ivvaṉaivom ippaṭikkumivai kavucikan cenneṟpeṟṟān-

vutaman uṭaiyapiḷḷaiyāna tavarāca-
rācapaṭṭan eḻuttu § ippaṭikku [yi]vai

vutaman tiruvallamuṭaiyān paṭṭan
eḻuttu § ippaṭikku ivai kā

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with
Pugaḻśūḻnda puṇari, etc.

It records a gift of land in Kūvam alias Madhurāntakanallūr in Kanṟūr-
nāḍu in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅkoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by the mahā-sabhai of the
place, purchased from money in gold received from Śēkkiḻān Śelvan Chandira-
śēkharan alias Śakalavichchādira Viḻupparaiyan of Tīṅgāḍupākkam alias
Śōḻadivākaranallūr in Nīrvāḷūr-nāḍu in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭk-kōṭṭam to the image of the
god Vṛishabhavāhanadēvar in the temple of the god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiya-
mādēvar of Kūvam mentioned above. Among the pieces of land, mention is
made of one which belonged to a certain Kumāran Śivakkoḻundu Ēṟan who is
stated to have paid the tax on it from the king's ninth year to the 41st year.
The gift was intended to provide for worship, offerings, festivals and processions
of the image of the deity, which are detailed at length. The ūravar and the
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple at Kūvam had accepted the gift-land and had
agreed to carry out the services accordingly. Also records a gift of poṉ received
by the Śivabrāhmaṇas for burning a nandā lamp in the temple. A number
of representatives of the above-mentioned groups figure as signatories.

l.ta:
nnemi
naṭātti viḷaṅku jayamakaḷai iḷaṅkopparuvattu cakkarakoṭṭattu vikkiramattoḻi-
lāṟ putumaṇam puṇar

ḷamiriya vāḷuṟai kaḻittut toḷvali kāṭṭip porpari naṭāttik

potumaiyum poṉṉiyāṭaiyu naṉṉilappāvaiyu

r
cūṭa muṉṉai maṉuvāṟu perukakkaliyāṟu vaṟuppac ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ
cella veṇkuṭai yiru nilavaḷākam veṅkaṇu

ṉātu tirunila veṇṇilātikaḻa oru
tani meruviṟ puli viḷaiyāṭa vārkaṭaṟṟivā

rattup pūpālar tiṟai viṭutta kalañ-

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 331 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the same shrine.

This inscription records a gift of a śandhi lamp to the temple of the god
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr by Birāma
Āṇḍavaṉ of the same place. The gift was received by a number of
Śivabrāhmaṇas (names given) having right of worship in the temple, who had
agreed to burn the sandhi lamp in the temple accordingly, from the month of
Tai of the year quoted.

l.ta:
ttiranallūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiyārkku ivvūril irukkum pirāma
āṇṭavaṉ yika ko-

yilnampi comaṉātatevaṉum ivvaṉaivom ivvāṇṭait taimmātamutal oru
cantiviḷakku muṭṭāmal

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gobbūri Ōbarājayadēva-mahārāśayyaṉ: Śaka. 1532,
Sādhāraṇa, Mārgaḻi 1, Friday = 1610 A.D., November 30.

inscription.p:
This inscription records the assignment of providing for special daily
worship connected with the special festival of Tiruppaḷḷiyeḻuchchi during the
month of Mārgaḻi, of tha god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār. The assignment was
made by Kāñchī [. .]ppa-mudaliyār, the agent and the pārapattiyam of the
ruler, Guruvappar of Muḍippura-Uppalappāḍi, Venkiṭṭappar of Kaḍaikkūṭṭu-
Veṇumpākkam, Tiyagapiḷḷai of Tiruvoṟṟiyūr and the ūravar (local residents)
of Kūvam. It is stated that the rate for a day was probably 4 paṇam. The
allotment of days is given as follows: Tānattār I day; Madirāntaka Viḻup-
paraiyar Araśar and others 6 days; Udayādittadēvar and others 4 days;
Veṅgiyār and others 3 days; Toṇḍaimāns 3 days; Iruṅgaṇūr-kiḻavar 4 days;
Kumāra [. .] 4 days; Kaikōḷas 1 day, Kāval Perumāḷ-ṉāyakkar 1 day;
Ūrkkaṇakku (village accountant) 1 day; and ūṟp-podu (the local villagers)
2 days.

It is stated that this system should continue hereditarily by the families
of the above-mentioned groups and the decision was taken to this effect in
front of the shrine of the god of Tirukōvil-īśvaraṉ.
The Birmārāyar Tiruppichcharār-ṉāyiṉaṉ was the signatory.

l.ta:
ṟ cellāniṉṟa

cukkiravāra ṉāḷ śrīmaṉmakāmaṇṭelecuvara kop-

uṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭai •••••

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a nandā lamp to the god of the temple
of Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiya nāyaṉār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr and an
endowment of 30 Paḻaṅkāśu by Pichchaṉ Maṉṟulā Vēḷāṉ of Kuṟumuḍi.
Several Śivabrāhmaṇas whose names are specified had received the amount
and had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually in the temple.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvicaiyakaṇṭakopālatevarkku yāṇṭu
patiṉāṟāvatu kūvamāṉa tiyākacamuttiranallūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiya
nāyaṉārkku kuṟumuṭipiccaṉ maṉṟuḷā veḷāṉ pakkal ikkoyil civappirāma-
ṇaṉ

tamaṉ tiruvalla-
muṭaiyāṉ poṉṉapaṭṭaṉ makaṉ ulakāvuṭaiyāṉum tampimārum kācivaṉ
tevarāṉa poṟkoyil nampi comatevapaṭṭaṉum ivvāṇṭai āvaṇi māta-
mutal iṉṉāyaṉārkku tirunantā viḷakkukku nāṅkaḷ erikka kai-

kkoṇṭa paḻaṅkācu muppatu ippaḻaṅkācu muppatukkum oru tirunantāviḷakku
apo

rāttiram cantirātittavarai erippatākap poliyūṭṭākak kaikkoṇṭom
ivvaṉaivom ippaṭikku tavarācarācapaṭṭaṉ ceṉṉalpeṟṟā[ṉ*] eḻuttu

ip[paṭikku i]vai uṭaiyapiḷḷai makaṉ poṉṉampalakkūttapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu
ippaṭikku ivai tiruvallamuṭaiyā[ṉ*] poṉṉapaṭṭaṉ ulakāvuṭaiyāṉ paṭṭaṉ
eḻuttu ippaṭikku ivai poṟ(r)koyilnampi comate[vapaṭṭaṉ] eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 334 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Vīrarājēndra-chōḷadēva: Kulōttuṅga III: Year Lost: C. 12th-13th
century.

inscription.p:
This inscription is built in at the end, so the date and some other important
details are lost.

It records a gift of a nandā lamp to the temple of the god Tiruvi[ṟkōla-
muḍaiyanāyaṉār] of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam
in [Jaya]ṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by Attiyūrāḻvāṉ Āṇḍapirān of Gārggya-gōtra,
belonging to Kōṭṭūr in Ambar-nāḍu in the same maṇḍalam. It is stated that
he endowed some amount (details lost) of Anṟāḍu Naṟpaḻaṅkāśu for this purpose,
and that the amount was accepted by a number of Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple
who had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually from the month of Māśi of the
year quoted.

l.ta:
ṅkoṇṭa coḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattuk kūvamāna tiyākacamuttiranallūr
āḷuṭaiyār tiru vi ••• •••••

pirān paṭṭaṉ pakkal ikkoyilil civappirā ••• ••• ••••

vāṇṭai mācimā

yūrāḻvān āṇṭapirān paṭṭan pakkal poliyūṭṭāka nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa anṟāṭu
naṟpaḻaṅkācu ••• ••• ••••

pan tevarāna poṟkoyil
nampi eḻuttu ippaṭi aṟiven ikkoyil ••• ••••

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the temple of the god
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiya-nāyanār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr, by a woman
named Umaiyāḻvāṉ, wife of Paṇḍaraṅkiḻān, Tiruvaraṅgamuḍaiyān. This
gift was accepted by three people, whose names are given, who had agreed to
carry out the object of the gift.

l.ta: ya nāyanārkku paṇṭaraṅkiḻān tiruvaraṅkamuṭaiyān pāri umaiyāḻvāṉeṉ canti-

rāti[t*]tavarai erikkak kaṭavatāna cantiviḷakku oṉṟum erippatāka kai[k*]koṇ-

rom ippaṭikku ca[m*]matittu kai[k*]koṇṭom imuvarom ivai paḷḷaṉ
eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 336 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same Wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sum of 20 Paḻaṅkāśu for a nandā lamp
to the same temple of the same Kūvam occurring in No. 354 above, by Kachchik-
kaḍambaṉ Taḻuvakkuḻaindāṉ-uyyavandāṉ alias Śembiyaṉ Viḻupparaiyaṉ of
Paḻaiyaṉūr in Paḻaiyaṉūr-nāḍu in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam. It is stated that the
amount was accepted by a number of Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple (names
given) who had agreed to burn the lamp perpetually from the month of
Chittirai of the year quoted.

ippaṭikkivai kaucikaṉ ceṉṉeṟpeṟṟāṉ mahā
paṉ tevarāṉa poṟkoyil nampi eḻuttu itu ci

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 337 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

This inscription is damaged in some places. It records that the tānattār
of the temple of the village mentioned in No. 349 above instituted a service
called Kulaśēkharaṉ-sandhi in the temple and that the king was requested to
arrange to provide for this. Accordingly the king made a gift of a village (name
lost) as a tax-free dēvadāna from the month of Āṉi in the year quoted above.

l.ta:
uṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiyār koyil tāṉattāṉattār ikku iṉṉāyaṉārkku
namperāl [ka]-

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription contains a praśasti of the king commencing with ••• •••

It states that the residents of the four ūr (places) viz., Madhurāntakanallūr
in Kaṉṟūr-nāḍu in Maṇayiṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, Maṭṭai-nāḍu
in Śeṅgāṭṭu-nāḍu, Narasiṅgamaṅgalam, and Aśvadaraiyaṉ-kāraṇai, decided
to sell their lands in the 2nd year of the king, because of the fact that they
could not identify as to which belonged to whom. Then on the offer made by
the Śivabrāhmaṇa Ēṟaṉ Akkāḷi-bhaṭṭaṉ of Kāśyapa-gōtra of the temple of the
god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār of Madhurāntakanallūr to purchase the lands, they
were sold to him by the ūravars of the four places mentioned above. He re-
claimed the lands and made them cultivable. He later on, from the 20th
year of the king, gifted the lands to the temple mentioned above, to provide for
various services, worship, conduct of festivals, etc., therein.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the temple of the god
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiya-nāyanār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr in Maṇaviṟ-
kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Kākku-nāyakaṉ alias Teṉṉavaṉ
Viḻupparaiyaṉ Varantarum Perumāḷ, a Dēśi-vēḷāṉ of Vaigavūr in Ūṟṟukkāṭṭuk-
kōṭṭam in the same maṇḍalam. It is stated that he endowed a sum of 10 paṇam
which was received by four Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple, viz. Araśampiḷḷai,
Tāḻi-bhaṭṭaṉ and Tiruvallamuḍaiyāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ, all of Gautama-[gōtra] and
Poṟkōyilnambi of Kāśiva (Kāśyapa)-gōtra, who had agreed to burn the sandhi
lamp from the month of Āvaṇi of the year quoted.

l.ta:
tiripuvaṉaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvicaiyakaṇṭakopālatevarkku yāṇṭu
irupatteṭṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu maṇaviṟkoṭṭattuk kūvamāṉa

eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.
This inscription is built in at the beginning.

It appears to record lands with a total extent of 1,000 kuḻi in some loca-
lities by a certain Pallavaraiyaṉ for various services, nandā lamp, and offerings
to the god of the temple of Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyanāyaṉār, and the acceptance
of the gift by the tāṉikar of the temple who had undertaken to carry out the
object of the gift.

Representing the donors, Kuṟumuḍich Chūḍiyanāyaṉ Pallavaraiyaṉ, and
Kurumuḍit-Tiruvēṅkaṭapperumāḷ Irāśanārāyaṇap Pallavaraiyaṉ figure as
signatories. Among the tāṉikars Tiru Aṇṇāmalaiuḍaiyāṉ Śōmaṉā-bhaṭṭaṉ
of Gautama-[gōtra] and Poṟkōyinambi Sōmaṉādēvaṉ of Kāśyapa-[gōtra]
figure as signatories.

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 340-A of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is also fragmentary.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp by a certain Attippēraiyaṉ, the son of
Vīraśōḻappēraiyaṉ, to the temple of the god Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyanāyanār.

l.ta:
paḷḷa viracoḻapperaiyaṉ makaṉ attipperaiyaṉ pakkal ikkoyiṟ civappirāmaṇa-
rom

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 340-B of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is also extremely fragmentary.

It seems to record a gift of a sandhi lamp to the same temple occurring in
No. 360 above, by a certain Kumaraśāttaṉ Vāḻavandāṉ Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyāṉ
Pāriūṉaṉ Maṟṟaṉ of the same place (name lost). Of the Śivabrāhmaṇas of
the temple the name of Śivakkoḻuna-bhaṭṭaṉ Śeṉṉalvaittāṉ of Gautama-
[gōtra] is preserved.

l.ta:
ṭayanāyaṉārkku ivvūrk kumaracāttaṉ vāḻavantāṉ tiruviṟkolamuṭaiyāṉ pāriūṉa
maṟṟaṉeṉ innāyaṉārkkuc canti viḷakku erippatāka ikkoyiliṟ kāṇiyuṭaiya
civappirāmaṇar kautamaṉ civakkoḻunapaṭṭaṉ ceṉṉel vaittāṉum meṟ-

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is built in at the beginning, and hence several important
details are not available.

It records a gift of 10 paṇam of money for burning a sandhi lamp in the
temple of Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyanāyaṉār in Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by a certain araiyaṉ (full name not preserved)
of Śivapuram in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in the same maṇḍalam. The gift was
accepted by the same Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who figure in No. 358 above.

l.ta:
••• yāṇṭu irupattu eṭṭāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu kūvamāṉa
tiyākacamuttiranallūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭaiya nāyaṉārkku immaṇṭa-
lattu maṇaviṟ koṭṭattuc civapurattu

tamaṉ tiruvallamuṭaiyāṉ ulakāḷuṭaiyāṉ paṭ-
ṭaṉum kācivaṉ poṟkoyil nampi comaṉātevapaṭṭaṉum iv-

••••• kaikkoṇṭa paṇam pattu ippaṇam pattukkum orucanti viḷakku
cantirātittavarai erippatāka poliyūṭṭākak kaikkoṇṭom ivvaṉaivom
ivai ceṉṉel peṟṟāṉ aracapaṭṭa

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same maṇḍapa.

p.b:
Bukkarāya, son of Harihararāya II Kali 4488 [Prab]hava Aṟpaśi 25,
(Thursday) = 1387 A.D. October 24.

inscription.p: This inscription is damaged.

It records a grant of 500 kuḻi of land in Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudra-
nallūr and Tribuvaṉamādēviviḷāgam, both situated in the Kāñchīpura-
mārāchchiyār (Kāñchīpura-mahārājyam) by Ōbaḷadēvamāhārāśaṉ described
as Piḷḷaiyār. The 500 kuḻi comprised of lands given away by ten different
persons at the rate of 50 kuḻi each.

l.ta: ••• vāṉaṉ eḻuttu ivai cempiyataraiyaṉ eḻuttu ivai
ivai toṇṭaimāṉ aruḷāḷap perumāṉ eḻuttu § i ••••

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 343 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
The last part of the inscription is missing.

This inscription records a gift of a nandā lamp to the temple of the god
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam
in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam by Śaṅgambai, wife of Tumbūraṉ Tiruvaraṅga-
muḍaiyāṉ of the same place. To provide for this a sum of 15
Aṉṟāḍu Narpaḻaṅkāśu was endowed by her from the month of Appaśi of the
year, which was received by the temple Śivabrāhmaṇas, viz. Kauśikaṉ Śenneṟ-
peṟṟāṉ, Gautamaṉ Uḍaiyappiḷḷai, Gautamaṉ Tiruvalamuḍaiyāṉ, Kāśyapaṉ
Dēvappiḷḷai, and Bhāradvāji Ka(Ga)ṇapati-bhaṭṭaṉ who had agreed to burn
the lamp for ever.

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 344 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Sakalalōkachakravartin Rājanārāyaṇa, Tirumallinātha Śambuvarāya:
Year 16: C. 14th century.

inscription.p: This inscription is built in at the end.

It appears to record a gift of land in Tirubuvaṉamādēvi viḷāgam for worship
and a sandhi lamp to the god Tōvala Kūttappiḷḷaiyār, probably of the temple
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār, in Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr. The donor's
name and other details are lost. Mentions a locality called Tiruveṉkāṭṭuttaḻuval.

l.ta:
tti irācanārāyaṇaṉ tirumallinātaṉ campuvarāyaṟku
yāṇṭu patiṉāṟāvatu kūvamāṉa tiyākacamuttiranallūr u ••• ••• ••••

ppaṇṇiṉa tovalakūttapiḷḷaiyār tiruviruppuk kuḻi nūṟṟukku śrīpaṇṭārattukku
ta •••• viṭṭa tiruveṇkāṭṭuttaḻuvalil teṉpāti nik[ki] vaṭapa ••••

iṟukka[ka]ṭavarkaḷākavum tovalakūttappiḷḷaiyārkku pūcaikkum cantiviḷakku ••

āṉa te[va]

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 36 Naṟpaḻaṅkāśu for burning a nandā
lamp in the temple of Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār in Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudra-
nallūr in Maṇaviṟ-kōṭṭam in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by a certain Kavuśikan
Kākkunāyakka-bhaṭṭan of Tiruvīḻimiḻalai. The temple Śivabrāhmaṇas
Kauśikaṉ Dēvappiḷḷai, Gautaman Uḍaiyappiḷḷai, his younger brothers, viz.
Gautaman Śōma-bhaṭṭan and Gautaman Tiruvalamuḍaiyān-bhaṭṭan and
Kāśyapan Dēvappiḷḷai who received the gift of money had agreed to burn the
lamp from the month of Chittirai.

l.ta:
viṟkoṭṭattuk kūvamāna tiyākacamuttiranallūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruviṟkolamuṭai-
yārkku tiruviḻimiḻalaik kavucikan kākkunāyakka

oru tirunantāviḷakku cantirātittavarai celuttak kaṭavomāṉom
ivvaṉaivom ippaṭikku ivai

n tevappiḷḷai eḻuttu § ippaṭi aṟiveṉ ikkoyil pāracavan •• maṟ
•• n eḻuttu śrīmā

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is damaged and incomplete.

It refers to a particular service rendered during festival days to the god
Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār of Kūvam alias Tiyāgasamudranallūr by the Iruṅgaṉūrk-
kiḻavars of the place.

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the temple-kitchen.

This inscription records an endowment of Rupees 130 with a certain Taṇi-
yāttaṉ of Maḻavarāya-gōttiram, belonging to Piḷḷaiyār-kuppam, and records
that the interest derived from this should be used annually for the special
abhishēka on the Āṉi-Tirumañjaṉam days for the god Chiṟsabhāpati of the
(temple of) Tirupurāntakasvāmiyār, by the above-mentioned Taṇiyāttaṉ and
his descendants. The above-mentioned abhishēka service was instituted
by a certain Araśappamudali, son of Poṉmugari Tiruviṟkōlamuḍaiyār of
Kumārachchēri Kumāra[tair]-gōttiram, who endowed Rupees 80 for this and
the balance of Rupees 50 was contributed by many others.

l.ta: varuṣam m pirati varuṣam m api-

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 348 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up in a grove of trees near the village.

This undated record in characters of c. 17th century records, the raising
of a grove which was gifted to Tiṁmappanāyani by Timmānāyani, son of
(name lost) at Kuvvaṁ; the donee was to enjoy the fruits of the grove. Kūvvaṁ
was under the administration of the donor.

l.te: ddu gōhattya braṁhattya cēśina pāpāna bō-

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 349 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in another grove of trees.

This inscription, in corrupt language, records a gift of a flower garden
and an Iluppai tree grove to provide for a lamp to the god Tirupurāntaka-
svāmiyār by Aṇṇāsāmimudaliyār and others, sons of Muttiyā-mudali of
Vaṟāya-gōttiram of Kumarachcherivu.

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 350 of 1909.)
KŪVAM, TIRUVALLORE TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab near the temple tank.

p.b: Qutb-Shāh of Gōlkoṇḍa: Śaka 1[5]9[2] Virōdhikṛit = 1671 A.D.

inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. X, No. 763.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 351 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Agastyēśvara temple.

This inscription contains a praśasti of the king, commencing with Pugaḻ-
mādu viḷaṅga, etc., Mentions his queen Avanimuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records that a certain Kaṇṇapākkiḻān Paṟpan Ulaganādan paved the
garbhagṛiha (sanctum sanctorum) of the temple of the god Tiruvagattīśvaramu-
ḍaiya-mahādēva of Ulakkaiyūr alias Rājamahēndra-nallūr in Ōymā-nāḍu in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and that he also set up a pedestal (śrīpādapīṭham)
a lamp-post (ney-tāṅgi) and an image of the god Vighnēśvara and gifted a
nandā lamp to the god Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiyār. It states that the sabhai
of the tiruvuṇṇāḻigai (i.e., the members in charge of the central shrine),
received a sum of 5 kaḻañju of poṉ from the donor and had agreed to burn
the lamp in the central shrine for ever.

At the end it is stated that the inscription is a copy of that which was
engraved on the jamb of the doorway of the tiruvuṇṇāḻigai (sanctum sancto-
rum) the same as No. 376 below.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟa

maṇimuṭi[cū*]ṭi minava[r*] nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara yeṉai
mannavariyaluṟṟiḻitara

tikkaṇaittuntan cakkara naṭātti [ vijaiyāpiṣegam] paṇṇi • • virasiṁhāsanattu
avanimuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya kovirājagēśari panmarāṉa cakkara-
varttikaḷ

l.ta:
vaṟkku yāṇṭu nāṟpattonṟāvatu jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻa-
maṇṭalattu oymānāṭāṉa

kattu talañceytu śrīpātapīṭamum neytāṅkiyuñ camaittuc-
cātti piḷḷai

pākkiḻān paṟpan ulakanātan

tirununtāviḷakku oṉṟu-

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 352 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Kaḍal
śūḻnda pār mādarum, etc. Mentions his queen Ulaguḍai Mukkōkkiḻānaḍigaḷ.

It states that a certain Appaṉ Śāttaṉ of Kuḍippaḷḷi, the hamlet of
Neḍiyam in Eyilṇeḍuṅgala-nāḍu in Vijaiya Rājēndra-vaḷanāḍu in
Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam and another man named Oraḷi Udaiyaṉ-perumāḷ of
Uratti in Uratti-nāḍu in Veṇkuṉṟak-kōṭṭam went for hunting. There the
arrow aimed by the latter at a pulvāy (bird) accidentally hit the former who
died due to this. Then the various groups of important people of the maṇḍalam,
like the paṭṭapperumakkaḷ, periyanāṭṭār and paṉṉāṭṭār assembled and ordered
that Oraḷi Udaiyaṉ Perumāḷ, to atone for the killing, should gift a nandā
lamp to the god Vātāpiviṭaṅkar in the temple of the god Tiruvagattīśvara-
muḍaiya-mahādēvar of Ulakkaiūr alias Rājēndranallūr in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-
maṇḍalam. Accordingly the man gave the lamp and 32 cows and a bull
to provide for this. The sabhai of the central shrine of the temple accepted
the animals and agreed to burn the lamp.

l.ta:
kaṭalcūḻnta pārmātaruṅ kalaimātaruñ cimātarum pormātarum
vantu vāḻa nāṟkaṭalcūḻ puvi eḻum pāṟkaṭal cūḻ pukaḻ parappac cotimaṇi
maṇimuṭi puṉaintaruḷiyaṟu camaiyamu maiypūtamu neṟiyil vantu pārippat teṉṉa-
varuñ ceralaruñ ciṅkaḷaru mutalāya

maṉṉavar vantiṟai kuṇantu cevippa vāḻi ūḻi varu ceṅkoleḻupārumiṉi
kaḷippac cempoṉ

vaḷanāṭṭu eyilneṭuṅkalanāṭṭu
neṭiyattuk kuṭippaḷḷi appaṉ cāttanum veṇkuṉṟakkoṭṭattu urattināṭṭu
uratti oraḷi utaiyaṉ perumāḷum pariveṭṭai poṉa iṭattil oraḷi utaiyaṉ
perumāḷ pulvāyai eyta ampu piḻaittu iv-

vappaṉ cāttaṉai ampupaṭṭu paṭṭamaiyil itukkup paṭṭapperumakkaḷum periyanāṭṭā-
rum paṉṉāṭṭārum kūṭi iruntu pirāyacittam vitittapaṭi immaṇṭalattu
ulakkai ūrāna rāje

taṉukkākap pakainiṅka ivvo[ra*]ḷi utaiyaṉ perumāṉeṉ vaitta tirununtā
viḷakku oṉṟukkum ikkoyil tiruvuṇṇāḻikai (ca)

yamākak
kaikkoṇṭa pacu muppattiraṇṭum iṣapam oṉṟuṅ kaikkoṇṭu ittirununtā
viḷakku oṉṟuñ cantirātittavarai erikkakkaṭavom ākac camma-

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same temple.

This inscription is incomplete and it contains the praśasti of the king
commencing with Pūmēvi vaḷar tiruppoṉ, etc., and mentions the queen
Buvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It states that Irājēndradēvar gave 100 kaḻañju of gold for raising a stone
structure (tirukkaṟṟaḷi) to the god Tiruvagattīśvaramuḍaiya-mahādēvar of
Ulakkaiyūr alias Irājamahēndranallūr in Ōymā-nāḍu alias Vijaiyarājēndra-
vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam. The money was entrusted to ūr of
the place. Of this a sum of 50 kaḻañju was expended to raise the structure
up to the level of the paṅchāṅga. The structure remained unfinished thereafter,
because the balance of 50 kaḻañju was otherwise spent out in course of time.
When the artisans and the Śrī-Māhēśvaras demanded that the stone structure
should be finished, the ūr people were not able to do that. To make good the
loss a sum of 20 kaḻañju of poṉ was spent to set up an image of the god
Umāskandasahita in the temple as it lacked one. Then the inscription
proceeds to refer to a gift of two vēli of land as dēvadāna to provide for the
services in the temple, but stops.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] pūmevivaḷar tiruppoṉ [ puṇara nāmevu kalaimakaḷ nalam peritu
ciṟappa vijaiyamāmakaḷ velpuyat tiruppa icaiyum celviyum eṇṭicai
viḷaṅka nirupar vantiṟaiñca niṇila maṭantaiyait tirumaṇam puṇarntu cirvaḷar
tarum maṇimuṭi] kavitteṉa maṇimuṭi cūṭi mallai ñālattup palluyirellā-

hāsaṉattu puvaṉamuḻutuṭaiyāḷoṭum viṟṟiruntaruḷiya korājakecaripanmarāna
tribhuvaṉaśśakravattikaḷ śrīkulottuṅkacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu añcāvatu

tevar ivvūr ūrom pakkal tanta pon
nūṟṟuk kaḻañcuṅkaikkoṇṭu tirukka-

nikkippoṉ aimpatiṉ kaḻañcuṅ kāla virotattālantarappaṭṭa pinpu tirukkaṟṟaḷi

nikki nivantan nellai muṟṟukkuṅ kollai nilam iruvelikku

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place.

This inscription records the following order made by the ūravar and the
tāṉattār of Ulakkaiyūr alias Rājamahēndra-nallūr to the Kaikkōḷar of the
street around the temple of the god Tiruvagattīśvara-muḍaiya nāyiṉār. It
states that the Kaikkōḷar who had previously deserted the street might
reoccupy the street again and continue their profession of weaving. While
levying tax on them, a lease amount of 4 paṇam towards taṟikkaḍamai, vāśal-
paṇam, śōḍi, śūla-vari, vāl-vari, talai-ārikkam, natta-vari, paḻa-vari, pudu-
vari, pala-kāṇikkai and other taxes and one paṇam towards tax on cotton
threads, (both) totalling to 5 paṇam, on every loom should alone be received
for each year from the year Śukkiṟa.

l.ta:
paṅkuṉi mātam pattāntiyati
ulakkaiyūrāṉa irācamakentiranallūr ūravarum tāṉattārum ivvūr nāyaṉār
tiruvaka •••

muṭaiyanāyiṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākattil kaikkoḷaṟku kalluveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭi ivarkaḷ
yiṟai āṟṟāmal oṭippoy tirumaṭaiviḷākamum pāḻāy nāyiṉārum pū[cai ko*]

ḷḷāmal koyilumaṭaittukkiṭakkaiyi[l*] iṉṉāḷ mutal tirumaṭaiviḷākattile
kuṭiyum pukuntu taṟiyum iṭṭu neyakkaṭavarkaḷākavum kaṭamai koḷḷu-

mutal cantirātittavaraikkum cella āṇṭu oṉṟukku
taṟi oṉṟukku koḷḷum taṟikkaṭamai vācal paṇam coṭi cūlavari

vālvari talaiārrikkam nattavari paḻavari putuvari palakāṇikkaiyum yuṭpaṭa cakalāyaṅ-
kaṭkuk kuttakai āka koḷḷum

ippaṇam añcu oḻiya eṟakkoṇṭārruṇṭākil piṟama[ha]tti-
yār tocam [ha]ttiyār [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 355 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Door-post of the entrance into the same temple.
This inscription is damaged.

This is the original of which a copy was inscribed later on the south
wall of the temple and dealt with above in No. 372.

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 356 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Brahmin Street.

This inscription in characters of about the 6th century A.D., records
that this was caused to be made by a certain Adiaraiśan to Piritivi-viṭaṅkar
(Pṛithviviṭaṅkar).

edition.l: pirativi viṭa[ ṅ-
k] ku ratiarai-

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 357 of 1909.)
OLAKKŪR, TINDIVANAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a slab set up near the village chāvaḍi.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 112.

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 358 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the ruined Śiva temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 248.

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock near the temple.
The inscription refers to the ruler as one ‘who took the head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records the gift of 90 sheep for a nondā lamp to the god Taṇakka-
malai-Āḷvār by Iḷaṅgōmuḻāṉ Nākayaṉ Taṇakkamalai of the village.

l.ta: kku nicatam uḻakkumaṭṭi erippatāka vacca cā-

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same place.

This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who took the head of the
Pāṇḍya’. It is damaged.

It records a gift of 80 sheep for a nondā lamp to the temple of the god
Mahādēvar Taṇakkamalai Āḷvār of Tāyaṉūr in Śiṅgapura-nāḍu by a certain
(name lost) of Maṇḍaikuḷattūr in [••• •••] kuḍi-nāḍu.

l.ta: hātevar taṇakkamalaiyāḷvārkku oru nontā viḷakku uḻakku ni-
catam erippatāka vaicca cāvā muvāpperāṭu eṇpatu ivai tāyaṉū-

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 361 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same place.
This inscription refers to the king as one ‘who took the head of the Pāṇḍya’.

It records the gift of 90 sheep for a nondā lamp to the temple of the god
Mahādēvar Taṇakkamalai Āḷvār of Miyvaḻit-Tāyaṉūr in Śiṅgapura-nāḍu
by a certain Bhāradvājan Kēsuvaṉ Śiṅgaṉ, a member of the āḷuṅgaṇam of
[Ka?]ṇṇāṉamaṅgalam in Kīḻ-Āmūr-nāḍu of Tirumuṉaippāḍi. The gift is stated
to have been entrusted to the ūrār of Tāyaṉūr for executing the object.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE, TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the same rock.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 178.

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 176.

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On a rock in the backyard of a private house.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III, No. 175.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 365 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another rock.

p.b: Śaka 1478, Naḷa, Śiṅga (Āvaṇi) śu. 6, Tuesday, Svāti = 1556 A.D., August 11.

inscription.p:
This inscription is engraved by the side of a long sketch representing the
linear measure, carved on the rock.

It records, pointing to the carving, that this is the kōl (rod) used for
measuring lands in three villages, one called Malaiyaṉūr, the second Toṟu-
[ppām ?], and the third (name lost) and that this was (the decision) of a certain
Pārañji Tiṁmaiyaṅgarāśāyar, the agent of Kōvilt-Tiruvēṅkaḍa, the Jīyar
of the big temple of Tirumalai Tiruppati, who is described as Vēdamāṟga-
pratiṭṭāchārya, Parama-aṅsa-parivviṟāśakāchāriya and Vēdāntāchāriya.

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 366 of 1909.)
TĀYAṈŪR, GINGEE TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A third rock in a field.

This inscription refers to a certain Vāṇādarāyar, the agent of Vīrappa-
nāyakkar Ayyaṉ.

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Vyāghra-
pādēśvara temple.

This inscription is in Grantha characters of c. 11th century and in Sanskrit
verse.

It records that a minister of king Rājēndrachōḷa whose lotus-like feet
were bowed to by the heads of kings, named Sabhānartaka and surnamed
Kāliṅgarāja and Mānāvatāra, the ruler of Maṇavil, built a temple for Śaṁbhu,
using stones at Siddhaliṅga. The composer of the verse was one Āṇḍapiḷḷai-
bhaṭṭaṉ of Tirukkōvalūr. (See No. 390 below.)

inscription.head:
No. 389.
(A.R. No. 368 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a land called Koṇḍāḻi as tax-free dēvadāna
to the temple of the god Triuppuli-bagavar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a Brahmadēya, in
Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu by a certain
Muttirattān Gaṅgaikoṇḍāṉ Parasamaiyakōḷari who sold it to Bhāradvāji
Tirunīlakaṇṭaṉ Sūryadēvaṉ and Kāḷidāsaṉ of the same place. The sabhai
of the place accepted the gift and agreed to pay all the taxes in respect of the
land.

edition.l:
svastiśrī [||*] [ korā] jakecaripanmarāṉa tribhuvaṉacakravattikaḷ śrīkolottuṅka
coḻadēvarkku yāṇṭu 16 āvatu malā-

l.ta:
yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār
tiruppulippakavar muttirattān kaṅkai-

vatānam koṇṭāḻi eṉṉum cey paḻampaṭi nilam aiñcu
mi[n]nilattukkum cillaṟai amañci āṟṟuk-

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 369 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is in Sanskrit verse and in Grantha characters of c. 11th
century.

It records that a certain Narttaka, surnamed Mānāvatāra, the ruler of Maṇavi
built by stones a temple (vimāna) provided with a prākāra surrounded by
areca-palms and a maṇḍapa, on the banks of a river to the god Paśupati, whose
feet were worshipped by the sage Vyāghrapāda, at Siddhaliṅga, a great
agrahāra noted for its wealth and beauty during the time of the ruler Jayadhara.
See No. 388 above where this is recorded in a general way.

l.gr:
nṛpatē rnnarttakākhya śśilābhissa prākāram vimānam kramukavarivṛtammaṇḍa
pēnavyadhattya [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of (1/4) śey of land in the southern hamlet
called Koliyūr of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēya in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu
alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, by the assembly (sabhai) of the place to the dancing
woman named Tiruppulippagavar Nirttaviṭaṅki and her descendants, as tax-
free nirttabōgam, for performing dance before the image of the god Tiruppulip-
pagavar when it was taken out in procession.

l.ta:
vaṟku yāṇṭu patineṭṭāvatu malāṭāṉa jaṉanātavaḷa
nāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu

yom tiruppulippakavarkku tiruviḻā eḻuntaruḷumpotu muṉ
niṉṟu nirttañ ceyya nirttappo-

kamāka tiruppulippakavar nirttaviṭaṅkikkum ivaḷaṉṉuvayattukkum ceytu kuṭutta nila-
māvatu eṅkaḷūrt teṉ

piṭākai ko[li]yūril kiḻkaḻaniyiṟ peruvāykkālukku vaṭakku tāṉattār nilattukku
meṟku kālcey iṟai-

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 371 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is interesting.

It records a gift of a nandā lamp and 32 cows to provide for this to the
temple of the god Tiruppulippagavar of the same place as occurred in No. 391
above, by the temple Śivabrāhmaṇa Bhāradvāji Pañchaṉadi-periyān who
prayed for a son and was blessed with one. In lieu of the cows a sum of 10
Anṟāḍu-Naṟkāśu was received by the temple Śivabrāhmaṇas who had agreed to
burn the nandā lamp for ever in the temple.

l.ta:
tittu āṇpiḷḷai peṟṟuvaitta tirunantāviḷakku onṟukku pacu muppattiraṇṭukkāka
ikkoyilil civa-

ṇarom eṅkaḷu[paiyattāl] koṇṭa anṟāṭunaṟkācu pattu ikkācu pattum
kaikkoṇṭu ittevar-

tittavaṟ erikkak kaṭavomāka cammatittom
ikkoyil civa

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 372 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a nandā lamp for use in the sanctum
sanctorum of the temple of Tiruppulippagavadēvar in Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam
in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu by a certain
Kayiṟūr Pichchaṉ Araiśaṉ of Āṉattar Śiṟuveṇṇainallūr (?) in Ēḻūr-nāḍu who
was also the Kiḻāṉ of Tirumuṉaippāḍi. It is stated that he endowed a sum of
8(7/8) Kaḻañju of poṉ for this purpose and the same was received by the
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple who had agreed to carry out the object of the
endowment for ever.

Also records the gift by the same donor 80 palam in weight of metal,
weighed by the balance called Tirumāltirupāṭṭak-kōl, for the (making of)
the nandā lamp.

l.ta:

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is incomplete.
It refers to Śiṟṟiṅgūr in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu

l.ta:
vaṟkku yāṇṭu patiṉ-
muṉṟāvatu malāṭāṉa jananātavaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu

inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 94.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 375 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is damaged and refers to the king as one ‘who took
Kachchi and Tañjai’.

It records a gift of land by the sabhai (assembly) of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a Brahmadē-
yam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu, as dēvadana for offerings to the god
Tiruppulippagavar of the place. It is stated that the sabhai had exempted this
land from the payment of the levies śilliṟai and echchōṟu.

l.ta:
vaṉukku yāṇṭu aiñcāvatu
malāṭṭuk kuṟukkaik kūṟṟattu
