inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 376 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records that the ūr (i.e. the villagers) of Marudūr sold to
Iṟaivāṅkuḍi-kiḻavaṉ called as eṅgaḷ magaṉār for a sum of 75 kaḻañju of poṉ
the right of using (1/5) of the water that flowed through the big sluice in the
channel in their village, through which water was taken to Balipaṭṭi, a Bali-
puṟam of the god Tirupulippagavar. It is stated that the water thus sold was
for irrigating the land of the person mentioned. It is also stated that in case
of the stoppage of the flow of water for this purpose a sum of 2 kaḻañju of poṉ
was to be remitted.

l.ta: m tiruppulippakavar tiruppalippuṟamāṉa palippaṭṭikku eṅkaḷūril nīr
kalliṉa vākkāl vaḻiye nīr eṅkaḷūr tūmpukalakki lippe-
niril aiñcopāti nir pāya urittākac ce-
tu kuṭuttom marutūr ūrom eṅka makaṉār iṟaivāṉkuṭikiḻava-
ituve vilaiyāvatāka viṟṟuk kuṭuttom eṅkaḷ makaṉā riṟaivā-
ṉkuṭi kiḻavaṟku marutūr ūrom innilattukku nir mañci eṉṟu kāṭ-
ṭavuñ collavum peṟātomākavum innilattukku nir pāccā niṟ-
kattiṟpāṉai aṉṟāṭu kovukku irukaḻaiñcu poṉ maṉṟappe[ṟu]-
m eṅkaḷ makanār iṟaiyāṉkuṭikiḻavaṟku marutūr ūrom i[tu*]

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 377 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 47.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 378 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

This inscription records a gift of a standing nandā lamp for lighting the
sanctum sanctorum of the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavar-dēvar of
Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-
vaḷanāḍu by a certain Sadiri, the daughter of Malaiyaṉ alias Rājēndrśōḻach-
chēdirāyan and wife of Tillaināyakan Dēvarakaṇḍaṉ, the resident (or owner)
of the northern bank of Periṅgūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi Periṅgūr-nāḍu in Gaṅ-
gaiykoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. It is stated that she gave 32 cows, 16 on the
spot and 16 to be purchased for an amount of 9(1/5) Aṉrāḍu Naṟkāśu at the rate
of (1/2) and one and a half mā of kāśu a cow. It is further stated that the
Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple received the 16 cows and the money equivalent
for another 16 cows and had agreed to burn the lamp as desired by the donor.

It also records that the donor woman gave 70 palam of bell-metal for
making the standing lamp, necessary quantity of bronze for an eating plate
(parikalam) and 29 palam of bell-metal for a tripod (aṭṭaṇaikkāl) and one
kāśu. The Śivabrāhmaṇas received these articles and the money with an
undertaking that they themselves would attend to the repairs of these articles.

l.ta:
vanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrīvikkiramacoḻatevaṟku yāṇṭu muṉṟāvatu
malāṭākiya jaṉanātavaḷa-

munṟukku
m teyvum ūnamum nāṅkaḷe tirkka kaṭavomāka ivaḷpakkal

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 379 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the same maṇḍapa.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIII, No. 41.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 380 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 500 kuḻi of land as tax-free tirunāmattuk-
kāṇi, to provide for offerings to the god Āḍavallār (i.e., Naṭarāja) in the temple
of the god Tiruppulippagava-nāyaṉār of Śiṟṟiṅgūr in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in
Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, by the Śāliga-nagarattārs of Tirukkōvalūr
residing in Marudūr alias Maduraikkuvāytta Perumāḷpuram, in the same
nāḍu. It is stated that the gift-land was given to the Nagarattārs as Perumāḷ-
kāṇi probably by the king.

l.ta:
ḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakavanāyaṉāṟku iṉ-
nāṭṭu marutūrāna ma-

turaikkuvāyttaperumā[ḷ*]purattu tirukkovalūr nakarattārom ikkoyil
mutaliyār āṭavallārke ciṟukālaiccantiyum amu-

tuppaṭikkuṭpaṭṭaveñcaṉattukkum viṭṭa naṉcai nilamāvatu eṅkaḷukku perumāḷ kāṇi-
yāka

na immarutūrāna maturaikkuvāyttaperumāḷpurattu iṉnāyaṉāṟku tirunāmattukkāṇi
tiruppulippakavaṉukku itaṟku

iṉnāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇi nilamaṟumāvukku meṟku paṉaṅkāṭṭuppuṟam taṭi
iraṇṭu kuḻi
pācaṉam cittiraikkār uḷḷiṭṭa payirkaḷ aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum uṭpaṭa ca

ka viṭṭom maturaikkuvāyttaperumāḷpurattu tirukkovalūr cālikanakāttārom
itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 381 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This incomplete inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing
with Pugaḷmādu viḷaṅga, etc.

It refers to the sale of land by the ūrār of Śēmam-Āṟṟūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi
Empēṟṟūr-nāḍu in Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu. Also refers to the festival in
the month of Vaikāśi of the god Tiruppulippagavar in Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam
in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu vi[ḷa*]ṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭi

cūṭi villavar nilaikeṭa maṉ[ṉa*]var kulaitara eṉai manna[va][r*] iriyaluṟṟiḻi
tarat tikkaṉaittu

vaḷanāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭi empeṟṟūrnāṭṭuc cemamāṟṟūrom nila vilai āka viṟṟa
nilam malāṭākiya jananātavaḷa

yam ciṟṟiṅkūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavar
tiruvaiykācit tirunāḷ

inscription.head:
No. 403.
(A.R. No. 382 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pugaḻ-
mādu viḷaṅga and mentions the queen Ulaguḍaiyāḷ.

It records a gift of land, 1,650 kuḻi in extent, as tax-free dēvadāna for offerings
and lamps at the service of tiruppaḷḷiyeḻuchchi of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar
of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-
vaḷanāḍu by a certain Pūvan Marudan alias Āḻvāṇaṅgāṟa Malaiyamān of
Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi Dāmar-nāḍu. It is stated that the donor paid a
sum of 17 kāśu as price for the land to the sabhai of Śiṟṟiṅgūr.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇara urimaiyi-

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 383 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the sale of land, 700 kuḻi in extent, as tax-free
dēvadāna by the mahāsabhai of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukaiy-kūṟṟam
in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu for an amount of money (not specified)
agreed to by the sabhai, to a certain Āḍavallāṉ Vāsudēvan alias Muḍikoṇḍaśōḻa
Mūvēndavēḷāṉ, the madhyastha of Muññūṟu alias Paṇḍitagōshṭhi-saruppēdi-
maṅgalam in Ōymā-nāḍu. It is stated that he endowed it to provide for the
services and offerings connected with the procession on every Sunday and
the morning worship of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar.

l.ta: ḻvār tevatānattukkum rājarājan vāykkālukku te[ṟ*]kum āka
patiyā kuṇṭi[l*] kuḻi aimpatu nikki niṉṟa kuḻi eḻuṉūṟum uḷnila-
tupaṭi arici patakkum kaṟiyamutu oṉṟum neyyamutu uḻakkum tai-
yiramutu irunāḻiyum uppamutu uḻakkum aṭaikkāyamutu pākku pattu-
m veṟṟilaiyamutu irupatum tiruviḷakkeṇṇai nāḻiyum tirunāya-

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 384 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 126.

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 385 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift by a certain Tirunāvukkaraiśi of one gong
and three trumpets towards which he gave a sum of 5 kaḻañju of poṉ to the
temple of the god Tiruppulippagavar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam. It is
stated that the temple managers (kōyiluḍaiyār) received the gift and agreed to
provide the above-mentioned articles to the temple.

l.ta:
yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr
āḻvār tiruppulippakavar koyiluṭaiyom i

koṭṭa tirunāvukkaraiciyeṉ vaitta cekaṇṭikai niṟai eḻu-
pattaim palattālum kāḷamiraṇṭināl niṟai eṇ palattālum tanikkāḷam
oṉṟināl niṟai nāṟpalattinālumiṭak kaṭavomāka ittirunāvukkar

koṇṭa poṉ aiṅkaḻañcu ippoṉ aiṅkaḻañcuṅkoṇṭu ivaiyaṉaittum

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 386 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same maṇḍapa.

p.b:
Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva: Year 31 or
1100-01 or 1103-04 A.D.

inscription.p:
This damaged inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing
with Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc. It mentions the queen Avaṉimuḷuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records a sale of land, 1250 kuḻi in extent, as tax-free dēvadāna for the
tirupaḷḷiyeḻuchchi service and offerings at it to the god Tiruppulippagava-
dēvar of the same Śiṟṟiṅgūr occuring in Nos. 406 etc., above, by the sabhai
of the place. The land was purchased by a certain [••• •••] laiyan alias [••• •••]
jēndraśōḻa-vāṇakularājaṉ Agamuḍaiyān who endowed the same for the above
mentioned purpose. It records also a gift of an eating plate (parikalam) for
use in carrying food offerings at the time of the service.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ
puṇara

urimaiyiṟ ciṟanta maṇimuṭicūṭi miṉavar nilaikeṭa villavar kulaitara eṉai mannavar
iriyaluṟṟi-

cāttiyaruḷit tiruvamutaricikkum kaṟiyamutu •• kku neyyamutukku ceviṭaraiyum
tayiramuturikku aṭaikkā-

yamutu pākku nā ••• •••• m veṟṟilai amutu pattukkum ••••
mutu arici nānāḻikkuc cantirātittavaṟ nivantamā-

vatāṉamāka koṇṭu viṭṭa-
nilam patiṉaṟu cāṇ kolā-

l āyirattu irunūṟṟampatukku [iru]ñcicey ••• ••• •••• koṉṟa-
mañci teṉ kūṟum āka āyi-

ntikku amutu ceytaruḷukaikku iṭṭa parikalam onṟināl iṭai
muppatu pa

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 387 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is incomplete.

It appears to record a gift of land by Ambalavaṉ Aiyyāṟaṉ alias Iṟai-
vāṉkuḍi-kiḻavan for providing music on the three occasions (sandhi) of
worship everyday to the god Tiruppulippagavar of Śiṟṟiṅgūṟ. Among the lands
forming a boundary to the gift land is one gifted to the god Vināyakaṉpagavaṉ.

l.ta:
••• ••• •••• ñ ciṟṟiṅkūr tiruppulippakavarkku ampalavaṉ aiyyāṟa-
ṉāṉa iṟaivāṉkuṭikiḻavaneṉ

••• ••• •••• cūṅ koṭṭuvataṟku vaitta nilamāvatu [maru]tuk
kāṭaṉ vāykkālukku kiḻakku ciṟṟiṅkūr nilam ikkāṭaṉ vāykkālu-

paṭukol[lai]kkum palippaṭṭikku meṟkum te[n]pārkke-

••• ••• •••• yaṉ vināyakaṉpakavaṉ nilattukku vaṭakkum vaṭa
pārkkellai ekacūraṉukkut teṟkum āka ivvicai

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 388 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b: Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva: Year 36 or 31 = 1100-01 A.D. or 1105-06 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 2500 kuḻi of land as tax-free maḍappuṟam
to provide for food-offerings to the Āṇḍārgaḷ praying in the temple of the god
Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēśam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam
in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, by a certain woman Sūryyadēvi alias
Niṉṟatavañjeidāḷ, wife of Nānūṟṟuvan Malaiyamān alias Rājēndrachōḷach-
chēdirāyan of Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi (see No. 422 below), after purchas-
ing the land from the sabhai (assembly) of Śiṟṟiṅgūr. Also records a gift of
nattam land, purchasing it from the Brāhmaṇa Bhāradvāji Kumārasvāmi-
pārāyaṇan and others, by the same Sūryyadēvī.

l.ta:
yom āḷuṭai
yār tiruppulippakavatevar koyilil kumpiṭ-

yāna niṉṟatavuñceytāḷ iṟaiili maṭappuṟamāka koṇṭuviṭṭa nilattukku kiḻ-
pārkkellai tiruppulippakavan-

vatikku meṟkum tenpākkellai aimperuṅkālukku vaṭakkum melpārkkellai
āccipiṭāri kiḻvarampukkum tan-

maccey kiḻvarampukkum tevatānam tiruvalakukuṇṭil kiḻvarampukkum nāṭakappaṅkukku
kiḻvarampil kaṇṇikālukkum
kiḻakku vaṭapāṟkellai maturāntakan vāykkālukku teṟkum āka ivvicai

patin aṟu cāṇkolāl kuḻi iraṇṭāyirattaiñnūṟum uḷnilam oḻivinṟi
emmil icai

poruḷ aṟakkoṇṭu iṟaiyili maṭappuṟamākak kuṭuttom itukku maṭṭum iraṇ-
ṭukkum toṭṭap

yāna niṉṟatavuñ ceytāḷ vilaipporuḷ aṟa iṭṭukkoṇṭu viṭṭa nattattukku
kiḻpārkellai māntoṭṭattu

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 389 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This is just the beginning of a long inscription. Here the name of the ruler,
his regnal year and Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, Kuṟuk •••• are
all that is preserved.

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 390 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is slightly damaged. It records a gift of 10 (probably)
kaḻañju of poṉ for two nandā lamps and one kaḻañju of poṉ for repairs and
remaking of the lamps to the god Tiruppulippagavar by Malāḍuḍaiyāṉ
nāṭṭāṉ Śiddhavaṭavaṉ. The money was entrusted to the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the
place (name not given). The weight of the two lamps is given as 400 palam.

l.ta:
ṇar pakkal
kuṭutta poṉ patin ••• ••• •••• kku ntiviḷakku iraṇṭināl
niṟai nūṟṟup palam nūṟṟup palamum ivai malāṭuṭaiyāṉ nāṭṭāṉ citta
iraṇṭum ūṉamunteyvum nikka kuṭutta poṉ kaḻañcu kaḻañcu[ṅ] kuṭutteṉ
vaṭavaneṉ itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 391 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is also slightly damaged. It records a gift made by
Malāḍuḍaiyāṉ [••• •••] of 10 kaḻañju of poṉ for 12 sandhi lamps for burning
during the evening service in the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavar Āḻvār
of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu. The gift was
accepted by the temple managers who had agreed to burn the lamps accord-
ingly.

l.ta:
yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakavar āḻvārkku malāṭuṭaiyāṉ
••• ••• •••• patiṉkaḻañccaippoṉ ippoṉ patiṉkaḻañcaikku
ivvāḻvārkku iravai

viḷakku erikka kaṭavomā-
nom ikkoyiluṭaiyom i-

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 392 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is built in, in the middle.

It records a gift of 25 kaḻañju of poṉ to provide for festival and a nandā
lamp to the image of god Āḍavallār set up in the temple of god [••• •••••]-
pagavar evidently of Śiṟṟiṅgūr in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jana-
nātha-vaḷanāḍu in Jayaṅgoṇḍaśōḻa-maṇḍalam, by a certain Āḍavallāṉ. The
gift was made by the Brāhmaṇi Kāḷi-Sōmāśi, wife of Ātrayan Kēsuvaṉ Māṟaṉ
of the same village. It was accepted by the temple managers on the Tiruvā-
dirai day of the month of Mārgaḻi.

It also records a gift by the same donor of some land situated in Śiru-
pāganallūr in the western hamlet of the village and near the tank called the
Śrīrājarājapērēri for offerings to the god of the main shrine.

l.ta:
āvatu jayaṅ-
koṇṭacoḻamaṇṭalattu malāṭ[ā*]ṉa jaṉanātavaḷa nāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu

kāḷi comāci pak[ka*]l koṇṭa poṉ irupata[ñ]-
kaḻaiñcukkum palicaiyāka ••••• arici eḻu[ka*]laney eḻukuṟuṇiyum ney

eṭṭumāvum iṉnilamum ivvariciyum koṇṭu nāḷ oṉ • •

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 393 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the verandah round the same shrine.
This inscription is slightly damaged.

It records a sale of 1,000 kuḻi of land as tirunāmattukkāṇi for a sum of 35
Aṉṟāḍu Naṟpaṇam by the members of the sabhai of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēśam in
Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, named Ātrayaṉ Nārā-
yaṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ, Aruṇagiri-bhaṭṭaṉ, his younger brother Tavappiḷḷai and others.
The land was sold to the merchant Uyyavandāṉ Tittikkāḍuvāṉ of Iṉṉambar, a
resident of Śiṟṟiṅgūr itself, who had endowed it to provide for food offerings
and lamp to the shrine of the god Aḻagiya Tiruchchiṟṟambalamuḍaiyār built
by him in the northern prākāra of the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavanā-
yaṉār of the place.

l.ta: uyyavantāṉ tittikkāṭuvāṉ eṟi aruḷappaṇṇiṉa nāyaṉār aḻakiyati-
tittikkāṭuvār koṇṭu viṭṭa nilattukku etta iṟaippaṟṟilak kūttāṇṭai ceṟu-
veṉṟu pokaviṭṭapaṭi nilattukku āḷuṭaiya piḷḷaiyār tevatāṉam atticceṟuvu-

aiññū-

ṟṟukkuṭukka koḷvatāṉa emmil icainta vilaipporuḷ aṉṟāṭu naṟpaṇam muppa-

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 394 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records the purchase of the village of Vilvalam as kāṇi by the
Kaikkōḷars including Kaḍalaḍaittāṉ of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēśam in Kuṟukkaik-
kūṟṟam in Irājarāja-vaḷanāḍu from the sabhai of the same place, and its donation
as dēvadāna to the temple of the god Tiruppulippagava-nāyaṉār of the same
place. It is stated that from the month of Kārttigai of the year quoted (i.e.
10th), all the levies and incomes from the entire village such as kaḍamai, antarā-
yam, kaṟpūra-vilai, poṉ-vari, vini-yōgam, upādi, pañchupili, sandhivigrahappēṟu,
iḍaiyar-vari, iṉa-vari, ērimīṉ-pāṭṭam, vāśal-vari, talaiyoppu, taṟi-iṟai, chekk-irai,
taṭṭoli, taṭṭār-pāṭṭam and other incomes, should be enjoyed by the purchasers.
They in their turn should annually pay paddy at the rate of 12 kalam per one
mā of nañjai land and 5 kalam per one mā of land with crops of Sittiraikār pāddy
irrigating by means of well-water; and 4 poṉ for the puṉśey, puṟavaḍai and
nattam (house-site) lands to the temple.

Two signatories named Śundarapāṇḍya-Kāliṅgarāyaṉ and Neṭṭūruḍai-
yāṉ figure.

l.ta:
tecam ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakavanāyaṉār koyiṟ tāṉat-
tārkkut taṅkaḷ koyiṟ-

yaṉārkkut tevatāṉamāka iṭṭa vilvalattukku cātaṉappaṭi nāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa
naṉcey pu-

ṭpaṭa maṟṟum varum aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum pattāvatu kāttikai mātam mutal variyiṟ
kaḻittu ikkaṭalaṭaittāṉ muḷḷiṭṭār kāṇipeṟ tevatāṉak kaṭamaikku iv-
vūr payirpārttu payirkoṇṭa naṉcey inaiyaṭinilam orumāvukku māttāl paṉ-

aṉaittu vaṟkattukkum āṇṭoṉṟukku nālu poṉṉum iṟuttu aṉupavippā[r]-
lvatāka kallilum cempilum veṭṭivittu ivvūr nāṉkellaiyilum tiru-

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 395 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Māṟavarman Tribhº Vīrapāṇḍyadēva: Year 15, Dhanus (Mārgaḻi), ba. 8,
Saturday, Hastam = 1348 A.D., December 13, f.d.t. ·65, f.d.n. ·60.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of land as tax-free tirunāmattukkāṇi by sale
for 62 Aṉṟāḍu Naṟpaṇam to the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavar of
Śṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Rājarāja
vaḷanāḍu by the Brāhmaṇa Ātrayaṉ Nārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭaṉ Malaipperumāḻ
Aruṇagiri-bhaṭṭaṉ and Tēvappiḷḷai of the same place. The names and bounda-
ries of the various pieces of lands comprising the gift are given in detail. One
of the channels forming the boundary of a particular piece of land is called
Kōpperumjiṅgaṉ.

l.ta: ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tiruppulippakavar nāyaṉār koyil ātitā

yaṇapaṭṭaṉ malaipperumāḷ aruṇakiripaṭṭaṉum tevappiḷḷaiyum ivviruvarom
kāṇivilai

nilamāvatu eṅkaḷ pūtāṉa iṟaiyili āṉa

teṟkkum melpāṟkellai kopperumciṅkaṉ kā-

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 396 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same verandah.

p.b:
Sakalalōkachakravartin Rājanārāyaṇa Śambuvarāya: Year 20, Makara
(Tai) śu. 3, Wednesday, Tiruvōṇam = 1358 A.D. January 10, f.d.n. ·04.
The tithi was Amāvāsyā.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the Śrīmāhēśvaras, stānikars and the Kaikkōḷars
of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēśam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Irāsādirāśa-vaḷanāḍu
met in the temporary shed (kāvaṇam) called the Ponvāśi-koṇḍān in the temple
of the god Tiruppulippagava-ṉāyaṉār of the place, to decide about a land.
The land is stated to have been gifted as dēvadāna previously by a certain Puṅgūr-
kiḻāṉ Tavapperumāḷ alias Viśvaṉādēvar, a Māyēśvara of Tiruppuṉgūr and that
the same was included in the government estate till the 17th year of the king and
tax (irāśa-karam) was paid accordingly. The above-mentioned assembly
arranged to get the land released from the royal holdings, and make it a tax-
free land from the beginning of the 18th year of the king. This land then
became the kāṇi of Viśvāṉadēvar.

l.ta:
pakṣattu titikaiyum putaṉum peṟṟa tiruvoṇattu nāḷ
irācātirāca-

nikarum kaikkoḷarum niṟaivaṟa niṟaintu kuṟaivaṟakkūṭi

tarukaiyil

[vilai]paṭikku kāṇiyāṭci kallilum cempilum veṭṭik koḷḷumpaṭikku cantirā-

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 397 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same verandah.

p.b:
Vīra-Dēvarāya-Uḍaiyar: Śaka 1329, Sarvajit, Mēsha (Chittirai,) śu-3,
Wednesday, Rōhiṇi = 1407 A.D., April 11, But the week-day was Monday.

inscription.p:
This inscription states that Śiṟṟiṇgūr was situated on the southern bank of
the Peṇṇai river and was a brahadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in
Irāsarāśa-vaḷanāḍu. It records that the sabhai of Vinōdanallūr in the same
nāḍu had bought some land from the Tirunāmattukkāṇi land of the temple of
Tiruppulippagava-nāyaṉār of Śiṟṟiṅgūr for digging an irrigation channel and
while digging, a land to the extent of one and half a mā was found in excess. In
lieu of this, the above mentioned sabhai gave away some fields from the
nañjai land of their village to the tānattār of the temple. This gift is termed
as tiruppaṇi-paṟṟu (i.e. the land given for temple repairs).

l.ta:
nāḷ irācarācavaḷanāṭṭu malāṭuk kuṟukkai-
kūṟṟattu peṇ-

[tanal]lūr capaiyār iṉṉāyaṉār tirunāmattukkāṇiyil eṅkaḷūrku nirpāya nāṅkaḷ
iṟuttu veṭṭukaiyil inilacetamāṉa nilamāy aḷantukaṇṭa nilam oru māvaraikkum

nilattukku nilamāka eṅkaḷūril nañcai nilattu kuṭiniṅkā tevatāṉattukku viṭṭa
āḷavantanāya^1^ṉār aruḷicceya[l*]paṭikku viṭṭa ce[ykku] kiḻakku tāṉakuṇ[ṭil]

m irumāvaraiyum taṇṭukarai aracukku kiḻakku tiruviḷakkupuṟam ellaikku meṟkku

cantiṟātittavaraiyum cella tiruppaṇi paṟṟāka viṭṭom inta nāyaṉār koyil
t[ā*]ṉattārkku capaiyārom itu

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 398 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Virūpākshadēva-Mahārāya, [son of] Vīrapratāpa Dēvarāyamārāya
Prauḍha Dēvarāya ?: Śaka 1407 = 1485-86 A.D.

inscription.p: Damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 399 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone built into the floor of the same verandah.

This inscription appears to record that the whole maṇḍapa was (by) Villavar
alias Maguḍattiyāgi.

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 400 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the prākāra in the same temple.

p.b:
Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva III: Year 9, Vṛiśchikam
(Kārttigai) 24, śu. 7 Sadaiyam = 1186 A.D., November 20.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Puyal-
vāyttu vaḷam peruga, etc. It mentions the queen Buvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records a gift of 5 vēli of land in the kuḍippaṟṟu in Śiṟṟiṉgūr to provide
for food-offerings during the attaśāmam (ardhajāmam) and the waking up
(paḷḷiyeḻuchchi) services in the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavardēvar of
Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷa-
nāḍu, by Malaiyamāṉ sūriyan Ṉirēṟṟāṉ Edirikaṇāyaṉ alias Rājarājamalai-
yakularāyan of Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-nāḍu. It is stated that from the
area where the gift land is situated the lands previously gifted as dēvadāna
were exempted, and present gift was given as dēvadāna without removing the
tenants cultivating it.

l.ta:
puyal vāyttu vaḷam perukap poyyātu nāṉ maṟaiyuñ ceyal
vāyppat tirumakaḷuñ jeyamakaḷuñ ciṟantu vāḻa veṇ-

mati poṟkuṭai viḷaṅka velventaraṭi vaṇaṅki maṇmaṭantai maṉaṅkaḷippa maṉu-
niti taḻaittoṅkac cakkaramuñ ceṅkolu-
n tikkaṉaittuñ cela naṭappa poṟpamainta muṭi puṉaintu cempoṉ vira-

yum viyāḻakkiḻamaiyum peṟṟa catai-
yattu nāḷ

yañ ciṟṟiṅkūrāḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavartevaṟkku tirumuṉaip-
pāṭināṭṭu kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cūriyaṉireṟṟāṉ etirikaṇāyaṉāṉa

malaiyakularāyaneṉ ittevaṟkku tiruvattacāmattukkum tiruppaḷḷiyeḻiccikkum
tiruvamutupaṭi celvatāka ciṟṟiṅkūr kuṭippaṟṟil viṭṭa nilattukku melpāṟkel-

lai [ti]ruppulippakavan vatikkuk kiḻakkuna teṉpāṟkellai kiḻakku nokkip pokiṟa

vaṭapāṟkellai maṇaṅko-

na[mu]kkālukkum maṉai pattu nālvaṉukkum cerikuṉṟukkum vaṭapuka ••• cuk-

veliyuṅ kuṭiniṅkāta tevatāṉamākavu maramañcāṭipaṭi kāvalāḷamañciyu maṟṟu-

ttukku tan miṇāṭṭiyaik kuṭuppān ittanmam iṟakkuvār itu panmā

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 401 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with pūmādu
puṇara puvimādu vaḷara, etc. It mentions the queen Tiribuvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records the gift of two vēli of land, after purchase, as iṟaiyili dēvadāna
for worship and offerings to the image of the goddess of the holy bed-room in
the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar in Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam
in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Miḷāḍu in Irājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by a certain Āḷavandāḷ,
daughter of Malaiyamāṉ Nānūṟṟuvaṉ Malaiyan alias Irājēṉdiraśōḻach-
chēdiyarāyan of Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi-Dāma-nāḍu, and wife of Malaiya-
mān Irāman Śūṟṟiyan alias Irājēndiraśōḻa Malaiyakularāyan of the same place.
It is stated that she caused the above-mentioned image to be set up for the
merit of her mother (āchchi) Uḍaiyān Sūryadēvi alias Niṟaitavañjeydāḷ. It
is stated that the land was purchased by Āḷavandāḷ through Vikiramaśōḻa-
chēdiyarāyaṉ, son of Irājavirājēndiraśōḻach-chēdiyarāyaṉ. Of the two vēli
of land, one vēli in Śiṟṟiṅgūr was to be a kuḍi-nīṅgā dēvadāna and to be culti-
vated by the sabhai of the place who should pay the income to the god. It
is stated that the donor also gave two kāśu to the sabhai in this connection.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇarap puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jayamātu virumpat
tantiruppatumalar maṉnavar cūṭa manniyavurimaiyāl maṇimuṭicūṭic ceṅkolceṉṟu
ticaitoṟum vaḷaṟppa veṅkalinikki me[yyaṟan taḻaippakkali]ṅka miriyakkaṭal
malai utaitu [valaṅkoḷḷ]āḻivarai yāḻi naṭātti irucuṭaraḷavum oru kuṭai niḻaṟṟac
cempon vira

yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭai
yār tiruppulippakavatevaṟku tirumuṉaippāṭit tāmanāṭṭukkiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ
nānūṟṟuvaṉ malaiyanāṉa irājeṉtiracoḻaccetiya rāyan makaḷ ikkiḷi-
yūr malaiyamānar irāman cūṟṟiyanāṉa irājentiracoḻamalaiyakularāyanakamuṭai
yāḷ āḷavantāḷ taṅkaḷ ācci uṭaiyān cūriyateviyāṉa niṟaitavañcey
tāḷukkāka eḻuntaruḷu tiruppaḷḷiyaṟai nācciyāṟku tiruppaṭi māṟṟuḷḷi-

ṭṭa nimaṉtaṅkaḷukkut tevatāṉamāka irājavirājentiracoḻaccetiyarāyan makan •
•• [yā]ṉa vikkiramacoḻaccetiyarāyaṉaiyiṭṭu ivvāḷavantāḷ iṟaiyili teva-
tāṉamākak koṇṭu viṭuvitta nilattukku kiḻpāṟkellai tiruppulippakavavatikku
meṟku •• ppumālai taṭiyiraṇṭukku meṟkut tenpāṟkellai turavoṭu
cekkum marutaṅkaḻanikkum karuppukkālukkum aracaṅkuṇṭilukkum viḷā[ka*]ttuk-
kālukkum naṭakkālukkum taṇṇāmpaṟavaikkum kuṇṭikai mukkukkuṭi iṭai-

peruceykkum kamukaṭikkum vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai taṇṇāmpaṟavaikku nirpāy-
n ••• kkālukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai aiymperuṅkālukkut teṟkum
āka ivvicai

nāṟperellaiyil uḷḷakappaṭṭa nilam oru veliyum kuṭiniṅ-
kāt tevatānamāy icciṟṟiṅkūril

yārey uḻutu tevaṟkukkaṭamai
iṟukka kaṭavā[r*]kaḷākavum tiruppulippakavavatikkuk kiḻakku viṭṭa nilattukku
kiḻpāṟkellai talaippāṭakattukku nirpā[y*]

llai nārāṇataṭi iraṇṭukkum pirappañcey teṟkil taṭikkum aimperuṅkālukku
vaṭakkum melpāṟkkellai tiruppulippakavatikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai putut
tiruttukkum ūḷaikkum tirunilakaṇṭanukkum caṭaiyanārmeṭṭukku teṟkum āka
ivvicainta nāṟpperellai yuḷḷakappaṭṭa nilam oru veliyum āka nilam iru
veli iṉnilam iruvelikkum ivvūr

yāṟku iṭṭa kācu iraṇṭu in
nilam cantirātittavaṟ celvatāka ceyvittāḷ kiḷiyūr malaiyamāna irāman
cūṟṟiyanāna irāje

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 402 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva II:
Year 13, 127th day = 1145-46 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pūmēvu
vaḷar tirupponmādupuṇara, etc. and mentions the queen Buvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records the gift, made by the king, and executed by Tribhuvaṉachakra-
vartti Kōnēriṉmai-koṇḍān, of the village Aḍaṟpāḍi Mēyūr alias Nallāvūr in
Kūḍal in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, yielding an income of 155(1/2) māḍai of money,
as iṟaiyili dēvadāna for worship and services to be done by the servants of the
deity and the superintendents of the temple affairs, to the god Mahādēvar
Tiruppulippagavar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in
Milāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu. The name of the gift-village was changed
into Śivapādaśekaranallūr. It is stated that this gift of the village with the
money income therefrom was registered in the official records by Anapāya
Mūvēndavēḷān, the tirumandira-vōlai. A certain Malaiyapparāyaṉ figures
as the signatory.

l.ta:
pūmevuvaḷar tirupponmātu puṇara nāmevu kalaimakaḷ nalam peritu
ciṟappa vijayamāmakaḷ velpuyat tiruppa ic

yin celviyum eṇṭicaiy viḷaṅka
[nirupar] vantiṟaiñca niṇilamaṭaṉtaiy

tu cirvaḷar taruma
maṇimuṭi kavittenat tirumuṭicūṭi mallai ñālattup palluḻikkellām ellaiyi-
linpamika viniteta veṇ-
kuṭainiḻaṟ kiḻc ceṅkolocci vāḻi pallūḻi āḻinaṭappac cempon vira

vatti koneriṉmaikoṇṭān milā-
ṭāna jananātavaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu

k kaṇkāṇi ceyvār-
kaḷukkum ittevaṟku veṇṭum nimantaṅkaḷukku iṟuppatāka rājarājavaḷanāṭṭuk
kūṭalilāpaṟpāṭi meyūrāṉa nallāvūr māṭai nūṟṟaimpattañcaraiyum patinmun-
ṟāvatumutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyiliyāka iṭṭu paḻam peyar tavirntu civapātacekara-
nallūr enum perāl variyiliṭṭu variyilārum varikku kūṟu ceyvār-

kaḷum eḻuttiṭṭa uḷvaritarac connom ivvūr iṟukkum māṭai nūṟṟaimpat-
tañcaraiyum patinmunṟāvatu mutal tevatāṉa iṟaiyili ākak kaikkoṇṭu it-
teṟku veṇṭum nimantañ celuttuka eḻutinān tirumantiraōlai anpāya
muventaveḷān ivai malaiyapparāyaṉ eḻuttu yāṇṭu

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 403 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same prākāra.
This inscription is built in at the bottom.

It records the sale of 1,500 kuḻi of land for a sum of 15 Aṉṟāḍu Naṟkāśu,
to the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar in Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēya,
in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, by certain people
of the same place, named Bhāradvāji Kumārasvāmi Tiruppulipagavaṉ,
Āttraiyan Tiruvaṇṇāmalai-uḍaiyān Uyyavandāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ and Āttraiyan
Veṇkāḍudēvan Akalaṅkan.

l.ta:
ivvaṉaivom nilavilaiyāvaṇam ivvūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavatevar
koyilil ā

ratevarkku nāṅkaḷ viṟṟukkuṭutta nilamāvatu ivvūrk kiḻkaḻaniyil
veṇkāṭutevaṉ akalaṅkaṉ

llaiyil kuḻi aiññūṟum ākakuḻi āyirattu aiññūṟum viṟpataṟkku kiḻpāṟk
kellai

meṟkum teṉpāṟkkellai kaḷar kuṇṭilukku vaṭakkum melpākkellai veṇ-
kāṭu tevaṉ akala-

ṅkaṉ uḷḷiṭṭa ceṅkollaikku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkkellai ūṟukāl vāykālukku
teṟkum āka iv-

vicainta nāṟpper ellaiyuḷ[ḷa]kappaṭṭa nilam uḷnilam oḻiviṉṟi patiṉaṟu
cāṇkolāl kuḻi āyirattaiññū-

ṟum viṟṟuk kuṭuttukkoḷḷauṭaiya emmilicainta vilaipporu(ḷ)ḷaṉṟāṭu naṟk-
kācu

ṉañcum āvaṇakkaḷiye ciḻikaiccela aṟakkoṇṭu veṭṭi muṭṭaiyāḷ ec-
coṟu uḷḷiṭṭa cilliṟai

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 404 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king, commencing with Pūmēvu
vaḷar tiruppoṉmādu puṇara, etc., and mentions the queen Buvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records a gift, made by the king and executed by Tribhuvanachakravatti
Kōnēriṉmaikoṇḍāṉ, of 10 vēli of land as tax-free dēvadāna in Āḍaṟpāḍi
alias [Kāma]vāṉavan-mādiripuram in Kūḍal in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, for
tiruppaḍimāṟṟu and other services in the temple of the god Tiruppulīśvara-
mahādēvar of Siṟṟiṅgūr in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu. A
certain Villavarayaṉ figures as the signatory.

edition.l:
||—pūmevuvaḷar tiruppoṉmātu puṇara nāmevu kalaikaḷ nalam peritu ciṟappa
vijaiyamāmakaḷ velpuyat tiru[ppayi]caiyin celvi[yum eṇ]ṭicai viḷaṅka
nirupar vantiṟaiñca nini[la]maṭantaiyait tirumaṇam pu[ ṇarntu] cirvaḷar taruma
maṇimuṭi kavitteṉa maṇimuṭicūṭi mallai ñālattu palluyiṟkellā mellai
yiliṉpam kaviṉteyti veṇkuṭai niḻaṟkiḻc ceṅkolocci vāḻipallūḻi
āḻinaṭappa

vanaccakkaravattikaḷ śrī kulottuṅkacoḻate-

veliyuṅ kaikkoṇṭu tiruppaṭimā-

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 405 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is slightly damaged.

It records the sale of 455 kuḻi of land for a sum of 4(1/2) Aṉṟāḍu Naṟkāśu by
two persons named Bhāradvāji Nakkapirān Tiruppulipagavaṉ and Kāśyapan
Tiruvaṇṇāmalai Kumārasvāmi of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-
kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, to the temple of the god Tirup-
pulippagavardēvar of the same place.

miyum ivviruvo[m*] nilavilaiyāvaṇam ivvūr āḷuṭaiy[ā*]r tiruppulip-
pakavartevar koyilil ātitā

rkkellai puṟakālukku meṟkun tenpārkkellai matirāntaka vāykkālukku
vaṭakkum melpārkkellai melaippuṟakālukku kiḻakkum vaṭapārkkellai nāṇaṟ-
kāl velikku teṟkum āka ivvicainta nāṟperellai uḷ[ḷa*]kappaṭṭa nilam

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 406 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the same prākāra.

p.b:
Tribhº Kulōttuṅgachōḷadēva II: Year 10, Mithuna
(Āṉi) 29 = 1143 A.D., June 23, (Wednesday).

inscription.p:
This inscription which is slightly damaged contains the praśasti of the king
commencing with Pūmēvu vaḷar Tirupponmādu, etc., and mentions the queen
Buvanimuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records the gift of 5 kāśu, 2(1/2) kāśu each by [••• •••••] Īśvaradēvan and
by Kauśikan Ārāvamudu Panmanābhan of Śiṟṟiṅgūr for two sandhi lamps to
be burnt in the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of the same Śiṟṟiṅgūr,
a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. It
is stated that the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple received the money and agreed
to burn the lamps.

pūmevuvaḷar tirupponamātu puṇara nāmevu kalai[ma*]kaḷ nalam
peritu ciṟappa vijayamāmakaḷ velpuyattiruppa icaiyin celviyumeṇṭicai
viḷaṅka nirupar

t tirumaṇampuṇarntu cirvaḷar tarumam aṇimuṭi kavittena
maṇimuṭi cūṭimallai nālattup palluyirkkellā melḷaiyilinpamiyalvinileyti
veṇkuṭ
ṅkolocci vāḻi pallūḻi āḻi naṭappac cempon vira

ṉanāyaṟṟut tiyati irupattoṉpatiṉāl rājarājavaḷanāṭṭu malāṭṭuk
kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu

yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr āḷūṭaiyār tiruppulippakavatevar
koyilil civa

tevan pakkal nāṅkaḷ koṇṭa kācu
iraṇṭar

yum āka kācu ai[ñ*]cu ikkācu aiñcuṅkoṇṭu ivai mutal iraṇṭu
viḷakku

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 407 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 16 kāśu to the temple of the god Tiruppuli-
pagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkai-kuṟṟam in Malāḍu
in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by the Brāhmaṇī Āṭkoṇḍāṉ-śāṇi, wife of the Śivabrāhmaṇa
Kāśyapan Periyāṇḍā[ṉ] of the same place, in connection with the setting up
by her of an image of the deity Kshētrapālap-piḷḷaiyār and consecrating it in
the said temple. Besides, a sum of 18 kāśu was also deposited in the temple
by her to provide for food-offerings to the same image during a sandhi.

l.ta:
patti ceykaikku śrī paṇṭā-
rattu oṭukkiṉa kācu patināṟu piḷḷaiyāṟku oru

ḷukaikku veṇṭuvaṉa ittevar śrī paṇṭārattile vaṭṭu celuttukaikku śrī
paṇṭārattu nāṉ oṭukkiṉa kācu patineṭṭu ikkācu patineṭṭum oṭukki

ttavaṟ celvatāka ceytu viṭṭen āṭkoṇṭāṉ cāṉiyeṉ itu
panmāhe

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 408 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pūmāḍu
puṇara puvimāḍu vaḷara, etc., and mentions the queen Mukkōkkiḻānaḍigaḷ.

It records a gift of land, by purchase, 500 kuḻi in extent, as dēvadāna to
the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam in
Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananātha-vaḷanāḍu, by Nīlisiṅgi, the wife
of Malaiyamān Tirukkalaimarundu alias Āḻvānaṅgakkāṟa Malaiyamāṉ of
Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍit-Dāma-nāḍu. The gift was intended for various
items of worship and offerings to the above-mentioned deity for the processions
conducted on all Amāvāsī days, fifty two in number, in a year. The land was
purchased from the mahāsabhai of Śiṟṟiṅgūr for a sum of 3 Aṉṟāḍu Naṟkāśu
by the donor. The inscription gives a detailed list of articles offered at the
worship and their equivalents in rice.

l.ta:
pūmātu puṇara puvimātu vaḷara nāmātu viḷaṅka jeyamātu virumpa
tan irupatu malar maṉṉavar cūṭa maṉniya urimaiyāl maṇimuṭi cūṭi ceṅ-
kol cenṟu ti-

cai toṟum vaḷarppa veṅkali niṅki veyaṟn taḻaippak kaliṅka miriyakkaṭal
malai utaittu valaṅkollāḻi varaiyāḻi naṭātti i[ru]cuṭaraḷavum oru kuṭai
niḻaṟṟi-
niyil koṉṟa mañci vaṭakkiṟ taṭi viṟṟukkuṭutta paricāvatu ittevarku amāvā

eḻuntaruḷukaikku tirumantirappoṉakattukku tiruvamutu arici nāṉāḻiyum eḻun-
taruḷip pukuntāl amutu
ceytaruḷat tiruvamutu arici kuṟuṇiyum āḷuṭaiyārkku eṟupaṭi

nti onṟukku
tiruvamutu arici nāṉāḻiyum caṭṭicoṟu [aiñcu]kku arici aiynāḻiyum āka
arici patakku aiynāḻikku nellut tūṇip

patakku nāṉāḻi uriyum kaṟiyamutu muppatiṉ palattukku nellu aiññāḻiyum
miḷakamutu iruceviṭaraikkum uppamutu urikkum nellu nāṉāḻiyum neyyamutu
uḻakkukkum tayiramutu irunāḻikkum ne-

lluk kuṟuṇi irunāḻiyum aṭaikkāyamutu pattukkum ilaiyamutu irupatukkum
nellu muṉṉāḻiyum tiruviḷakku eṇṇai aiññāḻikku nelluk kalaṉe muk-
kuṟuṇiyum āka tirup[li]ppapukavaṉ
marakkālāl nellu irukalaṉe muṉnāḻi āka amāvā

paṉniraṇṭukku nellu
irupattu nāṟkalaṉe tūṇi nāṉāḻikku tirumuṉaippāṭit tāmanāṭṭuk kiḷiyūr
malaiyamān tirukkalaimarun-

tāṉa āḻvānaṅkakkāṟa malaiyamāṉ akamuṭaiyāḷ niliceṅki koṇṭu viṭṭa
koṉṟa mañciyāṉa nilattukku kiḻpārkkellai ittevar tevatāṉam tiru-
valaku kuṇṭilukku meṟkum teṉpāṟkel-

lai taṉmac ceykkum ittevar [u]yyapuṟak koṉṟa mañcikku teṟkum mel-
pāṟkellai teṟkunokkippoṉa kaṇṇikkālukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkkellai it-
tevar tevatānam pariccāmiccey-

kku teṟkum ākavivvicainta nāṟperellaiyuḷḷakappaṭṭa uḷnilam oḻiviṉṟi
patinaṟucāṇ kolāl kuḻi aiññūṟum tevatāṉamāka viṟṟuk kuṭuttu ivaḷ
pāṭukoṇṭa aṉṟāṭu naṟkā-
cu muṉṟu ikkācu muṉṟum aṟakkoṇṭu ittanmañ

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 409 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same prākāra.

p.b:
Tribhº
Rājarājadēva II: Year 7, Tulā (Aippaśi) 3 = 1153 A.D. September 29, Tuesday.

inscription.p:
This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pūmaru-
viya tirumādum Puvimādum, etc., and mentions the queen Ulaguḍai
Mukkōkkiḻāṉaḍigaḷ.

It records a gift of land for the purpose of raising a flower-garden to provide
for flower garlands to the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟingūr, a brahma-
dēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by Maṇalūruḍai-
yāṉ Tiruvagattipaḻippili, the madhyasthaṉ of Maṇalūr alias Kalyāṇamahā-
dēvichchaturvēdimaṅgalam, a brahmadēyam, in Śeṅguṉṟanāḍu of Vāṇa-
gōppāḍi on the northern bank of the Peṇṇai river, in Rājarājā-vaḷanāḍu. The
land is stated to have belonged to the donor who had purchased it from
Bhāradvāji Āṇḍapiḷḷai Tiruppulippagavar and others.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum puvimātum jeyamātum nāmaruviya kalaimātum
pukaḻmātu

tu pulka arumaṟai viti neṟi taḻaippa varumuṟai urimai maṇimuṭi cūṭit
tiṅkaḷ veṇkuṭait ticai-

kka[ḷi]ṟeṭṭun taṅku taṉik kūṭattāṉeṉ viḷaṅkak karuṅkalippaṭṭiyai ceṅkol
turappa poru ka-

tirāḻi puvi vaḷarntuṭaṉ varavillavari īṭṭar miṉavar ciṅkaḷar pallavar nutali-
yar pāttivar paṇiya eṇṇaruṅ kaṟpaka maṇ-

varkku yāṇṭu eḻāvatu tulā-
nāyaṟṟut tiyati muṉṟiṉāl
ṭup peṇṉey vaṭakaraiy vāṇakoppāṭic ceṅkuṉṟa nāṭṭu

ṉ maṇalūruṭaiyāṉ tiruvakatti paḻippiliyeṉ iṉnāṭṭu malāṭṭuk kuṟuk-
kaikkūṟṟattu
vaṟkku tiruppaḷḷittāmañ cātti aruḷukaikku nāṉ tiruna
lippakavar uḷḷiṭṭār pakkal ṉāṉ vilaikoṇṭuṭaiyeṉāy eṉṉutāy iru
nilam vaṭavaḻi eṉṟu perkūvappaṭṭa nilattil nāṉ tiruṉa

nilattukkuk kiḻpāṟkkellai ciṟṟiṅkūruṭaiyān comaṉ tirucciṟ-
ṟampalamuṭaiyāṉ vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya ṉilattukku meṟkkum teṉpāṟkkellai
ippaḻi-

var tevatāṉam vaṭavaḻi
toṇṭikkuk kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkkellai ā

ṭṭaṉ uḷḷiṭṭār aracettak kollaikku teṟkum
āka ivvicai

nāṟperelḷaiyuḷ ākappaṭṭa nilam patinaṟucāṇ kolāl kuḻi
nūṟum iṉṉilattil u-

ḷpaṭṭa meṉokkiṉa maramuṅ kiḻnokkiṉa kiṇaṟum akappaṭa tiruppaḷḷittāmañ
cāttiyaruḷukaikkut tiruna

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 410 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº
Vikramapāṇḍyadēva II: Year 6, Kaṉṉi (Puraṭṭāśi) śu. 1, Sunday,
Hastam = 1274 A.D., September 2, f.d.n. ·53 f.d.t. ·04.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 1,000 kuḻi of land in Śiṟṟiṅgūr in Kuṟukkai-
kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Irārāja-vaḷanāḍu, by the nagarattārs of the same place
to provide for food-offerings at the waking up service and the Pulavar-talaivan-
sandhi, in the temple of the god Tiruppulippagava-nāyaṉār of theplace. The
land is stated to be a gift, probably from the king, to the nagarattārs called
the Perumāḷ-kāṇi. It is stated that they made the gift as tax-free tirunā
mattukkāṇi. The village of Marudūr is mentioned as forming a boundary of
the gift land.

l.ta:
irājarājavaḷanāṭṭu malāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu ciṟṟiṅkūr uṭaiyār tirup
pulippakava-

nāyaṉāṟku ivvūr nakarattārom iṉṉāyaṉāṟku tiruppaḷḷieḻicci pulavar talaivaṉ
canti amu-
tu ceytaruḷa amutupaṭikkum paṭiveñcaṉaṅkaḷukkum viṭ[ṭa] nilamāvatu eṅkaḷukku

tañ ceytaruḷiṉa ivūril kūttāṇṭāḷ tiruttu
akaḻukku me-

ṟku iṉnāyaṉār tevatāṉam vākicantiruttukkum eṅkaḷ kāṇiyāṉa puṉcai
kuraṅku kuttikkum va-

ṭakku iṉnāyaṉār tevatāṉam marutūr ellaikku kiḻakku iṉnāyaṉār cey
kaṇṇikkālukku teṟku
iṉṉāṉkellaikku uṭpaṭṭa nilattuteṟkaṭaiya kiḻamel va[ṟ] vaṭṭa kuḻi
iṉnāḷ mutal kār pacāṉam uḷḷiṭṭa payirkaḷ aṉaittāyaṅkaḷum uṭpaṭa ca
lvatāka tirunāmattukkāṇi iṟaiyiliyāka viṭṭu kuṭuttom nakarattārom itu

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 411 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

The inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pūmaru-
viya tirumādum, etc., and mentions the queen Ulaguḍai Mukkōkkiḻānaḍigaḷ.

It records a gift of 4 vēli of land in Marudūr alias Ābharaṇadāṇinallūr in
Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu, by Malaiyamāṉ Attimallaṉ Śokkapperumāḷ alias Rāja-
gambhirachchēdiyarāyan of Kiḷiyūr in Tirumuṉaippāḍi in Rājarāja-vaḷa-
nāḍu, for food-offerings to the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a
brahmadēyam, in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. It is
stated that various incomes specified relating to the gift land need not be
paid and that the gift would be maintained hereditarily.

l.ta:
pūmaruviya tirumātum jeyamātum nāmaruviya kalaimātum pukaḻmātum
naya

viti neṟi taḻaippa varumuṟai urimai maṇimuṭi cūṭi tiṅkaḷ veṇkuṭait ticaik kaḷi
ṟeṭṭu taṅku tani kū-

neṉ viḷaṅka karuṅkalippaṭiyaic ceṅkol turappa poruṅ[ka]tirāḻi puvi
vaḷarntuṭaṉavar vil-

lavarriṭṭar miṉavar ciṅkaḷar pallavar nutaliyar pārttivar paṇiya eṇṇaruṅ kaṟ
piṉ maṇṇakam puṇar

paṭi cennel amutuceytaruḷukaikku nāṉ viṭṭa nilamāvatu innāṭṭu marutūrāna
āparaṇatāṇi nal-

lū •• ṇam pokivatikku kiḻakku viṭṭa nilam mūnṟarai velikku kiḻpāṟkellai
ittevar tevatāṉa

••• kūṭṭattukkum pelipaṭṭikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkkellai tūṟṟaṟumāvukkum
ilattai yet-

tattukkum oṭaikkum teṟkil vāykkālukku vaṭakkum melpāṟkellai iv-
vilattai ēttattu mel

varampukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ivvilattaiyettattukkum perettattukku
pūṅkettattu-

kkum māñceṟuvukkum paṭappaic ceṟuvukku vamutu varampukku teṟkum āka
ivvicainta nāṟpere-

llaiyuḷ akappaṭṭa nilam patinālarai aṭik kolāl nilam muṉṟaraiveliyum
ivvatikku meṟku atta na-

ḷam ponnūr ellaikku kiḻakku koṟṟavāyileṉṟu peruṭaiya taṭi palavināl
ikkolāl nila-
m araiveliyum āka nilam nālu veliyum

yam perum-
ic cokkap pāṭikāval ciṟu-

pāṭikāval uḷḷiṭṭa āyaṅkaḷum āḷum amañciyum iṟukka [ka*]ṭavatallātatāka
ippaṭi eṅka-

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 412 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records that a bell weighing 50 (palam or some such word
is omitted), to the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar by a dēvaraḍiyār (dancing girl)
named Aṭṭiyār alias Vāgīśanaṅgai.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 413 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is built in at the bottom and hence the details of the gift
are not available now.

It, however, seems to record a gift of taxes on weavers' looms, etc., situated
in the streets around the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr
a brahmadēśam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Irājarāja-vaḷanāḍu and
probably income from Vikkirama-śōḻanallūr also, to the same god, by a certain
Ēḻiśaimōhan alias Jananātha-Kachchiyarāyaṉ, son of Āḷappiṟandān Araśanā-
rāyaṇaṉ of Kūḍalūr. (See S.I.I., Vol. XII, p. 170, No. 9).

edition.l: ku yāṇṭu 6- vatu kūṭalūr āḷappiṟantān aracanārāyaṇaṉ ma-

l.ta: ttu nālucikkarattu uṭpaṭṭiṉa kiḻiṟaippāṭṭamum taṟiiṟai taṭṭārpāṭṭa-
mum eṭuppiṉa [kūvina] aḷappiṉa niṟum • ṉavumellā • yittevark-

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 414 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Puyal
vāyppa vaḷam peruga, etc., and mentions the queen Buvaṉamuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records a gift of all incomes except the śiṟupāḍikāval, due from the
dēvadāna lands of the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavardēvar of Śiṟṟingūr
a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟan in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanādu to
Malaiyamāṉ Śokkapperumāḷ Poṉparappiṉāṉ alias Rājagambhīrach
chēdiyarāyaṉ, of Kiḷiyūr in the same nāḍu, by him to provide for the procession
during the festival of the above-mentioned god held in the month of Puraṭṭādi.

l.ta:
puyalvāyppa vaḷamperukap poyyāta nāṉmaṟaiyiṉ ceyalvāyppa
maṇamakaḷum jayamakaḷum [ma*]ṉaṅkaḷippa veṇpoṟkuṭai viḷaṅka velventaraṭi
viḷaṅka cakkaramuñ ceṅkolun tik-

kaṇaittuñ celanaṭappa maṉuniti taḻaittoṅka tirumakaḷum pārmakaḷuñ ciṟantu
vāḻa •• pakkakāl pulikāppa poṟpamainta muṭipuṉainta cempoṉ vira
varkku innāṭṭuk kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cokkapperumāḷ poṉparappiṉāṉa rāja-

ccetiyarāyaṉeṉ iṉṉāyaṉār puraṭṭātittirunāḷeḻuntaruḷukaikku ivvūr
tevar tevatāṉam nañcey pun-

ceykaḷil nāṅkaḷ koḷḷum āyaṅkaḷil ciṟupāṭikāval nikki maṟṟuḷḷa ep-
perppaṭṭa āyaṅkaḷum viṭṭen kiḷiyūr malaiyamāṉ cokkapperumāḷ poṉparap-
piṉāṉa rāja
ttiluḷḷār aḻivuceyvvār vallavaraiyaṉ cittam i[tu] pa

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 415 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of 700 kuḻi of land in Kiḷiyūr in Rājarāja-
vaḷanāḍu by the servant named Kūttan Paṉaiyaṉ alias Poṉparappiṉa
Malaiyamāṉ of the same place, purchased for 1 (1/4) kaḻañju of gold from the
sabhai of the same place, for two nandā lamps to be burnt in the temple of the
god Tiruppulippagavar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam
in Malāḍu in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu. It is stated that the gift of lamps was made
for the merit of the servant's master Rājagambhirachchēdiyarāyar who also
figures in No. 435 above. The lamps were received by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of
the temple who had agreed to burn them for ever.

l.note: Written in continuation of No. 413 above.

l.ta:
yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr āḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavarkku innāṭṭu kiḷiyūr malaiya-
māṉkaḷ kaṉmikaḷil kūttan paṉaiyaṉāṉa poṉparappiṉa malaiyamāṉeṉ eṅkaḷ
nāyaṉār rāja

l.note: Written one letter below the other.
Written one letter below the other.
l.ta:
kava-
ṉuḷḷiṭṭār toṇṭikku [meṟku] paḻantevatāṉattukku vaṭakku patinaṟu cāṇ
kolāl kuḻi

yārkkoṭukkiṉa pon kaḻañcekālippoṉ kaḻañce kāluṅ kaikkoṇṭu
viṟṟukkuṭutto mippaṉaiyaṉāṉa ponparappi
l.note: Written one letter below the other.

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 416 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is built in at the bottom.

It records a gift of a standing lamp made of bronze and bell-metal, a bell
and a vessel made of brass to the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of
Śiṟṟiṅgūr a brahmadēśam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Irājarāja-vaḷanāḍu
by a certain Śivadavaṉapperumāḷ •• Vāmaṉa-bhaṭṭaṉ [Vāḷappiṟaṉdā] Biramma-
rāyan, son of Kāśivaṉ Ādipurāṇaṉ Āṭkoṉṭāṉ bhaṭṭaṉ of Aiyyavēli belonging to
Pālaiyūr alias Irājanārāyanacharuppēdimaṅgalam. It is stated that the gift
was made for the merit of two dēvaraḍiyārs (dancing girls), named Śōḻiya-
nāchchi alias Poṉṉār Tiruvaḍimāṇikkam, daughter of Peṟṟāḷ of Śiṟṟiṅgūr,
and the former's daughter Āḷavandapiḷḷai alias Pirāṇattiyāgi-naṅgai.

l.ta: āḷuṭaiyār tiruppulippakavatevaṟku ivvūr tevaṟaṭiyāḷ peṟṟāḷ makaḷ
petimaṅkalattu aiyyaveli kācivaṉ ātipurāṇaṉ āṭkoṇṭāṉ paṭṭaṉ
makaṉ civatavaṉapperumāḷ •• vāmaṉa paṭṭaṉ [vāḷappiṟaṉtā] pirammarāyana i-
ṭṭa veṇkalamum tarāvum kūṭiṉa kuttuviḷakku kal iṭai[yā]l veṇkala-
•• maṇi kal iṭaiyāl pittaḷait tūṅkal iṭai

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 417 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XII, No. 207.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 418 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva I: Year 13, Mīna (Paṅkuṉi)
śn. 6, Saturday, Rōhiṇī = 1263 A.D., March 17. The tithi and nakshatra
ended on the previous day respectively at ·97 and ·22.

inscription.p: This inscription is damaged in some places.

It records that a certain Kaikkōḷa (name lost) of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēśam
in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Irājarājavaḷanāḍu, set up an image of
Āḷuḍaiyapiḷḷaiyār in front of the shrine of the god Tiruppulippagava-nāyaṉār
of the god's temple in Śiṟṟiṅgūr, and that the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the 40 circles
belonging to the temple had agreed to provide for food offerings and other
services to the above-mentioned image as well as for weeding out the grass
in the compound (śuṟṟālai), from out of the interest on 2,000 kāśu which they
had received from a certain Arindavaṉ Pallavaraiyar in the time of Kōpperuñ-
jiṅgadēvar.

l.ta: yaṉ eṟi aruḷappaṇṇiṉa āḷuṭaiyapiḷḷaiyārkku amutu ceytaruḷa
muṉṉāḷ kopperuñciṅkatevar nāḷil arintavaṉ pallavaraiyar pa-
m policaikku uṭalāka nāṅkaḷ amutu ceyvikkum nāḷ oṉṟukku
irunāḻi ariciyum veṇṭum kaṟiamutu veñcaṉamum(p) paṭaitta ••••

edition.l: ṇṭu tirumañcaṇamum paṇṇi tiruccuṟṟālai pullum tetti •••• [ candrādi]-

l.ta: tittavarai ceyvatāka intakācu kaikkoṇṭom ikkoyil nāṟpatu va-

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 419 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing with Pugaḻ-
mādu viḷaṅga, etc. and mentions the queen Avanimuḻuduḍaiyāḷ.

It records a gift of 64 cows on behalf of Malaiyamāṉ Śadiraṉ Malaiyaṉ alias
Rājēndraśōḻa Malaiyamāṉ of Kiḷiyūr on the southern bank of the Peṇṇai
river in Tirumuṉaippāḍi in Dāmar-nāḍu in Rājēndra-vaḷanāḍu for two nandā
lamps to be burnt in the temple of the god Tiruppulippagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr,
a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu alias Jananāthavaḷanāḍu.

l.ta:
pukaḻmātu viḷaṅka jeyamātu virumpa nilamakaḷ nilava malarmakaḷ puṇara
urimaiyiṟ ciṟaṉta maṇimuṭicūṭi villavar nilaikeṭa miṉavar kulaitara eṉai maṉ
navar iriyaluṟṟuḻittarat tikkaṉaittun taṉcakkaranaṭātti vijaiya

vaḷanāṭṭuttāmar nāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭip peṇṇait teṉkaraikkiḷiyūr malaiya
māṉ catiraṉ malaiyaṉāṉa

coḻa malaiyamāṉaic cārtti malāṭāṉa jaṉa-
nātavaḷanāṭṭuk kuṟukkaikkūṟṟattu

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 420 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 250.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 421 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 227.

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 422 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIX, No. 182.

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 423 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription is built in at the bottom and hence a few details of the
gift are not avilable.

It records a gift of a sandhi lamp to the temple of the god Tiruppulippa-
gavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a brahmadēyam, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam, in Malāḍu alias
Jananātha-vaḷanāḍū, by a certain Śemmāṟuḍaiyān Śōḻan Vichchādiran of
Śemmāṟu in Ēmappeṟṟūr-nāḍu in Tirumuṉaippāḍi in Gaṅgaikoṇḍaśōḻa-vaḷanāḍu.
It appears that he endowed some money in respect of this, which was accepted
by two Śivabrāhmaṇas of the temple, named Bhāradvāja Puliyan Śivabattaṉ
alias Nāṉūṟṟuva bhaṭṭaṉ and Bhāradvāja Marundu Poṟkōyil-nambi.

edition.l:
varkku kaṅkaikoṇṭacoḻavaḷanāṭṭut tirumuṉaippāṭi emappeṟṟūrnāṭṭuc cem-
māṟṟuc cemmāṟuṭaiyān coḻan viccātiran vaitta santi viḷakku 1 onṟukkum
ikkoyil civabrāhmaṇana bhāradvāja puliyan civapattaṉāṉa nāṉūṟṟuva bhaṭṭa[ ṉum
bh] āradvāja maruntu poṟkoyil nampiyum ivviru Continuation built in.

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 424 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: In the same place as above.
This inscription is incomplete in some places.

It records a settlement arrived at amongst the Brāhmaṇas and
Periyanāṭṭars residing on either bank of the kuḍakkāl (small canal) through
which water flows into the channel called the Tiruppuli(ppagava) ••
ṉūṟṟukkāl dug out by a certain Āṇḍār Araśukkaḍiyār for the god Tiruppulip-
pagavadēvar of Śiṟṟiṉgūr a brahmadeyam. The settlement appears to have been
over a dispute concerning water supplied from the canal of Marudūr.

l.ta:
vaṟku āṇṭār aracuk-
kaṭiyār veṭṭuvitta tiru[p]puli •••• ṉūṟṟukālukku manta kuṭakkāl potu-

ṇarum periyanāṭṭārum ā ••• ••• kūṭa
marutūrkkāl nīrtāḻvupaṭāmalellai kuṟittapaṭi jaṉaṉātaṉ mukattukkuk kiḻa[k*]kup
patiṉaiṅkolitaṉ meṟku muṉṉūṟṟai[mpatiṉ] kolākavumiraṇṭu kolukku naṭu-
vu patinaṟu kolākavu mippaṭi coṉṉomivvaṉaivo ••• •••• māhe

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 425 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
The meaning of this inscription is not very clear.

It appears to record that the temple trustees (tānattār), the sabhai and the
ūr of Śiṟṟiṅgūr a brahmadēyam in Kuṟukkaik-kūṟṟam in Rājarāja-vaḷanāḍu,
agreed to have Parāśivaṉ in Śiṟṟiṅgur as desired by the Parāśivar of
Tiruvadigai, Tiruttuṟai, Tiruveṇṇainallūr, Tiruvōmāttūr and Tirumudu-
kuṉṟam, all in Naḍuvil-maṇḍalam.

l.ta: tūr tirumutukuṉṟam ivūparācivar ceyyumpaṭi kalveṭṭik kuṭuttapaṭiye ivū-

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 426 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is built in at the bottom.

It records a gift of 500 kuḻi of land as iṟaiyili in [Tiruppulippagava]lūr for a
nandā lamp to the god Tiruppulippagavar, by a certain Maṉṉari Tiruppulip-
pērāṉ, son of Dēvapperumāḷ while Taraṉikkamaṅgaraśaiyaṉ was ruling over
the Tiruvadi-rājyam. It is stated that the land was probably left in charge of a
certain Kāḷi Kaḷappālaṉ.

l.ta: irācciyam cellu[kaiyil tevap]perumāḷ makaṉ maṉṉari tiruppuli-
kku tirunantāviḷakku o[ṉṟu]kku kāḷi kaḷappāḷaṉ tirutti[li]ṟai ili āka

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 427 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a gift of a bronze carrier for food, weighing 60
palam, by a certain Kaṇṇaṉ Śūraṉ alias Ākārasūra Malaiyamān to the god
Tiruppulippagavadēvar.

l.ta: ṉa ākāracūra malaiyamāṉ iṭṭa veṇkalat taḷikai o-

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar of a maṇḍapa in the same temple.

This inscription records that this pillar (on which the inscription is
engraved) was caused to be made by a certain Vaṁbuyiḻuttāḷ, daughter of
Iḍappāgaṅkoṇḍāḷ a dēvaraḍiyāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 428 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Another pillar in the same place.

This inscription records that this pillar (on which the inscription is
engraved, was made (caused to be made ?) by a certain Tillaināyiramāṇikkam,
who was the husband's sister of Uyyavandāḷ, a dēvaraḍiyāḷ.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 428 B of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A third pillar in the same place.

This inscription records that this pillar (on which this inscription is engraved)
was caused to be made by a woman named Aḍaikkalamteḷḷaḷ, a member of the
oil-monger community (vāṇiyaḷ).

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 429 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the Vāhana-maṇḍapa in the same temple.

This inscription records the grant, as tax-free dēvadāna, of a number of
taxes such as taṟi-iṟai, śēttiṟai, etc., levied by the nāṭṭārs from the residents
of the tirumaḍai-viḷāgam of the temple of Tiruppulippagavanāyaṉār of
Śiṟṟiṅgūr a brahmadēśam, in Kuṟukkai-kūṟṟam in Malāḍu in Irājarāja-vaḷanāḍu,
made by the king to provide for a service, named after the king, to the god,
instituted by a certain Kūttapperumāḷ, an aṅgavaidya and for a festival of the
same deity. It is stated that this royal order was addressed to the tāṉattār
(trustees) of the temple. A certain Tuñjalūruḍaiyāṉ and Pallavarāyar
figure as signatories.

l.ta:
tiruppulippulippakavanāyaṉār koyil tāṉattārkku iṉṉāyaṉārkku nam perāl
aṅkavayi

rilk kūttapperumāḷ kaṭṭiṉa cantikkum tirunāḷukku uṭalāka iṉ-
ṉāyaṉār tirumaṭaiviḷākattu irukku-

m kuṭimakkaḷ pakkal nāṭṭār koṇṭu potum taṟi iṟai ceṭṭiṟai ācu potu-
makkaḷaporkkaṭamai vaṭa • kkal erimiṉpāṭṭam cekku āyam taraku vācalappaṇam

maṭaiviḷākam nāṉ[kel]

tuñcalūruṭaiyāṉ eḻuttu
ivai pallavarāyar eḻuttu itu pa

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 430 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Nīlaviśālākshi-ammaṉ shrine in the same temple.

p.b: Tribhº Rājarājadēva Rājarāja III: Year 15 = 1237 A.D.

inscription.p: This inscription is damaged, so several details are lost.

It appears to record a sale of land in connection with a certain consecration
ceremony to some persons like Periyāṉ Pērayaṉ of a certain nāḍu (not specified)
by Paḷḷi-bhaṭṭaṉ Śōḻaṉ alias Periṅgūrp-pērayaṉ, a resident of Śiṟṟiṅgūr, a
brahmadēyam.

l.ta: yañ ciṟṟiṅkūr kuṭi paḷḷipaṭṭaṉ coḻaṉāṉa periṅkūrpperayaṉe-
ṉ innāṭṭu oḻukaraipa ••• ••• kku mivan tampi periyān perayaṉu-

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 431 of 1909.)
SIDDHALIṄGAMAḌAM, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A slab set up in the Brahmin street.
This inscription is in modern characters.

It records that the members of the nāḍus of Vaḻudalampaṭṭu-chāvaḍi,
Paḍaivīṭṭu-chāvaḍi, Tiruchchirappaḻḻich chāvaḍi, and other nāḍus assembled
and decided to continue certain privileges granted during the time of Dēvarā-
yar-mahārāyar, to a certain maṭha called Śiṟṟiṅga-maṭha belonging probably
to the Iḍaṅgai community of the above-mentioned territories. Signatories
were the mudalis of the various nāḍus and Meykāval Śōḻagaṉār of the Iḍaṅgai
community.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 432 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the Ādiliṅgēśvara temple.

This inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script is damaged. It records a gift of
sheep for two perpetual lamps to the god of the temple of Śūraṅguḍi-nāṭṭī-
chchuvaram of Ādaṉūr in Śūraṅguḍi-nāḍu by •••• kaṉ-Araiyaṉ on behalf
of two persons including Eṭṭi. The gift of 25 sheep each was entrusted to
four individuals including •••• kkan-Tama[rā]kki, Kaṇakkaṉ Iruṅkīraṉ,
Kaṇattāreṭṭi alias Seṅgōḍaṉ-Maśaiyaṉ, a shepherd from Perumpulivalam.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 433 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script is damaged and incomplete.

It records a gift of 25 sheep for a lamp to the god of the temple Nāṭṭīs-
varam of Ādaṉūr in Śūraṅguḍi-nāḍu, by a certain Śiṟiya Araiyaṉ Māṟaṉ of
the same place. A certain shepherd of the place is stated to have received
the sheep.

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 434 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the south wall of the Ādiliṅgēśvara temple.

p.b:
Māṟavarmaṉ-Vikrama-chōḷa-pāṇḍya-dēva (Junior contemporary of Jaṭº
Sundº chōḷa-pāṇḍya): Year 20 = 1047-48 (?)

inscription.p: Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 183.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 435 of 1909.)
ĀDAṆŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.
This inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script is damaged and incomplete.
It mentions Irāśarāśa-maṇḍalam. Other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 436 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.
This inscription is too fragmentary.

It mentions Āda[ṉūr] in Śūraṅguḍi-nāḍu and Muduguḍi-nāḍu and some
people like Pavittiramāṇi[kkam] Gaṅgadēvaṉ. It appears to record a gift
of a nandā lamp.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 437 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: West wall of the same temple.
This inscription in Vaṭṭeḻuttu script of 10th century is a fragment.

It appears to record a gift of a nandā lamp on behalf of a certain
Kaṇakkaṅ-kiḻavaṉ alias Puruḻamāṇikka-śeṭṭi by the ūrār who seem to have
given 2 mā of land in this behalf to a certain Piritip-pēraiyaṉ of Ādanūr.

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 438 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north and west walls of the same temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 174.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 439 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: On the north wall of the same temple.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 142.

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 440 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar lying in front of the same temple.
This inscription is in Veṭṭeḻuttu script.

It records a gift of two pillars (on one of which this inscription is engraved)
by a certain Āmbalaṅkōḍaṉ of Śellūr in Muḻaiyu-nāḍu in Śōṇāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 441 of 1909.)
ĀDAṈŪR, KOILPATTI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: A pillar of a maṇḍapa in front of the same temple.

This inscription records a gift of this pillar (i.e., the one on which this ins-
cription is engraved), by Iṉiyāṉ Kūṭṭaṉ alias Ovāda-kūttaṉ of [Kā]ttipāḍi].

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 442 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
South wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Muṇdīś-
varamuḍaiyār temple.

p.b:
Māṛaº Vīra Pāṇḍya (?): Kollam 616, Āṉi 4, śu. 11, Wednesday, Svātī =
1441 A.D. May 31.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 100 paṇam for a nandā lamp to be burnt
in the temple of the god Mūṉṟiśvaramuḍaiyār of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias
Maṇappaḍaivīḍu in the north-east quarter of Śrivallavaṉmaṅgalam, a
brahmadēśam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, by a trustee of the temple named Māttāṇḍaṉ-
Perumāṉ alias Villavarāyar. The gift money was accepted by Śrīkailāya-
muḍaiyāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ Aṉavaratadānapperumāḷ, Tirunelvēliuḍaiyār, Veṟṟikkuvēl-
eḍuttaperumāḷ Irāśākumāra-bhaṭṭaṉ and Peṟṟaperumāḷ-Aṟamvaḷatār the
dēvakaṉmis of the 30 circles belonging to the temple. These persons had
agreed to burn the lamp hereditarily out of the interest on the gift money.

At the end of the record, besides the above mentioned people, Mūṉrīśura
Mūppaṉ and Nāṇambi Ayyaṉ Villavarāya Āśāri, the siṟpi (sculptor) of the
village, also figure as signatories.

m śrīvallavaṉ-
maṅkalattu vaṭakiḻ piṭākai ampala-

paṭi kaṟpittu piṭipāṭu paṇṇikkalluveṭṭi kuṭuttamaikku ippaṭikku śrīkailāya
muṭaiyāṉpaṭṭaṉ aṉavaratatāṉapperumāḷ eḻuttu ippaṭikku meṟpaṭi

tirunelveli uṭaiyār eḻuttu ippaṭikku veṟṟikkuveleṭuttaperumāḷ
irācakumārapaṭṭaṉ eḻuttu ippaṭikku aṟamvaḷattār eḻuttu ippaṭikku muṉṟi-
cuṟamuppaṉ eḻuttu
ippaṭikku ivvūril ciṟpikaḷil nāṇampiayyaṉ villavarāya ācāri eḻuttu

inscription.head:
No. 466.
(A.R. No. 443 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Māṟaº Vira Pāṇḍya (?): Kollam 616, Āṉi 4, śu. 11, Wednesday, Svātī,
= 1441 A.D. May 31.

inscription.p:
This inscription records that the services of reciting the holy hymns and
the provision of a flower-hall in the temple of the god Mūṉṟīśvaramuḍaiya-
nayinār of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇappaḍaivīḍū in the north-eastern
quarter of Śrīvallavaṉmaṅgalam, a brahmadēśam, in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, were
arranged to be the proprietory right of the 15 circles (of the temple) by the
Bhikshā-maṭha. It states that the privileges accuring therefrom were to
be enjoyed by Uyyaneṟikāṭṭiṉar Vaṉattuvāḷvittār, a disciple of Āgamaśīla-
dēvar alias Umaiyorupāgar of the lineage of the Bhikshā-maṭhā, and his
successors. (cf: No. 471 below).

The signatories were Śrīkaiyilāyamuḍaiyāṉ-bhaṭṭaṉ Aṉavaratadāṉapperu-
māḷ of the temple, Tirunelvēliuḍaiyār, Veṟṟikkuvēleḍutta Perumāḷ
Irāśakumāra-bhaṭṭaṉ, Aṟamvaḷattār, Krēntīśvaramuḍaiyāṉ Mūṉṟīśvara-bhaṭṭaṉ
Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira Śīkaraṇattāṉ, Māttāṇḍāṉ-perumāḷ alias Villavarāyaṉ, the
temple trustee, and Nāṇambi Ayyaṉ Villavarāsar the siṟpi (sculptor), of
the place.

l.ta:
śrīvallavaṉmaṅkalattu vaṭakiḻ piṭākai ampalattāṭinallūrāṉa maṇappaṭai-
viṭṭil uṭaiyār muṉṟi

muṭaiyanayinār koyilil tiruppāṭṭum oti ik

itukku uḷḷa uṭaimaiyum peṟṟu pala

nivataṅkaḷukku uḷḷa peṟu peṟum

inscription.head:
No. 467.
(A.R. No. 444 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

p.b:
Kollam 719 or 711 Kārttigai 13, śu. 10, Tuesday, Uttirāḍam
Irregular = 1544-45 A.D.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of paddy and other articles monthly, for
worship and offering to the temples of the gods Kāṇaviniyaperumāṉ, Mūṉṟī-
chchuramuḍaiyanayiṉār, Emmaṇḍalaṅkoṇḍa Pāṇḍīchchuramuḍaiyanāyiṉār
and Chēravaṉ-uganda-Pānḍīchchuramuḍaiya-nāyinār at Ambalattāḍi-nallūr
alias Maṇappaḍaivīḍu in the eastern quarter of Śīvalavaṉmaṅgalam by the chief
of Jeyatuṅga-nāḍu, who was called Veṉṟum maṇkoṇḍa Śrī-bhūtala ••
vīravaṉmar, who had the title of Śaṅkaranārāyana-Mūttavēḷaikkāṟaṉ
Je[ya]tuṅga-nāḍu.

l.ta:
mūttaveḷaikkāṟaṉ veṉṟum maṇkoṇṭa śrī pūtala •• viravaṉmar oṉṟu pe-
ruṭaiya ceytuṅkanāṭṭu muttavarāy nām

civalavaṉmaṅkalam kiḻpiṭākai ampalattāṭinallūrāṉa maṇappa[ṭ*]aivīṭṭil yīc-
curaṉ pāṭakaṉukku nayiṉār kāṇaviniya-

mātam oṉṟukku koṭṭai mukkā-

yilukku mātam oṉṟukku koṭṭai mukkā-

kku koṭṭai muṉṟumāmukkāṇiyum kiḻaippāṭṭām nayiṉār ceravaṉ ukanta pāṇ-
ṭīccuramuṭaiya nayiṉār koyilu-
kku mātam oṉṟukku koṭṭai araikkālum āka

inscription.head:
No. 468.
(A.R. No. 445 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

This damaged inscription contains the praśasti of the king commencing
with Pugaḻmādu viḷaṅga, etc., and mentions the queen Avanimuḻudum Uḍaiyār.

It records a sale of land to the dēvakaṉmis of the temple of the god Mahā-
dēvar Mūṉrukaiyīśvaramuḍaiyanāyaṉār of Ambalattāḍinallūr in the eastern
quarter of Uttamaśōḻach-chaturvvēdimaṅgalam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam in Rājarāja-
pāṇḍi-nāḍu, as tirumaḍaippaḷḷippuṟam by the mahāsabhai of the village. It
is stated that the land in question was lying fallow and the dēvakaṉmis were
required to reclaim it and use its products for the above-mentioned service
in the temple. It is stated that the land lying amidst the sold land, and belong-
ing to the god Tirunelvēliuḍaiyār are excluded. Amongst the boundaries of the
land in question, the river Muḍikoṇḍaśōḻappērāṟu, [••••] maṇimaṅgalam, and
Uttamaśōḻa-vāykkāl figure.

The signatories include Mahādēvaṉ, Vāsudēvaṉ, Śeṇpagā ••• ṉ of Kōmaḷam
Śrīkṛishṇaṉ, Gōvindaṉ, Śaṅkaranārāyaṇaṉ and Śrīmāhēśvaraṉ.

ciṟa[n*]ta maṇimuṭi cūṭṭi

inscription.head:
No. 469.
(A.R. No. 446 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of the same maṇḍapa.

p.b:
Tribhº Sundarapāṇḍyadēva II: Year 10, Viṛiśchika
(Kārttigai) śu. 8, Rēvatī = 1248, A.D., November 25, The nakshatra was
Pūraṭṭādi.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 150 paṇam by a certain Tirunāvuḍaiyār
of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇappaiḍaivīḍu in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam to provide for
food and other offerings to the images of the god, goddess, and Iḷaiya-nāyaṉār
(i.e., Subrahmaṇya ?) set up by him in the name of Sundarapāṇḍya Kaṇḍiya-
dēvar in the temple of the god Mūṉṟukaiyīśvaramuḍaiyanāyaṉār of the place.
It is stated that the money was received by the Śivabrāhmaṇas of the 30
circles attached to the temple, who had agreed to carry out the object of the
endowment.

l.ta:
vatināḷ kiḻakaḷakkūṟṟattu ampalat-
tāṭinallūrāṉa maṇappaṭaiviṭṭu uṭaiyār muṉṟakai

ṇam paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu iṉṉāyiṉār
koyilukku ivar cuntarapāṇṭiyak kaṇṭiyatevar perāl eḻuntaruḷap paṇṇiṉa
nāyakarum nācciyārum iḷaiyanāyaṉāṟkum amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi uḷaviṭṭa nitta
nimantattukku niccayitta amutupaṭi aruci aṟunāḻiyum kaṟiyamutu veñ-
ceṉattukkum ma-

ip-
paṇam nūṟṟu aimpatukkum iṉṉāyiṉāṟkku ippaṭi uḷḷa amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi
uḷḷa nittanimantam muṭṭāmal cantirātittavaṟc cella kallilum cempilum
veṭṭikkoḷvatāka ippaṭi cammatittu

inscription.head:
No. 470.
(A.R. No. 447 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall as above.

p.b: Kollam 617, Āḍi 7, ba. 12, Thursday, Mṛigasīrsha = 1442 A.D., July 5.

inscription.p:
This inscription records a gift of 100 paṇam in aṉṟāḍu vaḻaṅgum Neṉmēni
Kaliyugarāmaṉ for certain services and offerings in the temple of the god
Mūṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyanāyiṉār of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇappaḍaivīḍu in
the north-eastern quarter of Śrīvallavaṉ-maṅgalam, a Brahmadēyam in Kīḻkaḻak-
kūṟṟam, by Māttāṇḍaṉ perumāḷ alias Villavarayar, the temple trustee (dharma-
kaṟttar). It is stated that the money was received by the Tiruvottamaśāmam
Aḻagiyāṉ Śēndīśuvaramuḍaiyāṉ Mūṉṟīśuvara-baṭṭaṉ and Kariyakuḻalaḻagiyāṉ,
two of the dēvakaṉmis of the 30 circles of the temple, who had agreed to offer
worship and offerings out of the interest from the endowment amount, on every
two pradōsha days, on every Aviṭṭam day and on every Svātī day, which was
the natal star of the donor, every month, besides using some amount for incenses
also.

Besides the two dēvakaṉmis mentioned above, Vaḷattu Vāḻvittār, a tapasvi
of the temple and Nāṇambi Ayyaṉ Villavarāya Āśāri, a siṟpi (sculptor) also
figure as signatories.

l.ta:
yam śrīvallavaṉ-
maṅkalattu vaṭakiḻ piṭākai ampalattāṭinallūrāṉa

maṇappaṭai viṭṭil uṭaiyār muṉṟīcuvaramuṭaiyanāyiṉār koyilil muppatuvaṭṭattil
amarnta tevakaṟmikaḷil tiruvottacāmam aḻakiyāṉ centicuvaramuṭaiyāṉ
muṉṟicuvarapaṭṭaṉum meṟpaṭi kariyakuḻalaḻakiyāṉum om

iṉṉāyiṉār koyilil taṉmakaṟtā māttāṇṭaṉ perumāḷāṉa villavarāyaṟku
piṭipāṭu paṇṇik kuṭutta paricāvatu iṟṟaināḷ ivar pakkal vāṅkiṉa aṉṟāṭu
vaḻaṅkum neṉmeṉi kaliyukarāmaṉ

ippaṇam nūṟṟukkum uḷḷa palicai

oṉṟukku āṭi aruḷum neykāppu irunāḻiyum ivar piṟanta nāḷ cotikku
āṭi aruḷum neykāppu irunāḻiyum

mātam toṟum nāṅkaḷ naṭatta kaṟpitta tūpattiraviyamum āka ivvakaippaṭi
mātam oṉṟukku

vatam nālukku āṭi aruḷum neykāppu aṟunāḻiyum tūpattira-
viyamum cantirātittavaṟ cellac cantati

kāṇippaṭi naṭattip potumpaṭi kaṟpittu piṭipāṭu paṇṇik kalluveṭṭik kuṭuttom
māttāṇṭaṉ perumāḷāṉa villavarāyaṟkku ippaṭikku ivai tiruvottacāmam
aḻakiyāṉ centi

ivvūril ciṟpikaḷil nāṇampi ayyaṉ villavarāya ācāri eḻuttu §

inscription.head:
No. 471.
(A.R. No. 448 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the same maṇḍapa.

p.b: Tribhº Vīrapāṇḍya: Year 2+21, Āḍi 27 = C. 1448 A.D.

inscription.p: This inscription is damaged in some places and is incomplete at the end.

It records a grant of 25 mā of land as dēvadāna for worship, offerings and
procession to the god Mūṉṟīśvaramuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of Ambalattāḍinallūr alias
Maṇaṟpaḍaivīḍu, in the north-eastern quarter of Śrīvallavaṉmaṅgalam,

a Brahmadēśam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, and 5 mā of land as maḍappuṟam to [Āga-
maśīladēvar] alias [Umaiyōrupāga] Mudaliyār of the lineage of the Bhikshā-
maḍa. Owing to the incomplete nature of the record, other details like the
donor, etc., are not available.

kaḷakkūṟṟattu

kāl nāyiṉār polintuniṉṟapirāṉ tiruviṭaiyāṭṭattukku kiḻakku taṭi o-

nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ mākāṇiyum muṉṟāṅ kaṇṇāṟu kiḻakku

niṉṟum taṭi oṉṟu nilam mukkāṇiyum meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyiṉ
mākāṇiyum meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ araikkā-

lum kālukku meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ mūṉṟu mā
mukkāṇiyum meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikai nāyiṉā-

r polintuniṉṟapirāṉ tiruvuṭaiāṭṭattukkum vāykkālukkum meṟku taṭi
iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ mukkālum meṟku taṭi oṉṟu nilam muk-

kāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ kālum meṟku taṭi oṉṟu nilam araimāviṉ mukkālum
nālāṅkaṇṇāṟu meṟku niṉṟum taṭi iraṇṭu nilam araimā araikkāṇi

muntirikaiyiṉ eḻumāvaraiyum kalu • kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyiṉ
araiye mākāṇi nāyiṉār poli ••• niṉṟa •• ṉa tiruvuṭaivaṭṭattu-

kkum kiḻakku taṭi oṉṟu nilam araimāvaraikkāṇi muntirikaiyiṉ eḻumāvarai
nāyiṉār cuvāmi tevatāṉa iṟai ilikkum vaṭameṟkku oṉṟu nilamu

araikkāṇi muntirikaiyiṉ eṭṭumā mukkāṇiyum uṭciṟuvāykkālukku • itaṉ
kiḻakku taṭi oṉṟu nilam mukkāṇi arai-

kkāṇi muntirikaiyiṉ arai nāyiṉār cuvāmi tevar iṟai ilikku kiḻakku taṭi
iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇiyiṉ ka •

lum kālukku kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi muntirikaiyiṉ araiyum kiḻakku
taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyiṉ araiyum kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam muk-
kāṇiyiṉ eḻumā

[mu]ntirikaiyiṉ araiyum añcāṅ kaṇṇāṟu kiḻakku niṉṟum taṭi oṉṟu nilam
mukkāṇiyiṉ eṭṭu mā mukkāṇiyum meṟku taṭi oṉṟu nilam kāṇi a-

tāṉa iṟai ilikkum kālukkum vaṭameṟku taṭi oṉṟu nilam kāṇi ā-

inscription.head:
No. 472.
(A.R. No. 449 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records a grant of 20 mā of land in Viḷankaḷappaṟṟu in
Śrīvallavaṉ-maṅgalam and 1/2 mā of land for flower-garden and grove, one oil-
mill, twenty looms and puñjai lands, flora and fauna, all these probably in
Ambalattāḍinallūr alias Maṇappaḍaivīḍu in the northern part of Śrīvallavaṉ
maṅgalam, a Brahmadēśam in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam, to the temple of the god Mūṉṟīś-
varamuḍaiya-nāyaṉār of the place. It is stated that the gift was intended to
provide for food-offerings, flowers, clothes, and nandā lamp which formed part
of daily routine services to the god, and for worship on such special occasions
as vishu. ayana and eclipses, on days when the star Bharaṇi fell as well as for
worship with doubled provisions on days when the natal star of the king fell.

A certain Māttāṇḍāṉ Śīvindiramuḍaiyāṉ and a sculptor (name lost)
figure as signatories.

l.ta:
ramuṭaiya nāyaṉāṟ koyilil teva-
kaṉmikaḷukku iṉ-

ṉāyiṉāṟku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi tirupparivaṭṭam tirumāvali tirunantāviḷakkuṭpaṭṭa
nittiya nimantaṅkaḷu-

kku tevatāṉamāka viṭṭa nilam āvatu śrīvillavaṉmaṅkalattu viḷaṅkaḷappaṟṟil
citevi vāykkālukku

vaṭakkum muṉṟāṅkaṇṇāṟṟu kiḻakku niṉṟum taṭi oṉṟu nilam oru mā araik-
kāṇikkum kālukkum meṟku taṭi

iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaiyum itaṉ meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu
nilam kāṇikkiḻaraiyum itaṉ i-

ṭai taṭi palanilam muṉṟu māmukkāṇi uṭaiyār [parā]ntevaramuṭaiya nāyaṉār
tevatāṉattukku meṟku taṭi muṉ-

ṟu nilam kāṇi muntirikaiyum nālāṅ kaṇṇāṟu meṟku niṉṟum taṭi iraṇṭu
nilam mukkāṇikku kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu ni-

lam mukkāṇiyum itaṉ kiḻakku taṭi muṉṟu nilam oru mā araikkāṇiyum
itaṉ iṭai taṭi palanilam muṉṟu [mā a]-

araikkāṇi muntirikaikku kiḻakku taṭi oṉṟu nilam araikkāṇiyum aiñcāṅ
kaṇṇāṟu kiḻakku niṉṟum taṭi pala nilam

oru māvarai muntirikaikkum kāṇikkum meṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyum
itaṉ meṟku taṭi iraṇ-

ṭu nilam kāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaiyum itaṉiṭai taṭi palanilam iraṇṭu
mākkāṇikku meṟku ta[ṭi]

iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyum āṟāṅkaṇṇāṟu meṟku niṉṟum taṭi nālu nilam
oru mā mukkāṇikku [kiḻa]-

kku taṭi nālu nilam oru mā muntirikaiyum itaṉ kiḻakku taṭi nālu nilam oru
mā araikkāṇi muntirikai-

yum itaṉiṭai taṭi pala nilam nālu māvaraiye araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻaraikku
kiḻakku taṭi oṉṟu

nilam kāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaiyum ēḻāṅkaṇṇāṟu kiḻakku niṉṟum taṭi pala
nilam muṉṟu māvaraiye

araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻaraikku meṟkutaṭi iraṇṭu nilam oru māmuntirikaiyum
itaṉ meṟku taṭi i[ra]-

ṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyum eṭṭāṅ kaṇṇāṟu meṟku niṉṟum taṭi oṉṟu nilam
arai māvaraikkāṇikku

kkiḻakkut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi muntirikaiyum itaṉ kiḻakkut taṭi
iraṇṭu nilam oru māvum itaṉ kiḻakku-

t taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi muntirikaiyum itaṉ kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam
mukkāṇi araikkāṇi munti-

rikaikkum kālukkum kiḻakkut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyum kiḻakkuttaṭi muṉṟu
nilam araimāvaraikkāṇi munti-

rikaiyum itaṉ kiḻakku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇiyum oṉpatāṅ kaṇṇāṟu
kiḻakku niṉṟum taṭi mūṉṟu nilam oru mā-

varaikkāṇi muntirikaikku vaṭamēṟkut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇi
kiḻaraiyum itaṉ vaṭak-

kut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi araikkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻaraiyum itaṉ

oru mā muntirikaik kiḻaraiyum itaṉ meṟkut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi
muntirikaiyum itaṉ meṟku

ttaṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi muntirikaiyum pattāṅ kaṇṇāṟu kiḻakku niṉṟum
taṭi muṉṟu nilam kāṇi mun-

tirikaiyum itaṉ meṟkut taṭi iraṇṭu nilam mukkāṇi muntirikaik kiḻaraiyil
cāttaṉ viḷaṅkuḷamuṭaiyār aiya-

pātti nikki nilam muntirikaik kiḻaraiyum itaṉ vaṭameṟku taṭi iraṇṭu nilam
araimā muntirikai kiḻa-

ramuṭaiya nāyiṉār tevatāṉam nikki nilam araik
kāṇi muntirikai

kiḻ araiyum ākattaṭi aṉpattu añciṉāl nilam irupatumā iṉṉilam irupatu
māvum iṉṉāyiṉāṟkut ti-

runaṉtāvaṉamum toppum uṭpaṭṭavakaikku ellai āvatu kiḻ ellai kaṇṭaṉ
kāl piṟkaraiyil kuruttukku

meṟkut teṉ ellai kaṇṭaṉ kālukku vaṭakku mel ellai nāyiṉār kāṇa-
iṉiyaperumāḷ koyilukkuk ki-

ḻakkil teṉmaṭalāṉa nārācattukku kiḻakku vaṭavellai taṉporuntap perāṟṟuk-
kut teṟku āka iṉṉāṉ-

ku ellai uṭpaṭṭa talattil tirumaṭaviḷākam niṅkalāka uḷḷa nilattil koṭi-
iṭṭukkoḷḷum nilam arai-

māvum cekku oṉṟum taṟi irupatum puṉceyum māvaṭai māvaṭaiyumkūṭa aṟunūṟṟu
oru pattu ayyāmāṇṭu pacā-

ṉa mutalukku orupattu āṟām āṇṭu taimātam patinālāntiyati tanta aḷa-
vukku tāṅkaḷum ittaravu piṭipāṭākak-

kaiyyāṇṭu koṇṭu iṉṉilam irupatumāvil kaṭamaiyum ūrmāvicainta cilavarip
peruvariyum mā-

vaṭai maravaṭaiyilum cekkiṟai taṟikkaṭamai koṭittoṭṭam puṉcai uṭpaṭṭa
vakaiyilum mutalām mutal

koṇṭu iṉṉāyiṉāṟku amutupaṭi cāttuppaṭi tirupparivaṭṭam tirumālai tirunaṉtā-

kkum uṭpaṭa iṭṭap paṭittarattiṉ paṭiye cantirā

yum tāmu naṭattikkoṇṭu potavum pāṟkka ivai

inscription.head:
No. 473.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1909.)
MAṆAPPAḌAIVĪḌU, TIRUNELVELI TALUK, TIRUNELVELI DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Same wall.

This inscription records an order of the king (name not mentioned)
addressed to a certain Periya Tiruvaḍi Nayiṉār of Māṟaṉmaṅgalam and the
dēvakaṉmis and the tāṉattāṟ of the temple of the god Mūṉṟīśvaramuḍaiyanā-
yiṉār of Ambalattāḍinallūr, alias Maṇappaḍaivīḍu in the north-eastern quarter
of Śrīvallavaṉ-maṅgalam, a Brahmadēśam, in Kīḻkaḷak-kūṟṟam. The order
related to the grant of land in Maṇakkuḍi, Marudānambikuṟuchchi and
Maṇappaḍaivīḍu to the temple of the above-mentioned god, by the king to
provide for the procession, both in the morning and in the evening, every-
day of śrīibali, for the bathing ceremony of the image of the god Maṇavaikku-
vāytta Perumāḷ on every Chittirai star day (in a month) for the deitys festi-
vals, and for worship and offerings on Puṇaragam (?) (Punarpūsam ?) days
in the month of Māśi every year, the birth-day of the king. As ordered, the
above-mentioned parties had agreed to conduct the worship, services and festi-
vals in the temple heriditarily.
The signatory was a certain Peṟṟa Perumāḷ, the village accountant.

l.ta: rkku iraṇṭu canti civali eḻuntaruḷumpaṭiyum cittirai cittiraikku [ma]
lāppuṟam eḻuntaruḷumpaṭiyum mācimātattil puṇarakam āṭṭai piṟa-

m puḷḷiyum iṭṭu inta puḷḷiyil melvārattukku atikāriyai-
yk koṇṭu kuṟiyum vāṅkik koṇṭup puḷḷiyilu uḷḷa ne-
llukkum tevatāṉa mutalukkum maṟṟum nelvakai paṇa-
